chapters.title: Chapter 2197 Location Of The Spirit Tree
chapters.body:
Chapter 2197 Location Of The Spirit Tree

Hearing the man’s offer, Yuan couldn’t help but smile.

“I see. So you’re going to attack the Asura Clan. How many people do you already have with you?” he asked.

The man then said, “We have the majority of the top factions on our side. The Asura Clan might be powerful—undoubtedly the most powerful in the Primordial Realm—but if we all work together, not even they will be able to stop us.”

“You sound very confident. Have you fought the Asura Clan before?”

“Nobody has seriously fought the Asura Clan in ages. However, they are not omnipotent. Moreover, they have fewer than ten people in their faction, while we have over ten thousand experts. There’s no way we will lose to them.”

“…”

After a moment of silence, Yuan asked, “When do you plan to attack the Asura Clan?”

“We’re already fully prepared, so we can attack whenever. Right now, we’re just waiting for an opportunity to launch our attack.”

“In other words, it might happen a week or a hundred years from now,” Yuan shook his head. “Unfortunately, I am short on time.”

“Why are you in such a hurry?”

Yuan pondered for a brief moment before responding, “Because I am hunted by the Chaotic Sect, and the longer I am away from my family, the more dangerous things get for me.”

“What? Why is the Chaotic Sect hunting you?” The man frowned at this news.

Yuan gave a brief explanation of his situation with the Chaotic Sect.

“Those arrogant bastards are always trying to take advantage of others! It’s because of this behavior that we decided not to include them in our plan, as people like them are likely to backstab us.”

“But you don’t need to worry. As long as you join us, not even the Chaotic Sect will be able to touch you.”

“If you say that, it’ll be hard for me to refuse,” Yuan said with a smile.

After a moment of silence, he continued, “Alright, I’ll join your cause. However, can you still tell me the location of the Spirit Tree? I won’t approach the Asura Clan alone. I just want to have peace of mind knowing where I can find them.”

“Even if you know the location, you won’t be able to enter it without the Asura Clan or the Inferno Alchemist’s assistance.”

“What’s the Inferno Alchemist have to do with this?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“Well, the Spirit Tree exists in its own dimension, and it can only be accessed by the Asura Clan or the head of the Inferno Alchemists.”

“What’s their relationship with the Asura Clan?”

The man stared at Yuan with a baffled expression. “You… you truly know nothing of this world? Just how isolated has your life been until now?”

Yuan silently shrugged.

“Anyway, the Inferno Alchemists are basically the only alchemists in the Primordial Realm. But without spiritual energy, they can hardly practice their craft. That’s why they struck a deal with the Asura Clan—unlimited pills for them in return for access to the Spirit Tree.”

“Our lack of access to the Spirit Tree is also another reason why we cannot attack them whenever we want.”

“Are the Inferno Alchemists not on our side?” Yuan questioned.

The man shook his head and said, “No, those guys only care about alchemy and money, and since they already have access, they wouldn’t want to jeopardize it.”

“Then how do you plan on reaching the Spirit Tree, much less attacking the Asura Clan? Do you intend on forcing the alchemists to lead you to the Spirit Tree or something?”

“You’re quite insightful. That’s right. We are going to force them to take us to the Spirit Tree. The Inferno Alchemists barely have a hundred members in their faction, so it won’t be difficult to subdue them.” Yuan found it amusing how this man was so quick to reveal their schemes to someone who was no more than a stranger.

‘Does he have ulterior motives, or is he really just this… loose-lipped?’ he wondered inwardly.

“So, where’s the Spirit Tree located?” Yuan asked as he retrieved two spirit stones and showed them to the man.

“The entrance to the Spirit Tree is located at the Burning Tree in the Land of Inferno, but like I said, you won’t be able to enter it without the Asura Clan or the Inferno Alchemists.” “I know. Here are your spirit stones.” Yuan casually handed him the spirit stones.

“Take this.”

In exchange, the man handed him a token and continued, “This will not only serve as an identification but also a communication device. If it activates, you must immediately head to the Spirit Tree, as that is when we will launch our attack.”

“Alright.” Yuan accepted the token.

The man left shortly after, while Yuan returned to his room to wait for the auction payout.

A week passed in the blink of an eye, and during this time, several more individuals approached Yuan for his offer.

In case the man lied about the location of the Spirit Tree, Yuan acted like the offer was still available to confirm the location.

And out of the eight people that approached him, three of them mentioned the Burning Tree, while the other five all gave him different locations.

“The Burning Tree, huh? Just wait a little longer, Xiao Hua. You’re almost home.”

After a whole week had passed, Yuan returned to the Chaos Auction House for the payout.

“So, do you have my money, or do I have to make a trip to the Chaotic Sect?”

Chen Cheng quickly responded, “Rest assured, esteemed guest, you will receive your payment. Regardless of your situation with the Chaotic Sect, we cannot steal your money or treasures at the auction house, or nobody would dare to trust us again.”

“Please, follow me. Your money is stored in another room.”

Yuan nodded and followed Chen Cheng to a different room, where over a dozen large chests were gathered.

“The money is all here. You can count them if you’d like.”

With a sweep of his divine sense, Yuan verified that the chests held all the money. But amidst the coins, something unusual stood out.

A few of them had a subtle and unique aura hidden within them. This update ıs available on NoveI★Fire.net

“Thanks.” Yuan pretended not to notice it and tossed all of the money into his spatial ring.

chapters.title: Chapter 2198 Second Chaotic Heart
chapters.body:
Chapter 2198 Second Chaotic Heart

After storing the money, Yuan turned around and began walking toward the exit.

“Esteemed guest… It’s not too late…” Chen Cheng suddenly spoke, halting his movements.

Yuan turned to face him and asked, “What are you talking about?”

Chen Cheng sighed before elaborating, “It’s not too late to apologize to the Chaotic Sect. Just like the gold coins, you should have more spirit stones in your possession, right? You can show your sincerity by offering some of them to the Chaotic Sect, and there’s a good chance they’ll forgive you.”

Yuan smiled and responded, “You should really work at a different place. Someone like you does not belong in a place like the Chaotic Sect.”

Chen Cheng shook his head and said, “I grew up in the Chaotic Sect. It’s not a place I can leave whenever I feel like.”

“Is that so? What a pity. As for apologizing to the Chaotic Sect… I’d rather they hunt me,” said Yuan before walking out of the room.

Shortly after Yuan left, Chen Cheng went to another room, where the Chaos Elders were waiting.

“It’s been done. He accepted all of the money without any issue,” Chen Cheng informed them.

“Great job. Once we take care of him, I’ll consider giving you a promotion,” said the man.

Chen Cheng didn’t respond to that statement and silently bowed.

“Is there anything else I can do for you?”

“No, we’ll handle the rest,” said the woman as she stood up with an excited smile on her face.

“It’s time to hunt.”

Meanwhile, Yuan returned to the hotel and retrieved the coins that had a subtle aura hidden within them.

“Looks like a tracker. They’ll probably attack once we leave this city,” Yuan calmly remarked.

“So, what are we going to do?” Mu Xuelian asked.

“This doesn’t change anything. We’ll still head to the Spirit Tree. If they attack us, we’ll just take care of them. If you’d rather not get involved, you can wait here.”

“No thanks. If I stay behind, I might as well have never entered this place with you,” she refused without hesitation.

“In that case, do whatever you want. It’s not like I was going to ask you to help me, anyway. I can deal with them myself.”

“Then when are we going to leave?” Mu Xuelian asked.

“In a few days, after I absorb all of the Chaotic Essence from the Chaos Coins,” he calmly replied.

“Okay.”

Yuan stowed away the Chaos Coins with the aura and took out the untampered Chaos Coins from his spatial ring, piling them into a little mountain before him in the middle of the room.

‘With this much Chaotic Essence, I can easily create another Chaotic Heart.’ Yuan thought to himself as he sat before the mountain of coins.

Without hesitation, he began his cultivation.

Over the next several days, Yuan’s second cultivation, which had been stagnant at Chaotic Master since he left the Primal Expanse, soared towards the heavens.

<You have absorbed Chaotic Essence>

<You have absorbed enough Chaotic Essence for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached Early Chaotic Commander>

<You have absorbed enough Chaotic Essence for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached Intermediate Chaotic King>

<You have absorbed enough Chaotic Essence to start forming your second Chaotic Heart!>

<You have begun forming your second Chaotic Heart!>

<You have absorbed enough Chaotic Essence for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached Perfect Chaotic Emperor>

<You have successfully formed your second Chaotic Heart!>

<Your second Chaotic Heart has merged with your Chaotic Dragon Core, drastically strengthening it!>

<You have reached Early Chaotic Overlord!>

By the time Yuan finished absorbing all of the Chaotic Essence, his cultivation reached Early Chaotic Overlord, which was akin to a cultivator who had just entered Bronze Immortal. However, his prowess was obviously incomparably more powerful than that of a Bronze Immortal.

“If I had known how much these coins would benefit my cultivation, I would’ve sold a few more spirit stones,” he sighed, seriously considering selling more spirit stones later. “…”

Mu Xuelian, who had witnessed everything, was left speechless. As an ordinary cultivator, she couldn’t perceive Yuan’s second cultivation, so to her eyes, nothing had changed. Yet she could clearly feel that something about him was profoundly different.

Shiva, who watched through Mu Xuelian, was also quite stunned to witness a human cultivating Chaotic Essence.

‘A human possessing Chaotic Hearts, huh? That shouldn’t be possible without a completed Eternal Constitution… How many secrets are contained within that body?’ Shiva thought to herself.

As for the Chaos Coins, once Yuan absorbed all of their Chaotic Essence, they crumbled into nothingness, leaving behind only a mound of gray dust that resembled ashes.

This also meant Yuan had obliterated billions worth of the Chaotic Sect’s currency. If word of it ever spread, it would spark chaos across the region and send shockwaves through the world’s financial balance.

“Alright, I’m ready to leave the city,” Yuan said.

Mu Xuelian nodded, and together they departed not long after.

Meanwhile, somewhere in the city, the Chaos Elders had been waiting for Yuan to finally leave the city.

“Get ready! He’s finally on the move!” The woman said to the man after noticing Yuan had left the city through the tracker.

The man nodded before turning to face the others in the room.

“You guys ready?” “I was born ready.”

“Finally, we can stretch our arms. I was beginning to die of boredom.”

Thus, the Chaos Elders, along with several members of the Chaotic Sect, began trailing Yuan through the tracker. Yet, they held back their assault, choosing instead to wait until he had ventured far enough from the city.

After days of aimless wandering, Yuan and Mu Xuelian finally stopped moving when several presences materialized out of thin air, closing in from every direction.

Within moments, five figures surrounded them, each one emanating the oppressive aura of a God Ascension expert.

“About time you showed up. What took you so long?” Yuan spoke out loud as he faced the Chaos Elders.

“So you knew,” muttered the male Chaos Elder.

Yuan retrieved one of the coins they used to track him and flicked it at him. Get full chapters from novel✶fire.net

“How could I not when you made it so obvious?”

chapters.title: Chapter 2199 Confronting The Chaos Elders
chapters.body:
Chapter 2199 Confronting The Chaos Elders

“So you knew we were tracking you, yet you still decided to leave the city alone? I can’t even imagine what’s going through that mind of yours,” remarked the female Chaos Elder, her tone full of mockery.

“It’s nothing complicated. There’s just no reason for me to worry. After all, you’re not a threat to me,” Yuan calmly responded.

“Still trying to act tough? Not only are you just an Immortal, but we outnumber you.”

Yuan chuckled. “So what? You may be God Ascension cultivators, but you only have slightly more spiritual energy than I do. Right now, you’re no different from mere Immortals.”

“This little bastard is very talkative…” mumbled one of the other cultivators.

“Elder Pan, can we kill them already?” said another.

The male Chaos Elder, surnamed Pan, glared coldly at Yuan as he spoke. “I don’t know what hole a bastard like you crawled out from, nor what background you have—but no one who insults the Chaotic Sect walks away unscathed. Still, being the generous man that I am, I’ll grant you one final chance.”

“Be a good boy and hand over everything in your possession, and I will grant you a swift and painless death.”

Yuan smiled and replied, “If you want my stuff, you’ll have to work for it. However, the results might not be what you’re expecting.”

“What a pointless struggle.”

“If you want to die a horrible death, allow me to help you!”

One of the experts suddenly lunged at Yuan with a Divine-grade spear in his grasp.

Yuan didn’t even bother dodging the attack and stood still as the spear struck his chest.

“What?!”

To the wielder’s shock, the spear slammed into Yuan and stopped dead, unable to push forward. In the midst of the wielder’s shock, Yuan grabbed the spear and casually yanked it from his grasp.

“This is how you use a spear.”

Yuan surged with Chaotic Essence as he lunged the spear, the thrust detonating with cataclysmic force—splitting the air in a deafening boom and rending open the very fabric of space as it hurled at the shocked expert.

“What are you doing?! Dodge it!”

The spear expert snapped out of his daze at the sound of Elder Pan’s voice, and he hastily summoned his spiritual energy in an attempt to block the incoming strike.

However, due to their environment that forced everyone to be mindful of their spiritual energy usage, the expert subconsciously held back the amount of spiritual energy he used.

Due to this mistake, the spear ripped through his spiritual energy like paper before carving a gaping hole through his body. That being said, even if the expert had used all of his spiritual energy to defend, it wouldn’t have been enough to block the strike.

Before the spear expert could recover, Yuan closed the gap and flicked his arm that was surging with Supreme Sword Aura, striking the man’s head and sending it spinning skyward. ᴛʜɪs ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀ ɪs ᴜᴘᴅᴀᴛᴇ ʙʏ N()velFire.net

With his physical body destroyed, the man immediately tried to flee with his soul. However, Yuan extended his arm in the direction the man was fleeing and mumbled, “Shiva’s Eternal Frost.”

In the next instant, the temperature plummeted as if they’d been thrust into the ninth region of White Hell, and everything within ten thousand miles was sealed in ice.

The other four cultivators did not freeze outright, yet they felt their blood beginning to solidify. The fleeing soul, however, was sealed entirely, entombed in eternal frost.

‘That bastard’s using my powers!’ Shiva exclaimed inwardly after witnessing the scene.

Though the power itself was pitifully weak, it was not something mortals could wield, making Yuan’s ability to unleash it a feat in its own right.

Meanwhile, Yuan was surprised by how demanding the technique was. A single strike had emptied his reserves in an instant, and that was only enough to allow him to barely activate it. Fortunately, his energy recovered just as swiftly.

‘As expected, a technique that surpasses human limits is quite demanding…’ Yuan smiled inwardly.

Instead of disappointment, excitement stirred within him at the thought of the day he could wield Shiva’s power to its fullest potential.

Yuan’s gaze swept over the remaining four, his eyes like ice as he murmured, “One down… four to go.”

Elder Pan and the others trembled—not from Yuan’s overwhelming might, but from the unfathomable aura that still lingered from Shiva’s Eternal Frost.

“What’s wrong? Don’t tell me you’ve lost all will to fight this quickly?”

Yuan’s words jolted them from their daze. Elder Pan barked, “Fool! You may have slain one of us, but that technique must have drained nearly all your energy!”

Yuan only smiled. “You’re not wrong. But unlike you, I can simply replenish it whenever I wish.”

“You’re really stupid if you think that bluff would!”

Suddenly, one of the cultivators vanished from sight and reappeared behind Yuan, his saber already cleaving toward Yuan’s neck.

This time, Yuan did not stand idle. He lifted his arm and caught the incoming saber between two fingers, stopping it cold in an instant.

“You feeling cold? Let me warm you up a bit,” Yuan murmured, unleashing the Primordial Fire—flames so searing they twisted the very air as they consumed the man.

“Ahhhh!”

The man’s screams tore through the sky as he plummeted to the ground, thrashing desperately in a futile attempt to smother the flames. But the Primordial Fire would not relent, and it devoured him piece by piece until only his soul remained.

Once again, the cultivator attempted to flee with his soul.

“You think you can strike at me and leave as you please?” Yuan sneered, summoning the Primordial Fire once more.

Flames engulfed the man’s soul, and since it was far more durable than flesh, the fire gnawed at it slowly, wringing screams of unending agony.

Yuan didn’t bother waiting for the Primordial Fire to finish devouring the soul. Instead, he turned his gaze upon the remaining three, their faces ashen, as though they had just seen a ghost.

“Not looking so confident anymore, huh?” Yuan remarked with a cold smile.

chapters.title: Chapter 2200 Confronting The Chaos Elders(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2200 Confronting The Chaos Elders(2)

“Damn it! What kind of mess did you drag me into, Elder Pan?! Elder Zi! You offended a monster!” The third cultivator shouted at the Chaos Elders. “It… it wasn’t supposed to be like this…” Elder Zi murmured, her dazed eyes clouded with disbelief, struggling to accept the reality before her.

“L-Let’s talk about this!” Elder Pan suddenly said. “If you agree to stop here, we’ll overlook the fact that you killed two of our members and act like we don’t even know each other!”

“And if I refuse?” Yuan asked just for the entertainment.

“Then you’ll be hunted by the entire Chaotic Sect!”

“If I kill all of you right here, would the Chaotic Sect even know who I am?” “They will! After all, we wouldn’t be here if we didn’t receive the Chaotic Sect’s approval! In other words, they know who you are and that if we don’t return, you’ll be the first one they suspect!” Elder Pan said.

“And what assurance do I have,” Yuan asked, “that if I spare you now, you won’t return with more to hunt me later? You can’t blame me for not trusting you, either.”

“That’s…” Elder Pan didn’t know how to respond.

No, it was more like he couldn’t, as there was simply no way he would let Yuan go after this humiliation.

Of course, Yuan already knew this, and he calmly declared, “I refuse your proposal. Now, die for me!”

Yuan activated True Dragon Awakening, causing his aura to skyrocket. However, he didn’t stop there and further strengthened himself with his second cultivation.

“Screw this—I’m out of here!” the third cultivator exclaimed, turning to flee without hesitation, abandoning the Chaos Elders. But before he could escape, Yuan summoned a wall of Primordial Fire, encasing them in a blazing sphere that sealed every path.

“W-Why?! We have no enmity, and I never even raised a hand against you!” the cultivator cried, his voice breaking as Yuan barred his path.

“You’re right, which is precisely why you’re still alive. Stay there and don’t lift a single finger until I tell you to do so if you want to live. I’ll be with you shortly after I deal with these two,” said Yuan before turning to look at the Chaos Elders, who were clearly panicking and trembling in fear.

“Wait… please… calm down! We were wrong, okay? Have mercy!” Elder Zi, the female Chaos Elder, pleaded with teary eyes.

Yuan coldly sneered, untouched by her tears, “Would you have spared me if you were in my shoes? Probably not.”

“But—”

As Elder Zi opened her mouth, Yuan drew Number One Under Heaven and unleashed a swift, devastating strike. A flash of golden sword light filled her eyes, and in the next heartbeat, his Supreme Sword Aura cleaved her body and soul clean in two.

Elder Pan stared at Elder Zi’s split corpse with a terrified expression. He couldn’t even begin to fathom how an Immortal could kill a God Ascension cultivator with such ease. Even if they weren’t as powerful as God Ascension cultivators in the Nine Heavens, they’re still beings that cannot be touched by mere Immortals.

“Was it worth it?” Yuan suddenly asked.

“What…?” Elder Pan muttered.

“If you think about it, things ended up like this because you couldn’t wait a few days. Try to be a little more patient in your next life, alright?”

Elder Pan trembled at Yuan’s words. Then, after a tense silence, his face flushed red as he roared, “If you think I’ll go down without a fight, you’re—”

However, Yuan didn’t even give him the chance to finish his sentence and swung his sword that was wreathed in a golden aura.

In an instant, Elder Pan’s entire being was erased from existence.

Yuan turned to look at the remaining cultivator afterward and spoke in a nonchalant voice, “Do you want to live?”

The cultivator, too terrified to speak, could only silently nod his head.

“Bring me to the Burning Tree within the Land of Inferno, and I’ll let you live.”

“The Burning Tree…? You might as well just kill me now. There’s no way we’ll get there alive. Even if we do, we won’t make it back,” he said.

“If you’re worried about the Asura Clan attacking us, rest assured—they won’t,” Yuan said calmly.

The cultivator frowned and asked, “Why are you so certain? Are you tied to the Asura Clan? No… if you were, you wouldn’t need me to lead you there…”

“Stop asking so many questions. Don’t forget your place—if you’re no use, say so, and I’ll cut you down immediately.” Yuan’s voice was flat and cold.

The cultivator gritted his teeth, fists white with tension.

After a moment of silence, he loosened his fists and murmured, “Fine. I’ll take you to the Burning Tree.”

“Good. Then let us be on our way,” Yuan said.

The cultivator sighed, as though surrendering to fate, and started flying south. “So, how are you related to the elders? And how did you end up in this situation?” Yuan questioned as they traveled. Thɪs chapter is updated by novᴇlfire.net

“There are twenty-four elders at the Chaotic Sect—well, twenty now since you just killed four of them. We were ordered by the Sect Leader to take care of the ‘insolent bastard’ who dared to insult and look down on our sect. In other words, I’m just following orders.”

“If that’s true, you’re truly unlucky.” Yuan shook his head, but he did not feel pity.

He continued, “Is the Chaotic Sect always like this?”

“Pretty much. We do not tolerate anyone who disrespects us.”

“I’m surprised you guys survived this long with that kind of mentality.” “It’s because we don’t let people look down on us that we’ve survived this long. As long as we don’t mess with the Asura Clan, we won’t go anywhere.”

“If you say so.”

A few days later—

“We’ve just entered the Inferno Alchemists’ territory,” said the cultivator.

“How long until we reach the Burning Tree?” Yuan asked.

“If nothing goes wrong, we should arrive in a week,” he said.

“Very well.” Yuan nodded.

chapters.title: Chapter 2201 Inferno Alchemists
chapters.body:
Chapter 2201 Inferno Alchemists

Three days had passed since they entered the Land of Inferno. Yuan suddenly noticed the cultivator’s pace quickening, as though impatience—or something else—was driving him forward.

“Is there something wrong?” Yuan asked him.

“W-What?” The cultivator muttered as he turned to face Yuan, looking startled by the question.

“You’re speeding up, almost like you cannot wait to arrive at our destination.”

“Isn’t that obvious? The faster we reach our destination, the faster I get to go home,” he said.

Yuan narrowed his eyes slightly and spoke, “What are you hiding? It’d be in your best interest to tell me the truth before you do something stupid.”

“I’m not hiding anything!” the cultivator snapped, his tone firm—but the flicker of nervousness in his eyes betrayed him.

Yuan fell silent for a moment before speaking again, “Let’s see… What are the chances that instead of taking me to the Burning Tree like I told you, you’re actually bringing me to another location?”

The cultivator’s body twitched at his words.

Seeing his reaction, Yuan continued with a cold smile, “What if you’re actually taking me to the Chaotic Sect instead?”

“That’s nonsense! You’re just making shit up!” The cultivator came to a halt and shouted with a flushed face. “If you want to kill me, just do it! There’s no need for you to make excuses!”

Yuan then said, “You know what? I don’t trust you, but I’ll still give you the benefit of the doubt. However, I must warn you. If you’re actually taking me to the Chaotic Sect, things won’t play out like you’re hoping, and you’ll be the reason for their destruction.”

“…”

The cultivator didn’t immediately respond, and after a moment of silence, he continued flying without a word.

Yuan also didn’t say anything and continued to follow him.

Several days later, Yuan remarked, “According to you, we should’ve arrived at the Burning Tree by now.”

“I… I accidentally took the wrong turn. We should arrive at our destination in five days. I apologize for the inconvenience,” he said.

“Is that so? Be more careful this time.” Yuan didn’t pursue the matter and patiently waited.

After another two days, they came to a halt when an individual wearing red and gold robes blocked their path.

“You’re not allowed to go any further unless you want to offend the Asura Clan. Turn around and leave,” said the man who was clearly from the Inferno Alchemists.

“Unfortunately, I cannot do that, as I have some business with the Asura Clan,” said Yuan.

The Alchemist immediately frowned at his words and replied, “The Asura Clan? Who are you? And what is your relationship with them?”

“We’re something like friends, I guess.” “…”

The Alchemist stared at Yuan with wide eyes filled with disbelief before he suddenly burst out laughing.

“Hahaha! Friends?! Surely, you must have a better lie than that! The Asura Clan has no friends!”

“Who are you to claim they have no friends? Are you that close with the Asura Clan?” Yuan questioned, which instantly halted the Alchemist’s laughter.

“If you claim to be his friend, what is your name? We’ll ask the Asura Clan about you, and if you turn out to be lying…”

“Tell them Tian Xian is here to see them. If I am lying, you can turn me into dog food or whatever you want,” Yuan responded with a confident smile.

“Don’t worry, I’ll do just that. Until we can confirm your identity, you will have to stay with us at the Inferno Alchemist’s headquarters. After all, we don’t want you to run away after making such a bold claim.”

“How long will this take?” Yuan asked.

“I don’t know since it’s not like we can contact the Asura Clan as we please.”

“…”

After a moment of silence, Yuan nodded, “Alright.”

Although he didn’t say it out loud, he decided to give them a month at most. If they couldn’t contact the Asura Clan by then, he would head to the Burning Tree by force.

“Umm… what about me? I have brought you to your destination, so…” The cultivator from the Chaotic Sect said.

Yuan silently glared at him for a moment before responding, “Yes, as promised, you’re free to go.”

“T-Thank you!”

Without hesitation, the cultivator immediately turned around and began fleeing like his life depended on it. The source of this content ɪs NoveI★Fire.net

Even as he put distance between them, Yuan’s voice trailed after him. “When you return, try to convince the Chaotic Sect not to seek revenge. You wouldn’t want to squander the second life I gave you, do you?”

“What was that about?” asked the Alchemist.

“Oh, don’t worry about him. He was just a guide.”

“…Whatever. Follow me.”

Yuan nodded, and he and Mu Xuelian followed the Alchemist back to the Inferno Alchemist’s headquarters.

A day later, they arrived at the Inferno Alchemist’s headquarters, a place built like a sect but on a much smaller scale.

When they arrived, the Alchemist brought Yuan to a guest house before leaving to report the situation to his superiors. Of course, he didn’t leave Yuan and Mu Xuelian unsupervised and had several people guard their place.

“What? A friend of the Asura Clan? What nonsense is that?”

The elders almost couldn’t believe their ears and were very dismissive about the situation.

“They’re definitely just some troublemaker trying to reach the Spirit Tree. Fools like them occasionally show up.”

“Right? Just kill them and save the trouble. We cannot bother the Asura Clan over such insignificant issues.”

However, the Alchemist who brought Yuan to their headquarters said, “That’s what I thought at first, too. But he’s unlike the others. There’s something different about him, and he’s strangely confident. Otherwise, he wouldn’t have followed me back here. What if he’s telling the truth? If we act too prematurely without even investigating a little and accidentally kill someone close to the Asura Clan… wouldn’t that spell the end of our faction?”

“…”

The elders found logic behind the Alchemist’s words, and the room quickly fell silent as they pondered their situation more seriously.

chapters.title: Chapter 2202 The Chaotic Sect'S Response
chapters.body:
Chapter 2202 The Chaotic Sect'S Response

“What Alchemist Shen said is logical. If those two are truly connected to the Asura Clan in some way and we kill them, it’d spell the end of our faction,” one of the elders said, breaking the silence.

“Then what should we do with them? The chances of them being ‘friends’ with the Asura Clan are slim to none, and if we bother the Asura Clan, it might affect our relationship with them,” said another elder.

“Why don’t we hold off on contacting the Asura Clan and examine them for a little while before we make any decision?”

“That’s a good idea. We can just stall this for a little. If they turn out to be troublemakers, we’ll kill them.”

Though the Inferno Alchemists’ stance might seem excessive, it revealed the depth of their fear toward the Asura Clan. In the Primordial Realm, even something as simple as approaching them to confirm an identity was anything but simple.

“I’ll let them know,” said Alchemist Shen, who guided Yuan and Mu Xuelian to their headquarters.

While the elders continued to contemplate the situation some more, Alchemist Shen returned to Yuan’s guest house.

“We will be sending someone to the Asura Clan to confirm your identity soon,” Alchemist Shen said to Yuan.

“So, how long is this going to take?”

“I’m not sure… but we’ll let you know immediately when we receive an answer.”

Yuan narrowed his eyes slightly and said, “There’s no way it should take more than a few hours, a few days at most. Don’t you have something to contact them directly?”

Alchemist Shen looked at him with a dumbfounded face and said, “Only our leader has direct communication with the Asura Clan, but even then, he can only use it during emergencies.”

Yuan sighed out loud, “Why are they so hard to contact?”

Alchemist Shen remained silent.

“In any case, we just need to wait, right? Alright, but don’t take too long.”

“Thank you for your understanding.”

Meanwhile, at the Chaotic Sect, the cultivator who managed to survive revealed the situation to the Sect Leader and other elders.

“What did you say?! A mere True Immortal killed four God Ascension cultivators?! Do you really expect me to believe that?!” The leader of the Chaotic Sect angrily shouted after receiving this news from the sole survivor.

“I swear on my soul it’s true!”

The room fell silent for a moment.

“Sect Leader Zu! We must avenge Elder Zi and the others!” One of the elders suddenly exclaimed.

“How dare that bastard kill people from our Chaotic Sect! Our elders, no less!”

Hearing that they wanted revenge, the cultivator quickly said, “No! We shouldn’t!”

Everyone in the room turned to look at the cultivator with deep frowns on their faces.

“Elder Jiang, what are you saying? Are you telling us not to avenge Elder Zi and the others? Are you out of your damn mind?!”

“Did you get brainwashed by that bastard or something, Elder Jiang?”

“Y-You don’t understand! That guy wasn’t human! He killed everyone in the blink of an eye, and he did so with immense ease! He might also have connections with the Asura Clan!” Elder Jiang exclaimed. “T-The Asura Clan…?”

The atmosphere in the room instantly changed at the mere mention of the Asura Clan. Google seaʀᴄh N0v3l.Fiɾe.net

“Elder Jiang.” The Sect Leader suddenly spoke in a chilling tone, causing him to tremble. “Putting the issue with the Asura Clan aside, are you claiming that a single Immortal is stronger than the entire Chaotic Sect, so we should not offend him?”

“That’s…” Elder Jiang could not find a proper response.

After a moment of silence, he shook his head and responded, “No, unless he’s as powerful as the head of the Asura Clan, there’s no way a single person could threaten our Chaotic Sect. I apologize. It seems I haven’t been thinking straight from fear.”

“Very well. Now, let’s get back to the other subject. You said he has a connection with the Asura Clan. How certain are you on that?”

“That’s… Honestly, I’m not very certain. He had me bring him to the Burning Tree. If he truly had a connection with the Asura Clan, he wouldn’t need me to take him there. However, there’s something about him that reminds me of those from the Asura Clan.”

The Sect Leader stood up and said, “Regardless of whether he’s related to the Asura Clan or not, I will not let someone who killed four of our people get away with it. Gather all of the elders. We’re going to avenge the others.”

“As you command!”

A week later, at the Inferno Alchemists’ headquarters.

“Master! We have an emergency! The Chaotic Sect’s leader and many of their members are currently at our gates!”

“The Chaotic Sect? What do they want from us?” The leader of the Inferno Alchemist was immediately alarmed by this news.

“They’re demanding your presence, and that’s all we know!” “What a bunch of arrogant bastards. Are they trying to start a war with us or something?”

The leader of the Inferno Alchemist went to meet with the Chaotic Sect shortly after.

“Sect Leader Zu, what is the meaning of this? To show up unannounced with so many of your elders… are you trying to invade us? Do you not fear the Asura Clan?”

Sect Leader Zu quickly responded, “Don’t worry, Leader Yang. We have no business with the Inferno Alchemists today. We’re here for that bastard who came to your place recently—the one who’s trying to go to the Burning Tree. Is he still here?”

“…”

Leader Yang responded, “Yes, he’s still here. Why are you looking for him?”

“That bastard killed four of our elders, and we’re here to make him pay for his crimes.”

“What…?” Leader Yang wore a dumbfounded look on his face as he was fed this information.

“Are you sure you have the right person? That man is only a True Immortal, and your elders are all at least God Ascension cultivators. Yet you claim he killed four of them?” he asked.

chapters.title: Chapter 2203 The Chaotic Sect'S Revenge
chapters.body:
Chapter 2203 The Chaotic Sect'S Revenge

“I am aware of how ridiculous it sounds, but it is the truth,” said Sect Leader Zu.

“Hmm…”

After a moment of silence, Leader Yang responded, “I understand your situation. Unfortunately, I cannot just hand him over to you.”

“Why are you protecting him?”

“First of all, he is our guest. If we hand him over simply because you demand it, our reputation will be tarnished. Second—and more importantly—that man may have ties to the Asura Clan. I trust I don’t need to spell out what that means,” said Leader Yang.

“Hahaha!” Sect Leader Zu suddenly burst out laughing. “Don’t tell me that man actually managed to fool the Inferno Alchemists with such an obvious lie!”

Leader Yang narrowed his eyes slightly and replied, “Are you saying that he’s lying about being connected with the Asura Clan?”

“Do I really have to answer such an obvious question?”

“Do you have any proof to back up your claim? Although we don’t have proof that he’s connected with the Asura Clan, we also don’t have evidence that he’s lying to us. It’s better to be safe than sorry.”

“Proof? I don’t need such a thing! Even an idiot can tell that he’s lying!”

“Then I may be an idiot. Can you enlighten me?”

“…”

Sect Leader Zu fell silent, as he did not have a proper response.

“Enough of the chitchat. Regardless of whether he’s related to the Asura Clan or not, it’s a fact that he killed four of my elders. This will be my last time asking. Will you hand him over to us or not?”

Sect Leader Zu’s aura suddenly started increasing.

“And what are you going to do if we refuse your demands? Attack us? Do you not fear the Asura Clan’s wrath?”

“Don’t worry, we won’t harm the Inferno Alchemists. However, we will drag him back with us even if we have to be a little forceful.”

“…”

Leader Yang fell silent. The Inferno Alchemists numbered barely a hundred; if the Chaotic Sect truly wished to annihilate them, resistance would be futile. However, due to their connection with the Asura Clan, the Chaotic Sect wouldn’t dare to do such a thing. Moreover, so long as no harm befell the Inferno Alchemists directly, the Chaotic Sect believed there was a good chance the Asura Clan would overlook the matter.

‘If we hand Tian Xian over to them, we would look like pushovers, and if he happened to really be connected to the Asura Clan, we would have no excuse. However, if we refuse and they forcefully take him away, our reputation would still take a blow, but at least, we can tell the Asura Clan that we had no choice since we’re no match for the Chaotic Sect.’

After weighing the choices, Leader Yang gave his answer, “My answer is no. We won’t hand him over to you. If you want to take him, you’ll have to do it with force.”

This way, the Inferno Alchemists wouldn’t be held responsible for Yuan’s death. Thɪs chapter is updated by novel-fire.net

Sect Leader Zu sneered before commanding the hundreds of disciples that came with him, “You heard him. Search every nook and cranny and bring that bastard back to me!” “Yes!”

At his command, the Chaotic Sect’s disciples invaded the Inferno Alchemists’ territory to look for Yuan.

“Leader! What should we do?! Are we really going to just stand here and let them invade our territory?! Even if they’re not here for us, they’ve crossed a line they shouldn’t!” The elders of the Inferno Alchemists asked as they watched the sea of disciples fly past them. Leader Yang shook his head and said, “There’s nothing we can do but let the Asura Clan deal with them.”

He turned to glare at Sect Leader Zu and spoke angrily, “You’ll regret this, Sect Leader Zu.”

In response, Sect Leader Zu merely sneered with disdain.

Meanwhile, Yuan stopped studying the Mystic Sword Tablet and sighed out loud, “Looks like I have guests.”

“Need my help?” Mu Xuelian asked as she watched him stand up and approach the exit.

“I’ll be fine.”

Stepping outside, Yuan spotted dozens of black-robed cultivators prowling the area, banging on doors as they wandered from house to house like a bunch of law enforcers looking for a fugitive.

“They sure brought a lot of trash…” Yuan sighed.

“H-Hey! I think that’s him!”

One of the disciples shouted when he saw Yuan.

“You might be right. He matches the description.” Yuan smiled and calmly spoke out loud, “If you’re looking for the one who killed your elders, you’ve definitely found him.”

“I-It’s him! Someone let the elders know that we have found him!”

The disciples immediately surrounded Yuan and his building.

Yuan swept the crowd with a mocking gaze. “Not a single God-Ascension among you—what do you expect to accomplish here?”

“With our Sect Leader and most of the elders present, resistance would be pointless. Do the sensible thing and surrender yourself peacefully.”

“Peace? You forfeited that option when you came here for revenge, even though I warned you—or did that one I spare not tell you not to come here?”

“Shut up! Who do you think you are, telling us what to do? Did you really expect us not to seek revenge after you killed four of our elders?!” one of them snarled, his tone full of killing intent.

“No, I figured you would come after me. However, I still held onto some hope. Anyway, let’s kill some time before your elders arrive, shall we?”

Before the disciples could even react, Yuan struck first. With most of them being at the Immortal Realm, it took him little effort to cut them down. Of course, he didn’t wield any weapon and used only his Supreme Sword Aura.

In mere moments, Yuan reduced their numbers by half, staining the place with their blood.

“W-What a monster!”

“How is he this powerful when we have the same cultivation?!”

“Fuck! I can see how he defeated our elders!”

Until they faced Yuan’s overwhelming might firsthand, most of the disciples had doubted that he could have slain their elders.

“We must keep him here until the elders arrive at all costs!”

chapters.title: Chapter 2204 Unfathomable Killing Intent
chapters.body:
Chapter 2204 Unfathomable Killing Intent

“Keep me here…?” A smile appeared on Yuan’s face as he stopped his killing spree. “I believe there is a misunderstanding. I have no intention of going anywhere, much less running away.”

“Do you really believe you can defeat the Chaotic Sect by yourself?! Our leader is at the 7th level of God Ascension powerhouse! He’s not someone you can even touch, let alone defeat!” “Seventh level, huh? It might be a little difficult, but it’s definitely doable.”

The disciples stared at him with stunned disbelief. After a moment of silence, they all burst out laughing.

“Hahaha! He really thinks he can defeat our Sect Leader! What a fool!” Get full chapters from novel✶fire.net

“That’s already beyond foolishness!” Meanwhile, at the Sect Leader’s location.

“Sect Leader! Elders! We have found that bastard!” declared the disciple who left to find them.

“Oh? Take us there.”

“Right away!”

Sect Leader Zu and the elders hurried after the disciple to Yuan’s guest house. What greeted them there left them stunned—the ground was strewn with the mutilated corpses of their disciples, blood soaking the earth in a grisly scene.

“Took you long enough,” said Yuan, who was casually sitting by his doorstep.

“I-Impossible… He killed all of them in such a short time?!”

The disciple who went to find the others was terrified. After all, it’d only been ten minutes at most since he left. “You bastard…” Sect Leader Zu growled through clenched teeth, fury blazing in his eyes. “Not only did you kill four of our elders, but you also slaughtered dozens of our disciples now!”

“You must be the Sect Leader,” said Yuan after noticing his cultivation at the 7th level of God Ascension. “Didn’t you get my warning? I warned you that if you came after me after I spared one of your elders, that I would kill all of you, right?”

Yuan’s gaze shifted to Elder Jiang, whom he’d spared.

“Who do you think you are to threaten my Chaotic Sect?! Not even the Asura Clan has threatened us!” Sect Leader Zu roared. “And you killed four of our elders! Do you really expect us to sit back and pretend it never happened?!”

“Why are you making it sound like I killed your elders for no reason? In the first place, your elders tried to rob and kill me, something you approved of. Was it wrong for me to kill them? Am I not allowed to defend myself? You should count yourself lucky I didn’t visit the Chaotic Sect to kill you. And yet here you are, marching to my door. You must really have a death wish.”

“Why are you making it sound like I killed your elders for no reason? In the first place, your elders tried to rob and kill me, something you approved of. Was it wrong for me to kill them? Am I not allowed to defend myself? You should count yourself lucky I didn’t visit the Chaotic Sect to kill you. And yet here you are, marching to my door. You must really have a death wish.”

Sect Leader Zu trembled, his blood boiling with rage. For millions of years, no one—not even the other powerhouses—had dared speak to him in such a manner. Yet now, a mere Immortal stood before him, looking down on him as if he were nothing.

A powerful aura erupted from Sect Leader Zu, flooding the air with a torrent of killing intent. Sensing his killing intent, even his own disciples trembled in fear.

“…”

Of all the foes Yuan had faced in this life, none radiated more killing intent than Sect Leader Zu, a testament to the countless lives he had already taken.

After countless eras, few cultivators from the Primordial Era still remained, and Sect Leader Zu was one of the rare survivors. Thus, it made sense for him to exude such killing intent. “Look at you—paralyzed with fear from nothing more than my killing intent,” Sect Leader Zu sneered, watching Yuan stand motionless, eyes slightly widened.

A cold smile curved Yuan’s lips. “Fear? I’ll admit, your killing intent is impressive, but only in the eyes of ordinary people.”

“Hahaha! Still pretending to be tough, huh? Then how about this?!”

Sect Leader Zu unleashed the full extent of his killing intent, so dense and potent that it began to take form in the air, a feat Yuan himself had displayed before.

With this much killing intent filling the air, the disciples around him, including the elders, subconsciously started backing away.

Channeling his killing intent, Sect Leader Zu forged a spear of crimson and black, its tip leveled menacingly at Yuan.

“Let’s see if you can still act so arrogantly after having a taste of my killing intent!” Yuan watched calmly as the spear hurtled toward him, making no move to evade. It pierced straight through his body, yet left no wound. Unlike Sword Aura or spiritual energy, pure killing intent held no power to inflict physical harm despite its seemingly solid form, which was why it passed through him so effortlessly.

Instead, killing intent assailed the mind. For those lacking mental strength or unaccustomed to its pressure, it could shatter their mind, leaving them a hollow shell, or worse, killing them outright.

Sect Leader Zu and the others watched with smug expressions, waiting for Yuan to break into screams.

Yet, that moment did not come.

Yuan slowly closed his eyes, and at once the atmosphere shifted. A red aura seeped from his body, spreading through the air and draping the place in an ominous presence.

“W-What is this…?” The disciples’ eyes widened as killing intent poured from Yuan’s body, swelling with each passing moment until it rivaled Sect Leader Zu’s killing intent.

But Yuan’s killing intent did not stop—it surged higher and higher until it pierced the black sky. His eyes bled crimson, and behind him unfurled a pair of ominous wings, seemingly wrought from blood itself.

“This is…”

Sect Leader Zu swallowed hard, his eyes wide with disbelief and horror. Yuan’s killing intent didn’t just eclipse his own. It devoured it, swallowing his aura whole.

Despite the countless lives he had taken over tens of millions of years, Sect Leader Zu could not even begin to fathom how many Yuan must have slain to cultivate such unfathomable killing intent.

Meanwhile, not far from their location and hidden within its own dimension, a handsome middle-aged man with sharp features suddenly opened his eyes, revealing irises of deep violet.

“This killing intent…” he murmured, a flicker of nostalgia stirring within him.

chapters.title: Chapter 2205 Fighting Sect Leader Zu
chapters.body:
Chapter 2205 Fighting Sect Leader Zu

“This killing intent… it feels familiar…” the middle-aged man murmured as he rose to his feet, stepping onto the surface of a small, tranquil pond where he had been seated in meditation.

After a moment of silence, he spoke in a slightly loud and clear voice, “Xiao Meilin.”

In the next moment, a tall and beautiful woman with facial features similar to Xiao Hua but much more mature appeared before him like a ghost.

“Is this about the killing intent?” she asked.

He nodded, “Investigate it.”

“Could it be… him?” The man turned to look at a certain object in the distance and responded after a moment of pause, “It’s a little earlier than anticipated, but it’s possible.”

A broad and somewhat sadistic smile appeared on Xiao Meilin’s face.

“I’ll be back,” she said before vanishing like smoke.

She disappeared the next instant.

Back at the Inferno Alchemist’s headquarters, once Yuan’s killing intent stopped increasing, he created a sword using it.

“Do you dare to ‘experience’ my killing intent?” Yuan asked in a provocative tone as he glared at Sect Leader Zu.

“That’s…!”

Sect Leader Zu hesitated.

Meanwhile, his disciples were all shaking uncontrollably just from being in the presence of Yuan’s killing intent.

“What’s with the hesitation? Don’t tell me… you’re afraid?” Yuan laughed, his voice dripping with mockery.

“DO NOT UNDERESTIMATE ME!” Sect Leader Zu roared, taking Yuan’s obvious bait as he flung his arms wide in a defiant, inviting gesture.

Without a word, Yuan flicked his sleeve, launching the sword of killing intent straight into Sect Leader Zu’s chest.

“Sect Leader!” his disciples cried in alarm, their confidence in his strength wavering as worry clouded their eyes.

In the next instant, Sect Leader Zu’s pupils blew wide, his eyes darkening until nearly black. He threw his head back and screamed in agony.

“AHHHHH!!!”

An unfathomable surge of killing intent flooded into him, dragging his mind into a torrent of unending nightmares.

“S-Sect Leader!” “You bastard! What did you do to our Sect Leader?! There’s no way that was just your killing intent!”

“He must have used some sort of technique!”

However, Yuan ignored them and continued watching Sect Leader Zu writhe in pain.

A minute later, blood trickled from Sect Leader Zu’s eyes. Moments after, it seeped from his ears, nose, and even his pores—until, in the blink of an eye, his entire body was drenched in crimson.

“K-Kill him!” For more chapters visıt novel·fire·net

One of the elders suddenly pointed at Yuan and roared.

Though fear gnawed at them, hundreds of disciples hurled themselves at Yuan, their own killing intent blazing. Yuan said nothing, and he simply awakened the True Dragon within and advanced to meet them head-on.

While Sect Leader Zu battled Yuan’s killing intent in the depths of his mind, his disciples clashed with Yuan in reality.

One minute… three minutes… ten minutes.

It took Sect Leader Zu thirteen grueling minutes, but at last he broke free from Yuan’s killing intent. Yet the reality that awaited him was not much better than the nightmares. Not only the ground, but even Yuan’s guest house was drenched in blood, its walls adorned with severed limbs and spilled entrails.

In the time it took Sect Leader Zu to break out of Yuan’s killing intent, Yuan had slaughtered half of his disciples.

“What… What is this…?” he muttered with a look of disbelief.

Yuan shrugged, “Don’t look at me. They attacked me first.”

“Y-You bastard!!!”

Once Sect Leader Zu snapped out of his daze and understood the situation more clearly, his body erupted with killing intent and spiritual energy.

“I’ll skin you alive!” Sect Leader Zu lunged at Yuan while wielding a Mythic-grade spear.

Yuan produced Number One Under Heaven and enhanced himself with Chaotic Essence to confront the Sect Leader.

“A Soul Weapon?!”

Sect Leader Zu almost couldn’t believe his eyes when he saw Yuan’s weapon, as there was no Soul Weapon in the Primordial Realm—at least until today. In fact, Mythic-grade was the highest quality of treasure that could be found within the Primordial Realm. Sect Leader Zu was not only injured and drained from resisting Yuan’s killing intent, but his weapon was also far inferior. His sole advantage lay in his cultivation at the seventh level of God Ascension—and if not for that, he would have already fallen to Yuan within a hundred exchanges.

“There’s no way…”

The watching disciples were struck speechless, their faces etched with disbelief. None of them could comprehend how their Sect Leader struggled against Yuan, a mere True Immortal that he should have been able to crush with a flick of his finger.

The elders eventually snapped out of their daze and shouted, “We need to help the Sect Leader!”

Without hesitation, they joined the battle against Yuan.

“By the heavens, what am I watching right now?”

The alchemists watching from afar were utterly bewildered. Yuan’s overwhelming prowess, effortlessly suppressing the Chaotic Sect alone, stirred memories of the Asura Clan.

“Don’t tell me he actually has connections to the Asura Clan…”

Leader Yang swallowed nervously and couldn’t help but feel relieved that they didn’t act too hastily and kill—try to kill Yuan. Not only would they have been slaughtered, but they would’ve also offended the Asura Clan.

“Hm? This feeling…”

Leader Yang’s brows shot up, and he quickly turned away from the battle before him as he sensed a familiar presence suddenly approaching their direction at the speed of light.

Before Leader Yang could even gather his thoughts, the owner of the presence appeared before the alchemists like a ghost, startling them.

However, when they saw and recognized this person’s beautiful face, they became even more terrified.

“Y-Y-You are! Crimson Empress!” “We greet the Crimson Empress!”

Leader Yang and the alchemists instinctively bowed. Yet the woman gave them no notice, as though they didn’t exist, and her gaze fixed solely on the two figures locked in battle nearby—or more specifically—the handsome man wielding a greatsword.

“Ahh…” The Crimson Empress suddenly released a euphoric moan.

“At last, you’ve returned…”

chapters.title: Chapter 2206 The Crimson Empress' Appearance
chapters.body:
Chapter 2206 The Crimson Empress' Appearance

“Do you recognize that man?” Leader Yang asked Xiao Meilin after hearing her mumbling.

However, she didn’t respond.

“Senior Crimson Emper—?”

Without turning to look at him, Xiao Meilin’s attention remained fixated on Yuan as she spoke in a chilling tone, “You’re disturbing a significant and special moment for me. If I hear another peep out of your mouth without my permission, I’ll kill you.”

Though she exuded no killing intent, Xiao Meilin’s words terrified Leader Yang and the other alchemists more than Yuan’s killing intent, and they no longer dared to open their mouths, even going as far as to halt their breathing just in case that annoyed her.

Meanwhile, Yuan continued to fight Sect Leader Zu. Although the other disciples tried to help, the clash between the two of them was so intense that they couldn’t even get close.

As the battle dragged on, Sect Leader Zu’s confidence gave way to unease. Initially, he was unconcerned about failing to overpower Yuan outright, given that his seventh-level God Ascension cultivation guaranteed significantly greater spiritual energy reserves. Sooner or later, Yuan should have been exhausted. Yet the expected weakness never came. Instead, with every clash, Yuan seemed only to grow stronger.

‘No! He’s not getting stronger! I am the one getting weaker!’ Sect Leader Zu cried inwardly.

‘How?! How the fuck does an Immortal have this much spiritual energy?! Or does he have a way to replenish his spiritual energy—’

“Bastard! You must be secretly recovering your spiritual energy with spirit stones! How many do you actually possess?!” Sect Leader Zu roared, misunderstanding the situation.

Since he couldn’t sense Chaotic Essence, there was no way for him to realize that Yuan was recovering his spiritual energy with it.

“Spirit Stones?” A smile appeared on Yuan’s face as he spoke, “Even if I were using them, so what?”

Sect Leader Zu gritted his teeth and suddenly stopped attacking.

“Wait! Let’s talk about this!”

“What?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“Think about it. Even if you’re using spirit stones to recover, you can’t have many left. Keep this up, and you’ll inevitably run out of spirit stones and still die by my hands. So why waste them? If you agree to apologize and hand over every last stone, I’ll spare your life and pretend as if none of this ever happened,” Sect Leader Zu said with a confident smile.

“Think about it. Even if you’re using spirit stones to recover, you can’t have many left. Keep this up, and you’ll inevitably run out of spirit stones and still die by my hands. So why waste them? If you agree to apologize and hand over every last stone, I’ll spare your life and pretend as if none of this ever happened,” Sect Leader Zu said with a confident smile.

Yuan fell silent for a moment before bursting out with laughter.

“Run out of spirit stones? Why don’t you see for yourself whether I’ll run out or not!” Yuan snapped open his spatial ring, releasing a flood of spirit stones that swiftly piled into a glittering mountain before him.

The jaws of Sect Leader Zu and the others dropped at the absurd, almost obscene sight. There were thousands—millions of spirit stones, enough to make everyone in the Primordial Realm a cultivator.

“Impossible! Why does he have so many spirit stones?!”

“They must be fake! There’s no way he has so much!” Suddenly, Sect Leader Zu clenched his fists and muttered, “You… there’s been a rumor of someone from the outside world entering the Primordial Realm going around recently. I didn’t believe it at first, but now… You must be that person!”

“What?! He came from the outside world?! Does that mean the Primordial Realm is open again?!”

The disciples couldn’t believe their ears, their eyes glued to the spirit stones.

However, Yuan quickly stored the spirit stones back inside his spatial ring the next moment.

“Now then, let’s see who falls first: you, or I when I run out of spirit stones,” Yuan said, his glare sharp as a blade.

Sect Leader Zu trembled at the challenge. ᴛʜɪs ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀ ɪs ᴜᴘᴅᴀᴛᴇ ʙʏ n͟o͟v͟e͟l͟f͟i͟r͟e͟.net

“I—!”

But just as Sect Leader Zu opened his mouth to respond, a surge of killing intent exploded nearby, crashing over the battlefield like a tidal wave.

Before Sect Leader Zu could even turn his head to face the source of the killing intent, it appeared behind him like a ghost and—

Pop!

Sect Leader Zu’s head suddenly exploded like a juicy watermelon.

“?!” Yuan’s eyes widened at this sudden development.

“Y-You are—! Crimson Empress?! What is the meaning of this?!” Sect Leader Zu, whose head was crushed but still survived as a soul, exclaimed in shock after seeing the identity of his attacker.

“Why? Because you ruined such a precious moment for me,” she responded in a nonchalant tone.

“What?! What nonsense are you—” Sect Leader Zu’s words died in his throat as he felt her cold glare fall upon him. In that instant, he was sure that if he dared to continue, she would shatter his soul.

“Crimson Empress…” Yuan muttered as he admired her beautiful and familiar appearance, recalling when Xiao Hua disguised herself as her in the past.

Xiao Meilin stopped paying attention to Sect Leader Zu without a second thought, turning instead to meet Yuan’s gaze with eyes burning with passion.

Yuan opened his mouth to speak, but before he could utter a word, Xiao Meilin suddenly charged forward and launched an attack on him.

“?!?!”

Her unexpected action left Leader Yang and Sect Leader Zu speechless.

‘She wasn’t on his side?!’ they cried inwardly.

“Since that bastard ruined it, allow me to continue it,” Xiao Meilin muttered in a low voice as she retrieved a Mythic-grade sword and swung it at Yuan. “What are you doing?” Yuan asked with a calm expression on his face as he blocked her attack.

Despite being attacked, there was no killing intent coming from Xiao Meilin, so he wasn’t worried that she was trying to kill him.

“Please excuse my behavior, Master Tian. However, I just cannot contain it any longer—”

A crushing pressure swept through the area as Xiao Meilin’s body erupted with the aura of a ninth-level God Ascension cultivator, intertwined with a surge of intense Supreme Sword Aura.

“My love and lust for you, that is!” she shouted as she continued her assault.

Although Xiao Meilin did not seem to have any desire to kill him, Yuan didn’t dare to underestimate her and instantly used Eternal Essence.

chapters.title: Chapter 2207 The Crimson Empress' Appearance(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2207 The Crimson Empress' Appearance(2)

“You have no idea how long I have waited for this moment, Master Tian!” Xiao Meilin laughed chaotically as she swung her sword mercilessly, despite the difference in their cultivation.

“You’re really enjoying yourself, aren’t you?” Yuan remarked with a stiff smile on his face.

For some reason, he didn’t feel the need to tell her to stop attacking him, almost as if he knew that it would be pointless and that the only way for her to stop was to satisfy her.

Meanwhile, Leader Yang and the others were left speechless by the unexpected situation.

“Why are they fighting? I thought they were friends?”

“You idiot, look at the Crimson Empress’ face! I have never seen her smile before, yet she’s smiling from ear to ear right now!”

“Y-You’re right! Good lord, who is that man?!” The men there couldn’t help but feel smitten by Xiao Meilin at this moment, even though they were terrified of her just moments ago, and it was caused by a single smile.

As for Yuan, he was barely just strong enough to fight Xiao Meilin without being completely overpowered.

Xiao Meilin was at the ninth level of God Ascension, essentially the peak of the cultivation world. Moreover, unlike the other cultivators, she possessed an abundance of spiritual energy in her Dantian, allowing her to fight without restraint.

Of course, she wasn’t using Celestial Qi, or Yuan wouldn’t stand a chance even with Eternal Essence.

A minute… ten minutes… an hour passed in the blink of an eye.

As Yuan continued fighting Xiao Meilin, his memories of the Asura Clan started to resurface, such as their first encounter, how they started as enemies but ended as allies, and his relationship with Xiao Meilin and the other members.

Noticing a profound flicker in Yuan’s eyes, Xiao Meilin remarked, “You dare think about other things while clashing swords with me? That arrogance—you haven’t changed one bit since we last met during the Primordial Era!”

Yuan smiled and said, “I can say the same to you. Don’t tell me you’ve spent your time in this place cultivating?”

“Isn’t that obvious? There’s nothing else to do in this shithole besides cultivating.”

“You were only at the second level of God Ascension the last time I saw you. To reach the ninth level while stuck in this place… very impressive.”

Xiao Meilin chuckled as she swung her sword, “You, on the other hand, have gotten a lot weaker.”

“Are you disappointed?”

“…”

After several exchanges in silence, Xiao Meilin responded with a sadistic grin on her beautiful face, “Seeing you weaker than me… it’s actually very exciting.”

Xiao Meilin’s expression sent chills down Yuan’s spine. “It won’t stay like this for long,” he quickly said.

“Then I should take advantage of this moment while I still can,” she said while licking her lips.

The next moment, Xiao Meilin’s prowess skyrocketed, and she started pushing back Yuan with seemingly no effort.

Even when using nearly every trick he had, Yuan still found himself completely overwhelmed by Xiao Meilin’s prowess.

‘Not only is she not using her Celestial Qi, but even her weapon is lacking! Yet, she’s overpowering me to such an extent!’ Yuan couldn’t help but smile despite the situation.

If Xiao Meilin were released to the outside world and upgraded her weapon, who knows how much more of a monster she would become?

After being tossed around by Xiao Meilin for several more hours, Yuan finally said, “Alright, how much longer do you intend to mess around? I need to speak to your father. It’s about Xiao Hua.”

Xiao Meilin stopped her movements and lowered her sword.

“My junior sister? Now that you mentioned her, where is she? Did you not come here with her?” she said as she looked around.

“She’s currently in my Dantian, but she cannot come out due to reasons.”

Xiao Meilin narrowed her eyes slightly and mumbled, “Could it be… did it already start?”

“What do you mean?” Yuan asked after hearing this.

Xiao Meilin shook her head and said, “I’ll let father explain it to you instead. I’ll take you to him now.”

“Alright.” Yuan nodded as he stowed away his weapon.

“Ah, right. Before we leave…” Xiao Meilin’s expression suddenly hardened as her cold gaze swept over Sect Leader Zu and the others. “What should we do with them? Want me to kill them all?”

Despite Xiao Meilin not paying attention to them, none of them dared to leave the scene. After all, even if they snuck away, they would never be able to truly escape. Orıginal content can be found at Novᴇl_Fire(.)net

“Have mercy!” Sect Leader Zu, who was still in his soul form, pleaded. However, Yuan remained indifferent as he commanded, “Kill them all.”

“As you wish.”

Immense killing intent erupted from Xiao Meilin the next instant, her body shrouded in a crimson aura that bled into the air, saturating the atmosphere with malice.

As Xiao Meilin raised her sword, every member of the Chaotic Sect instinctively turned to flee. But before they could escape, she brought her blade down in a single, fluid motion. In the blink of an eye, the air split—and so did they, their bodies torn apart into countless pieces all at once.

‘Power of the Void!’ To Yuan’s astonishment, Xiao Meilin was wielding the Power of the Void, an ability so rare that few cultivators could even comprehend it, let alone command it to such a degree, and although slightly, her understanding surpassed even his.

“What do you think?” Xiao Meilin asked, turning to Yuan with a faint flicker in her eyes, as though expecting his praise after annihilating the Chaotic Sect.

“Where’d you learn to wield the Power of the Void?” he asked.

“I comprehended it through studying Spiritual Rifts,” she calmly responded.

“Seriously? How long did that take?”

“Longer than it took me to reach the ninth level of God Ascension.”

“I see…”

“Ready to leave?” Xiao Meilin then asked.

“Yes—Actually, hold on.”

Yuan nearly forgot about Mu Xuelian and went to fetch her.

“Who’s that?” Xiao Meilin asked, her eyes narrowing in suspicion as they fell upon Mu Xuelian.

chapters.title: Chapter 2208 Meeting The Asura Clan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2208 Meeting The Asura Clan

“This is a friend of mine, Mu Xuelian,” Yuan casually introduced her.

“Hmm…” Xiao Meilin suddenly vanished into thin air before appearing before Mu Xuelian like a ghost.

“Can I help you?” Mu Xuelian calmly asked.

Xiao Meilin narrowed her eyes slightly and responded, “There’s something about you that I don’t like… What are you hiding?”

Yuan raised an eyebrow at Xiao Meilin’s words and wondered if she could sense Shiva, who was hiding within Mu Xuelian’s body. If so, that would be pretty impressive.

Sure enough, Xiao Meilin could sense something mysterious lurking within Mu Xuelian, something that made even a powerhouse like her instinctively tread with caution.

“I don’t know what you’re talking about,” Mu Xuelian shook her head.

“Whatever. Just don’t cause any trouble.”

Xiao Meilin vanished into thin air again. This time, she reappeared right beside Yuan.

“Let’s go, shall we?” Xiao Meilin said as she grabbed his arm and pulled him forward, speeding into the distance.

Sometime later, they arrived at the Burning Tree—a colossal black tree wreathed in purple flames. Beside it lay a vast, dried-up lake, its cracked surface reflecting the eerie glow of the fire.

Yuan gazed at the tree, his eyes narrowing slightly. Though its flames burned with fierce intensity, the heat was far weaker than he’d anticipated, and there was something inherently unusual about their nature.

“What kind of fire is that?” Yuan asked, unable to discern its nature.

Xiao Meilin shrugged. “Who knows? That tree was once just an ordinary one. But after being struck by countless bolts of lightning, it caught fire, and the flames have never gone out. It’s been burning for tens of millions of years now.”

‘I believe it’s a type of alchemy flame,’ Feng Yuxiang remarked within Yuan’s Dantian.

‘Alchemy flames, huh? Is there a way for me to collect it?’ he asked.

‘Yes, there is. Inside my spatial ring, you’ll find the Phoenix Sealing Jar. It’s a treasure that is specifically used to collect flames.’

Yuan ignored the ominous name and searched Feng Yuxiang’s spatial ring for the item. A moment later, he used it to collect the purple flames.

Xiao Meling watched his actions without uttering a word, almost as if it was none of her concern.

Only when Yuan finished with his business did Xiao Meilin speak, “Done?”

“Yes.”

Without another word, she swept her arm through the air, rending space itself open and revealing a pitch-black gateway to the void before entering it.

Yuan and Mu Xuelian silently followed her.

Moments after stepping into the void, a flash of blinding light engulfed Yuan. When his vision cleared, he found himself beneath a radiant sun suspended in a clear blue sky—a striking contrast to the apocalyptic gloom of the Primordial Realm he had just left behind. Not only that, but spiritual energy was abundant around them, almost as if they’d returned to the Nine Heavens.

“Did we leave the Primordial Realm?” Mu Xuelian questioned as she looked around with a puzzled face.

“No, we’re still inside the Primordial Realm,” Xiao Meilin said.

“But the spiritual energy here…”

“It’s from the Spirit Tree.”

“What is the Spirit Tree, anyway?” Yuan suddenly asked.

“You’ll find out soon enough,” Xiao Meilin replied with a mysterious smile.

“Now, let’s meet the old man.”

Not far ahead stood a vast estate, its ancient architecture reminiscent of the grandeur of the Primordial Era.

Even without using his divine sense, Yuan could sense many powerful presences coming from it.

When they reached the entrance, they were greeted by a handsome young man who looked to be in his early twenties.

“Let me introduce you to Xiao Chen, the youngest in our family. He’s my little brother.”

“Hm?” Yuan raised an eyebrow at this information and remarked, “But I don’t recall such an individual in the Asura Clan.”

“That’s because he was born after we entered the Primordial Realm,” Xiao Meilin explained. Follow current novᴇls on NoveI~Fire.net

“Born? Your father hooked up with someone in this place?” Yuan wore a dumbfounded look on his face at this revelation.

“Something like that.”

“Does that mean you have a new mother right now?”

Xiao Meilin silently nodded.

“…”

A strange sensation suddenly rippled through Yuan, stirring a buried memory of Xiao Meilin’s first mother and his bitter connection with her.

“You must be Master Tian. It’s an honor to finally meet you after all these years,” said Xiao Chen, snapping Yuan out of his thoughts.

Yuan looked at him and said, “You don’t have to address me as Master Tian since I never taught you. Just call me Yuan.”

“Meilin, you too. Tian Xian is long dead. I am now Yuan,” he continued while staring her in the eye.

“Is that an order?” she asked with a perplexed look in her gaze.

“No, it’s not.”

“Then I will continue to call you Master Tian since it’s what I am used to,” she calmly responded.

“If that’s what makes you more comfortable,” Yuan said with a defeated smile.

“Anyway, you can get to know more about Xiao Chen later. The old man is waiting for you.”

Yuan nodded, and they stepped into the residence. Yet instead of stopping inside, they continued straight through the estate and into the line of trees beyond.

A few minutes later, they reached a tranquil lake, where a handsome middle-aged man with a naturally sharp and stern countenance sat cross-legged upon the water’s surface, silently cultivating.

“Go ahead,” Xiao Meilin said to Yuan.

Yuan silently approached the lake the next moment.

When Yuan reached the edge of the lake, the handsome man slowly opened his eyes and spoke in a soft tone, “Forgive me, but I cannot move from this spot right now, much less give you a proper greeting.”

Yuan narrowed his eyes and asked, “Is there something wrong?”

“I must remain focused right now, so I’ll explain everything to you in a bit.”

“Take your time,” Yuan said before sitting down on the soft grass.

“Thank you, my lord.” The middle-aged man closed his eyes again and fell silent.

chapters.title: Chapter 2209 Meeting The Asura Clan(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2209 Meeting The Asura Clan(2)

While Yuan waited for the Patriarch of the Asura Clan to finish with his business, he recalled Tian Xian’s past with them.

After avenging Xiu Mei’s death, Tian Xian did not stop his rampage. Since the Celestial Emperor was the true reason behind Xiu Mei’s assassination, he swore that he would not stop until he killed the Celestial Emperor.

Aware of Tian Xian’s desire for his life, the Celestial Emperor had no choice but to dispatch the Asura Clan—his strongest allies at the time—to confront him.

Before the Asura Clan betrayed the Celestial Emperor and joined Tian Xian’s side, they were the most powerful family in the Divine Heavens with influence that surpassed even the Celestial Family.

However, as powerful as they were, they still obeyed the Celestial Emperor. While most people swore their loyalty to the Celestial Emperor out of fear or desire, the Asura Clan assisted the Celestial Emperor mostly because their ancestor, their first Patriarch, Xiao Xin, was a close friend of the Celestial Emperor.

At the Celestial Emperor’s orders, the Asura Clan sent elite warriors to deal with Tian Xian, but alas, not even they were able to stop Tian Xian. If anything, it ignited his fury and onslaught even further.

When even their most seasoned warriors failed to halt Tian Xian’s rampage, Xiao Xin commanded his son, Xiao Cangming, and his daughter-in-law, Fei Zhen’er, to confront him.

Xiao Cangming and Fei Zhen’er were the most powerful in the Asura Clan, only slightly weaker than Xiao Xin himself individually. Together, not only were they stronger than Xiao Xin, but they would even be able to give the Celestial Emperor a fair fight.

With such a powerful combo going after Tian Xian, everyone figured that it was only a matter of time before Tian Xian took his last breath.

Yet, after Xiao Cangming and Fei Zhen’er finally clashed with Tian Xian, the outcome sent ripples of shock throughout the entire cultivation world.

Not only did they fail to defeat Tian Xian, but the battle also claimed the life of Fei Zhen’er. Her death struck Xiao Cangming especially hard, as he had opposed his father’s decision to aid the Celestial Emperor from the start, and he had never wanted Fei Zhen’er to be involved. Dıscover more novels at NoveI~Fire.net

Many expected Xiao Cangming to pursue Tian Xian—the murderer of his wife—just as Tian Xian had once hunted Supreme General Mo for slaying Xiu Mei. Yet, to everyone’s shock, Xiao Cangming turned his fury and blade not toward Tian Xian, but toward his own father, Xiao Xin.

After killing his own father, Xiao Cangming turned his attention to the Celestial Emperor, who was baffled and enraged by this betrayal.

After a series of fateful encounters, Tian Xian and the Asura Clan eventually began working together. Naturally, their relationship was strained at first—after all, Tian Xian had slain Fei Zhen’er. Yet, as time passed and their cooperation deepened, the hostility between them slowly began to fade.

In time, Xiao Cangming came to forgive Tian Xian completely. However, Xiao Cangming had two daughters with Fei Zhen’er, and they still hated Tian Xian even after their father forgave him. Although they, too, would eventually come to forgive Tian Xian, it would take much longer, especially the youngest daughter.

A few days after Yuan began delving into Tian Xian’s memories, Xiao Cangming—who had been calmly cultivating atop the lake—suddenly opened his eyes and unleashed a deafening roar that shook the entire world. Snapping out of his trance, Yuan quickly assessed the scene: Xiao Cangming, once serene, now writhed in agony, veins bulging across his face and body.

“Meilin, is this normal?!” Yuan turned to look at her for answers.

“Yes, it’s normal,” she calmly responded. “He’ll be done in a few minutes.”

Witnessing her calmness—even nonchalance, Yuan quickly calmed down, and he continued to analyze Xiao Cangming’s state.

As time passed, blood started bleeding from Xiao Cangming’s eyes, nose, and ears. ‘He looks poisoned, but it cannot be something that simple… A curse, perhaps?’ Yuan tried figuring out Xiao Cangming’s condition but came up empty-handed.

A few minutes later, Xiao Cangming slowly calmed down, just as Xiao Meilin said.

After splashing his face with the cool water below, Xiao Cangming rose from the pond in a single motion, landing before Yuan with effortless grace.

“Thank you for waiting, my lord. How long has it been since we last met?”

“I’m not sure, since I have yet to restore all of my memories,” Yuan shrugged.

“Is that so… Well, the last time you visited, you were called Tian Yi.”

“Tian Yi…? The Immortal Monarch?” Yuan was surprised to learn that he’d visited the Asura Clan as the Immortal Monarch.

“That was several eras ago—at least a hundred million years.”

“In that case, it’s definitely been a while. Anyway, let’s return to my residence first. We have a lot to talk about, after all.”

“Alright.”

Yuan followed Xiao Cangming back to the Asura Clan’s residence.

As Yuan, Xiao Cangming, and Xiao Meilin entered the room, Xiao Chen escorted Mu Xuelian to the guest room to relax.

Once they were seated, Xiao Cangming looked at Yuan and asked, “Where do we begin?”

“Why don’t we start with your condition? Are you poisoned? Cursed? Even though Xiao Meilin has managed to reach the peak of God Ascension, you, on the other hand…” Yuan said.

Unlike Xiao Meilin, Xiao Cangming’s cultivation was only at the first level of God Ascension—far weaker than before he entered the Primordial Realm. Rather than advancing, his cultivation had actually regressed.

“My condition is neither poison nor a curse. It’s something akin to an illness,” said Xiao Cangming.

“What?” Yuan raised his eyebrows in a puzzled manner. “An illness? I don’t recall you ever being sick.”

“Well, it started after your last visit. More specifically, after I started growing the Spirit Tree,” he explained.
