“The Spirit Tree? You grew it? I thought it was something that originated from the Primordial Realm.”

Xiao Cangming shook his head and said, “No, it was entrusted to me by Tian Yi—by you during your last visit.”

“What?!” Yuan exclaimed at this revelation.

chapters.title: Chapter 2210 Meeting The Asura Clan(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2210 Meeting The Asura Clan(3)

“I told you to grow the Spirit Tree? Then…”

Seeing Yuan’s reaction, Xiao Cangming could guess why he was worried and said, “Indeed, I grew the Spirit Tree under your instruction. However, my illness did not originate from the Spirit Tree. That being said, it is related to my illness.”

“What?” Yuan raised his eyebrows in a puzzled manner.

Xiao Cangming continued to explain, “Firstly, about my weakened cultivation. It has nothing to do with my illness. To grow the Spirit Tree, I had to feed it my cultivation. Naturally, my cultivation would decline afterward, so I would have to regain my cultivation and repeat the process. I had recently finished feeding the Spirit Tree my cultivation, hence why I am only at the first level of God Ascension.”

Yuan swallowed nervously before asking, “How many times did you feed the Spirit Tree?”

Xiao Cangming fell silent for a moment before responding in a nonchalant tone, “Ten times. I cultivated to the peak of God Ascension from the first level ten times, so this will be my eleventh time.”

Yuan stared in shock at such a ridiculous answer. Although ten may seem like a small number, one must consider the time and effort it takes to reach the ninth level of God Ascension from the first level.

The vast majority of people cannot even do it once in their lifetime, much less ten. Even if one possesses the innate talent and ability to reach the peak of God Ascension, it’d take them millions of years to achieve it a single time.

Xiao Cangming continued, “Normally, the process isn’t as painful as what you witnessed today, but that has become the routine after I was inflicted with the illness.”

“Inflicted? So someone gave you the illness?” “Not someone. Something.” Xiao Cangming sighed.

“Not long after the Spirit Tree sprouted, something tried to devour it,” Xiao Cangming said. “It came without warning—like a lightning strike from a clear sky. I tried to stop it, and after years of fighting, I managed to keep the Spirit Tree from being devoured. However, I was inflicted with this illness during the fight.”

“What is this thing?” Yuan asked with a deep frown on his face, wondering if it was caused by an Eternal.

Xiao Cangming shook his head and said, “It had an ethereal form, so whatever attacked wasn’t its true body. However, even without its true body, it was more powerful than anything I’ve ever faced before.”

“How does this illness affect you?” Yuan asked.

“It attacks my Dantian from time to time,” Xiao Cangming replied. “When it does, I have to focus everything I have just to defend myself. In those moments, I can barely even move a finger.”

“Let me take a look,” said Yuan.

Xiao Cangming nodded and silently extended his arm, allowing Yuan to analyze his body with spiritual energy.

Sure enough, Yuan sensed an ominous energy coiling around Xiao Cangming’s Dantian—like a sinister flame devouring it from within. However, it was currently suppressed by Xiao Cangming.

Yuan retrieved the Sacred Chalice of Purification and said, “Feed it some of your blood. This treasure can remove pretty much any curse out there.”

Xiao Cangming didn’t question Yuan and followed his instructions.

The chalice consumed Xiao Cangming’s blood, but it did not react, meaning that he wasn’t cursed.

“Now that we’ve confirmed it’s not a curse, let me try to absorb it.”

Hearing this, Xiao Cangming instantly retrieved his arm and said in a stern tone, “Absorb it? What if it inflicts you, as well? Sorry, but I’ll have to refuse.”

Yuan shook his head and said, “I just want to see if it’s poison. Don’t worry, I have achieved True Poison Immunity in case it is.”

“It’s still too risky.”

“Cangming, have you forgotten who I am?” Yuan said with a slight frown on his face. “Or are you looking down on me?”

“No, that’s not it…” Xiao Cangming hastily responded.

“Then what is it? You have managed to survive this long with it, yet you don’t think I can handle it? Are you trying to say that you’re now above me?”

Xiao Cangming opened his mouth only to close it shut without uttering a word. Then, after a moment of silence—

“Alright, you win.” Xiao Cangming sighed in a defeated manner as he extended his arm again.

Xiao Meilin kept a serious expression as she watched their exchange, though she was smiling inwardly. This somewhat obedient, vulnerable side of her father was something she only ever got to see when he faced Yuan. Thɪs chapter is updated by nοvelfire.net

Yuan tried to absorb the ominous energy within Xiao Cangming the next moment, but alas, his poison immunity did not react to it, either.

“I guess it’s truly neither poison nor a curse…” Yuan shook his head as he released Xiao Cangming’s arm.

“But don’t worry, I’m sure we’ll figure it out.”

“While it would be great if I could get rid of this illness, it’s not like I cannot live without being cured. Though this illness was life-threatening at first, I have long since gotten used to it. Now, it’s just a mild annoyance, akin to a seasonal cold that comes and goes.”

“Even if you say that, we should definitely try to get rid of it. It might not threaten you now, but things can always change, especially when we know nothing about it.”

Xiao Cangming nodded, then said, “Now that you know my situation, let’s talk about you, my lord. What brought you to the Primordial Realm this early? The Spirit Tree hasn’t fully grown yet, you know? It’s about 95 percent there.”

“Early? Did we schedule my next arrival date last time?” Yuan asked with raised eyebrows.

“Not exactly, but you said that you’d return once the Spirit Tree is fully grown and ready to be harvested.”

“I see… well, the reason I rushed to the Primordial Realm is because of her—”

Yuan proceeded to summon Xiao Hua from his Dantian. Of course, she was still slumbering.

“Junior sister…” Xiao Meilin mumbled when she saw Xiao Hua’s face. Xiao Cangming remained silent, but his eyes were slightly squinted, as if assessing her condition.

chapters.title: Chapter 2211 The Solution To Xiao Hua'S Issue
chapters.body:
Chapter 2211 The Solution To Xiao Hua'S Issue

“I understand the situation,” Xiao Cangming muttered after a moment of silence. “The balance between the two souls within her is broken, and they’re currently clashing with each other—trying to consume each other.”

“What?!” Yuan exclaimed in shock after hearing the last few words in Xiao Cangming’s sentence.

“Their souls are trying to consume each other?! This is the first time I’m hearing this! I was told they were only fighting for control of the body!”

If he’d known it was this serious, he would’ve taken this matter much more seriously and visited the Primordial Realm much sooner.

“Perhaps that might have been the case initially, but from what I am seeing right now, that is no longer the case.”

Xiao Cangming looked at Yuan and asked, “How much do you know about our Asura Clan’s powers?”

Yuan closed his eyes to ponder, “The Violent Nebula Physique, right? Come to think of it, I don’t actually know much about it, other than the fact that it makes you immune to physical attacks.”

Xiao Cangming nodded and explained, “That’s normal, since we didn’t tell you about it. Of course, it’s not like we were trying to hide it from you. Honestly, even we didn’t know much about our own physiques in the past. However, as we continued to study and experiment with it, we’ve grown to understand our physique much better now.”

“Anyway, our physiques’ true power lies in ‘reincarnation’.”

“What…?” Yuan’s eyes widened in shock.

“Reincarnation,” Xiao Cangming repeated calmly. “But it comes with strict limitations and conditions that we still don’t fully understand, even now. And so far, only one member of our family has ever experienced it.”

“You mean Xiao Hua?”

“Yes, and she has experienced reincarnation more than once.”

“How does it work?” Yuan asked.

“Well, we call it reincarnation, but it technically isn’t true reincarnation,” Xiao Cangming explained. “She doesn’t return to the cycle to await rebirth like others do. Instead, she’s more like a phoenix—revived after death, her soul never truly departing.”

“Then how does she return?”

“After her death, her soul will wander the world until it finds a suitable vessel—usually before they are born. Once the conditions are fulfilled, she would take over that body, hence two souls in one body, and depending on the two souls’ compatibility, they will either exist in harmony or…” “They fight for control,” Yuan sighed.

“But there’s something that I don’t understand,” he then said. “Xiao Hua’s soul should be incomparably stronger, so why does it seem like she’s struggling?”

“It’s not that she’s struggling. If she truly wanted to consume the other soul, it wouldn’t take any effort. However, doing so could cause irreparable harm to the body. Most importantly, since she is aware that it isn’t her true body, she’s trying her best to hold back.”

“So she’s actually trying to stop herself from consuming the other’s soul right now?”

Xiao Cangming nodded. “Yes. Unfortunately, her soul is so powerful that once the process begins, she must constantly suppress herself. If she falters even for a moment, the other soul will be devoured. As for the other soul… she’s subconsciously trying to fight back, even though it’s doing more harm than good.” Chapters fırst released on NoveI(F)ire.net

“How often does this happen? And is there a solution to it?” Yuan then asked.

Xiao Cangming closed his eyes and sighed, “This is the second time it’s happened, and she’s reincarnated over a dozen times. There are only two ways we can fix this. Either Xiao Hua’s soul consumes the other and takes permanent control of the body, or we remove Xiao Hua’s soul and place her into a different vessel.”

“…”

After a moment of silence, Yuan asked, “What did you do the last time?”

“Xiao Hua consumed the other’s soul since that was the only option we realistically had.”

“Another question. Her personality… Who have I been talking to this entire time? Is it Xiao Hua, or the other soul? Or do they swap from time to time?”

Xiao Hua’s personality had remained largely consistent throughout their time together—aside from the moment of her awakening. From that, Yuan deduced that only one soul had been in control until the awakening, likely the body’s original soul. Still, he needed confirmation.

“For her new vessel to be compatible, the soul must closely resemble Xiao Hua’s—particularly in personality,” Xiao Cangming explained. “To maintain harmony, Xiao Hua must also share her memories with the vessel’s original soul. So even though two souls inhabit one body, they essentially become a single being. In simpler terms, her persona is a fusion of both. Even if the souls were separated, they wouldn’t differ much from each other.”

Hearing this, Yuan finally understood why Xiao Hua could seem like an innocent little girl at times, yet mature and cruel at others.

“That said,” Xiao Cangming continued, “this only holds true when the souls remain in harmony. The fact that they’re now in conflict means one of them has changed—so drastically that they’re no longer compatible.”

“So, what should we do, my lord?”

Without hesitation, Yuan responded, “Naturally, we’re going to separate them.”

“Then we will need to find another vessel for Xiao Hua.”

“Does it have to contain another soul?” Yuan then asked.

“That condition is only for when she reincarnates. Since we’re simply relocating her soul, a soulless vessel will work just fine.”

Though it might sound complex or unnatural, the process was akin to that of a God Ascension cultivator whose physical body had been destroyed and required a new vessel.

“Do you happen to have an empty vessel lying around—or enough materials to create one?” Yuan asked.

Xiao Cangming shook his head.

“Unfortunately, we’re still in the Primordial Realm, so things like creating a new vessel are simply unfeasible, as we need more than just spiritual energy.”

“Are you telling me that I have to leave the Primordial Realm to obtain a new vessel for her? Even if I can do that, I don’t think we’ll have enough time for that.” Yuan rubbed his eyes in a stressful manner.

However, before Xiao Cangming could respond, Feng Yuxiang’s voice resounded, “Young Master, if I may… we might already have enough resources to craft Xiao Hua a new vessel if we consider the treasures we already own—mostly mine.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2212 The Solution To Xiao Hua'S Issue(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2212 The Solution To Xiao Hua'S Issue(2)

‘Is that true? We have enough resources to craft Xiao Hua a new vessel?’ Yuan asked for confirmation just in case he misheard.

‘Honestly, it depends on how complicated you want the vessel to be, but for a temporary vessel, we should have enough,’ said Feng Yuxiang.

Like treasures, a vessel can be stronger or weaker depending on the resources used to create it.

‘What about your Soul Weapon? The one that used to be occupied by Ji Ran,’ Yu Ning suddenly reminded Yuan about the Baneful Abyss. ‘We could use it as a vessel until we craft her a proper one.’

Hearing Yu Ning’s words, Yuan asked Xiao Cangming, “I have a Soul Weapon without any occupants right now. Can I put her soul inside until we finish her new vessel? I have used it to house the soul of a God Ascension cultivator before without any issue.”

Xiao Cangming pondered for a moment before responding, “We’ve never tried putting her soul in a Soul Weapon before, but we have tried to temporarily contain her soul in a treasure with a similar function before. The result? She subconsciously broke out of the treasure to find a new and more natural vessel, damaging her soul slightly in the process. Unlike normal people, Xiao Hua cannot maintain consciousness as a soul, and she will instinctively seek out a proper one, likely a side effect of her powers.”

“Anyway, to properly answer your question, it might work for some time, but it’s risky, and if you don’t find her a new vessel in time, she will try to break out of the Soul Weapon and hurt herself in the process.”

“…”

After a moment of silence, Yuan asked, “How long does it take to craft a new vessel? I might have the resources with me.”

“I will have to see the resources first,” he quickly replied.

“Feng Feng. Show him the materials we have.”

Feng Yuxiang emerged from his Dantian and immediately started looking through her spatial ring.

“A phoenix, huh? The last time you came here, you also had a phoenix with you,” Xiao Cangming remarked as he stared at Feng Yuxiang.

“This is Feng Yuxiang. There are a few more resting within my Dantian right now.”

Lan Yingying and Yingzi also came out the next moment, revealing themselves.

Xiao Cangming stared at Lan Yingying for a brief moment before turning to look at Yingzi.

“It’s not exactly the same, but it’s similar,” he mumbled a moment later.

“Similar? To whom?” Yuan asked out of curiosity.

Xiao Cangming responded as his gaze returned to Lan Yingying, “She reminds me of that white-haired Divine Serpent. As for this one… what were they called again? They have the ability to manipulate shadows.”

“Shadow Dweller.” Xiao Meilin answered for him.

“Did I bring all of the Nine Divine Supremes with me the last time I visited?” Yuan asked with a raised eyebrow.

Xiao Cangming nodded, “Yes, and you claimed that you lot were on the way to confront Gods. How did that turn out?”

“How much do you know about the Outer Gods?” Yuan then asked.

“I am aware of their existence, but that’s about it. I don’t know anything about their purpose or prowess. You left without explaining much, if anything, after all.”

“Is that so… Well, about that fight…”

Yuan explained everything that happened after their clash with the Outer Gods—how they split the Divine Heaven into nine different realms and Earth, as well as the existence of Cultivation Online. Nᴇw ɴovel chaptᴇrs are published on novelFɪre.net

Xiao Cangming listened with intrigue.

“Nine Heavens and Cultivation Online, huh? The world sure has changed a lot since the Primordial Era, but I guess that’s only to be expected.”

“How long do you think it’s been since you were thrown into this world?” Yuan smiled.

“…”

After a brief pause, he asked, “Say… why did you decide to remain inside the Primordial Realm? I’m sure I could’ve taken your entire family out of this place the last time I visited. The fact you’re still here must mean that you decided to stay here instead, right?”

Xiao Cangming nodded, “That’s right. After all, I had something to do here.”

“You mean the Spirit Tree?” Yuan asked. “Does it have to grow in the Primordial Realm? Why can’t you cultivate it elsewhere?”

“Because such a thing can only grow in this kind of environment,” Xiao Cangming calmly replied. “A place utterly devoid of spiritual energy. Quite the paradox, isn’t it? The Spirit Tree generates spiritual energy—yet it cannot survive within it. It also needs spiritual energy to grow, but it must be force-fed by sacrificing one’s cultivation, like a hatchling unable to feed itself.”

“…I should’ve probably asked this earlier, but what even is the Spirit Tree? And why did I ask you to grow it?” Yuan then questioned.

“That…” Xiao Cangming’s expression suddenly turned serious, making Yuan swallow nervously.

“I don’t know.” Xiao Cangming responded in a serious tone.

“What?” Yuan’s eyes widened with shock at his response.

“My lord, if I may be blunt for a moment… you’re absolutely terrible at explaining things. In fact, most of the time you don’t explain anything at all and just give the bare minimum of instructions.”

Xiao Cangming rubbed his eyes and continued, “Do you know what you told me when you handed me the Spirit Tree?”

“Word for word, you said—nurture this to the best of your ability. I’ll return when it matures.”

Xiao Meilin couldn’t help but chuckle at his words.

“That being said, I am also at fault for not demanding more details.”

“That’s…” Yuan was left speechless.

“Maybe I’ll remember once I see the Spirit Tree for myself later,” he said after a moment of silence.

Xiao Cangming let out a faint sigh.

“Of course, the Spirit Tree isn’t the only reason I chose to remain here. After the war… I needed time to gather my thoughts—and to atone for my failures, both as a leader and as a husband.”

“…”

Yuan wasn’t sure how to respond, so he remained silent.

Suddenly, Feng Yuxiang’s voice resounded.

“Young Master, it’s done. I have gathered all of the resources that can be used to create a vessel.”

“We’ll continue this later,” Yuan said to Xiao Cangming before turning toward the table, where more than a dozen treasures lay neatly arranged.

chapters.title: Chapter 2213 Li Zhenwu
chapters.body:
Chapter 2213 Li Zhenwu

“Well, what do you think?” Feng Yuxiang asked as she showcased her treasures with a prideful air around her.

Xiao Cangmang took a moment to look over the treasures before responding with a surprised expression, “I’ll be honest with you. I can’t identify even half of the treasures here, but judging from their intense aura, I’m sure they’re all priceless treasures—beyond anything I anticipated. If we have this quality of treasure, we can definitely make a top-grade vessel.”

“In that case, allow me to introduce them to you,” Feng Yuxiang said as she began identifying the treasures and explaining their uses.

“This right here is the Exalted Divine Crystal, a treasure used to store a massive amount of spiritual energy. It can be used to create the Dantian.”

“The item right next to it is the Nine-Flame Heart Pill, which can be used to create the heart. Not only will it give her vessel improved resistance to fire, but it will strengthen any fire-element techniques.”

“Next is the Primordial Essence Root, which will be used for her spiritual veins and give her superior cultivation speed.”

Feng Yuxiang pointed at a bottle of red liquid and continued, “This is phoenix blood—mine, to be precise. However, we will need more blood, preferably human blood from the same family.”

“I can give my blood,” Xiao Meilin raised her hand.

Feng Yuxiang nodded and continued explaining the treasures, “This is the Heavenly Marrow Essence. It’s usually used for crafting treasures, but it can also be used to forge bones.”

“These are the main ingredients for her vessel. The rest are mainly used to enhance their effects, but I will still explain them.”

A few minutes later, Feng Yuxiang finished. Not just Xiao Meilin, but even Xiao Cangming was left speechless by Feng Yuxiang’s resources.

“Are you sure we can use such precious ingredients? Honestly, even I would want to move my soul into a vessel made with these ingredients,” said Xiao Cangming.

Feng Yuxiang looked at Yuan and said, “Originally, I planned on using them to strengthen the Young Master.”

Yuan smiled and said, “Just your thoughts are enough. Moreover, at my current level, these treasures will have minimal effect, so it’s better to use them for Xiao Hua.”

“Feng Yuxiang, right? I—the Asura Clan will never forget this debt,” Xiao Cangming said with his head slightly lowered.

Feng Yuxiang rubbed her nose and responded in a stiff tone, “Honestly, I am only doing this for the Young Master, so you don’t owe me anything.”

“It doesn’t change the fact that you’re the reason Xiao Hua will have a new vessel.”

“So, is this enough to craft her vessel? If so, how long do you think it’ll take?” Yuan asked a moment later.

Xiao Cangming nodded and replied, “This is more than enough. In fact, we can make a vessel with only half of it. The rest is just a bonus. As for how long it will take… I haven’t attempted to craft a vessel since the Primordial Era, so it might take me a couple of weeks just to be safe. I don’t want to mess up these precious ingredients, after all.”

“Alright, I’ll leave it in your hands then.” Thıs text ıs hosted at novelfire(.)net

Yuan placed Xiao Hua back inside his Dantian shortly after.

“I’ll need a few days to prepare myself. Make yourself at home until then.”

Xiao Cangming left to prepare a moment later.

Meanwhile, Xiao Meiling brought Yuan to Mu Xuelian, who was waiting in the guest room.

However, when they arrived, Mu Xuelian was not alone, and there was another beautiful woman in the room with her.

The two were quietly sharing tea, but the moment Yuan saw the woman’s face, his body went rigid, his eyes widening in utter shock and disbelief.

“Fei Zhen’er…?” Yuan muttered.

The beautiful woman greatly resembled Xiao Cangming’s dead wife—the one Tian Xian had personally killed in the past. The resemblance was so uncanny that he wouldn’t be surprised if this woman were actually Fei Zhen’er.

Seeing Yuan, the woman stood up and gracefully bowed to him, “You must be Master Tian. I have heard a lot about you from my husband. I am Li Zhenwu, and it’s an honor to finally meet you.”

Yuan turned to look at Xiao Meilin, who was standing right behind him and chuckling at his reaction.

“I knew you’d be surprised when you see her. The resemblance is uncanny, right?” she remarked.

Li Zhenwu smiled and said, “I was told that I greatly resemble my husband’s late wife, but I never really took it to heart. However, seeing your reaction… I cannot help but wonder just how much I resemble her now.”

Yuan swallowed nervously and replied, “You’re a splitting image of her. If you claimed to be Fei Zhen’er, I wouldn’t have doubted you in the slightest.”

“If you don’t mind me asking, are you busy right now?” Li Zhenwu suddenly asked.

“Me? No, I am not busy.”

Li Zhenwu clasped her hands together at his words and spoke with an excited smile, “In that case, can you tell me some stories about my husband? This might sound weird, but he only ever talks about you, so it’s not an exaggeration to say that I know you better than my own husband.”

“I don’t mind… but wouldn’t it be better if you just asked him?”

Li Zhenwu shook her head and sighed, “I have tried, but the topic always ends up shifting to you.”

“Alright, I’ll tell you everything I know about Cangming.” Yuan nodded.

“Thank you! Let me grab some more tea first!”

Once Li Zhenwu left the room, Xiao Meilin looked at Yuan and said with a smile, “Try not to kill her this time, okay?”

Yuan rubbed his eyes and sighed.

“I know you’re joking, but…”

Xiao Meilin chuckled for a bit before suddenly turning serious.

“Master… she doesn’t know how my late mother died—how you killed her, so try not to mention that.”

“…Alright.” Yuan nodded with a serious face.

chapters.title: Chapter 2214 Li Zhenwu(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2214 Li Zhenwu(2)

Once Li Zhenwu returned to the room with more tea leaves, Yuan began telling her stories of his adventures with Xiao Cangming.

Mu Xuelian stayed in the room to listen since she had nothing else better to do. Xiao Meilin also decided to stay for the nostalgia.

Meanwhile, Xiao Cangming began preparing to forge Xiao Hua’s new vessel, running countless simulations within his mind. Just as Tian Qiyuan could refine his crafting skills through mental training alone, true masters possessed the ability to temper their skills entirely within the realm of thought.

Naturally, this was no simple feat, and it placed immense strain on the mind and demanded extraordinary Soul and Mental Strength.

Three days passed in the blink of an eye, and Xiao Cangming emerged from his room with a confident look on his face. After doing hundreds of simulations within his head, he was now prepared to create Xiao Hua’s vessel for real.

“Where’s the lord?” Xiao Cangming asked Xiao Chen, who had been waiting outside for him to finish.

“He’s currently with mother and senior sister,” Xiao Chen calmly responded.

“Bring me to him.”

Xiao Chen nodded and, without a word, led Xiao Cangming to the guest room.

Even before they reached the room, Xiao Cangming could hear Li Zhenwu’s gentle laugh echoing in the halls.

When they arrived, Xiao Cangming walked through the already-opened door and said, “I should’ve introduced you to my wife first before I went into seclusion. I apologize.”

Yuan looked at him and casually dismissively waved his hand, “It’s fine. I was just telling her about our adventures in the past.”

“After all, you never talk about yourself,” Li Zhenwu said as she sipped on her tea.

“I don’t?” Xiao Cangming replied with raised eyebrows.

“You would try, but you always end up talking more about Master Tian here.”

Xiao Cangming opened his mouth but closed it when he realized she was right. Get full chapters from novel_fіre.net

“Anyway, I am ready to start crafting Xiao Hua’s new vessel. Do you want to watch, my lord?” Yuan nodded.

“You’re making a new vessel for Xiao Hua? This is the first time I am hearing this,” Li Zhenwu spoke with a surprised face.

“I’ll explain on the way,” said Xiao Meilin.

Sometime later, they all gathered in another room, one that was fortified with many powerful formations. The room also had a strong medicinal scent, and a cauldron sat in the middle of the room.

“The alchemists mainly use this place for their alchemy,” Xiao Cangming said. “Also, we will need an alchemist for this, so…”

Yuan followed Xiao Cangming’s gaze to Li Zhenwu and asked, “You’re an alchemist?”

She nodded, “Yes. In fact, I was once the leader of the Inferno Alchemists. I handed that spot to the current leader after marrying into the Asura Clan.” “Is that so…” Yuan wondered if Li Zhenwu’s connection to the Inferno Alchemists was the true reason behind their collaboration with the Asura Clan.

Xiao Cangming retrieved the ingredients for Xiao Hua’s vessel and placed them beside the cauldron.

“Here are the ingredients we will be using,” Xiao Cangming said to her.

Li Zhenwu approached the ingredients with a look of shock on her face.

“I don’t even recognize them,” she responded in a stiff tone.

“I’ll explain them to you now.” Xiao Cangming said before repeating what Feng Yuxiang told him.

A few minutes later, Li Zhenwu sighed. “You know, I would’ve appreciated a bit of warning about this—at least enough time to prepare. Although I have crafted vessels before, they’re all low-quality, made from trash ingredients.”

“This is like suddenly handing a craftsman who is only used to low-quality material the best materials in the world and telling them to create a masterpiece.”

“Sorry, but I forgot. It’s also been a while since I crafted a vessel, so I immediately went into seclusion. If you need some time to practice, we can wait for you. I will need a few days to prepare the ingredients, anyway.”

“Practice with what ingredients? Even if you give me a decade to practice with trash ingredients, it won’t help me with these priceless ingredients. Mental training won’t help either since I have never handled ingredients even close to the ones before me right now.”

“You’re the best alchemist in the Primordial Realm, so I’m sure you’ll be fine.”

“In here, perhaps. But I doubt I will be able to compare to even ordinary alchemists in the outside world.”

“Don’t sell yourself short now. Moreover, you’ll only be working as my assistant this time, as I will be doing most of the work. I just need you to assist me because my cultivation has declined back to the first level of God Ascension,” said Xiao Cangming.

“If I am only working as your assistant… guess that’s much more manageable,” she nodded.

During their whole conversation, Li Zhenwu’s gaze never left the ingredients, almost as if she were mesmerized by them.

Suddenly, she turned to Yuan and asked, “Are ingredients like these abundant in the world outside of the Primordial Realm?”

“No,” Yuan calmly shook his head. “Even in the Nine Heavens, such ingredients are considered a rarity—priceless, even.”

“And you’re willing to use them to craft a vessel for Xiao Hua? You’re very generous,” she said with a warm smile.

Yuan shook his head.

“There is no ingredient or material in this world that is more valuable than Xiao Hua.”

“Anyway, even though I’ll only be working as an assistant, I need to study these ingredients before we begin. Give me a few days,” Li Zhenwu said.

“Alright.”

“Master, since we still have some time, why don’t you spar with me? I’ve been itching to exchange swords with you for a while now.”

Yuan responded with a stiff smile, “Didn’t we spar with each other right before coming here?”

“That doesn’t count!” she quickly said.

“Why don’t you take him to the Spirit Tree instead?” Xiao Cangming suddenly said. “Oh? That’s a good idea. Let’s see the Spirit Tree first. If we have time afterward, I’ll spar with you,” said Yuan.

“Fine. I’ll take you to the Spirit Tree.” Xiao Meilin nodded.

chapters.title: Chapter 2215 Saaruk
chapters.body:
Chapter 2215 Saaruk

Sometime later, Xiao Meilin brought Yuan to the Spirit Tree, which was located only a few miles away from the Asura Clan’s residence.

Shortly after they left the residence, Mu Xuelian, who followed them, spoke, “Yuan, Lord Shiva has something to tell you.”

“Hm? What is it?” Yuan turned to look at her.

“She said that man doesn’t have long to live.”

“What?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“That first-level God Ascension cultivator we were just with.”

“Cangming? What is her reason for saying such a thing?” Yuan immediately stopped moving and glared at her with a serious frown.

Mu Xuelian closed her eyes and, after a moment of silence, responded in a different tone.

“That man has been afflicted with a particular technique by a certain Eternal,” said Shiva, who just took control of Mu Xuelian’s body.

“You mean his illness? So an Eternal was involved, after all…” Yuan sighed.

“What are you talking about?” Xiao Meilin asked.

Yuan explained calmly, “The beings we know as Outer Gods refer to themselves as Eternals—and your father’s illness was caused by one of them.”

“And how does she know that?” Xiao Meilin glared at Mu Xuelian with a suspicious face.

“Long story short, there’s an Eternal living within her.”

Xiao Meilin’s eyes narrowed at the revelation. “I knew there was something off about her the moment I saw her,” she muttered. “Now I understand why.”

“Master, why are you traveling with someone like that? Aren’t you enemies with these Outer Gods?” He nodded, “Yes, but there are many of them out there, and I am not at war with all of them.”

“Is that so? Well, this is news to me,” said Xiao Meilin.

“Anyway, what was that about father? He’s going to die soon? Can you elaborate on that? He’s managed to survive tens of millions of years with this illness, after all.”

“That’s because the technique used on him wasn’t meant to kill him quickly,” Shiva said. “It was designed to inflict pain for as long as possible. Tens of millions of years may seem long to mortals—but to us Eternals, it’s nothing. In fact, I have taken naps far longer than that.”

“Since you know what it is, do you know how to cure him?” Yuan then asked.

Shiva calmly nodded, “Of course. It’s actually very simple.”

“I’m listening.”

“You just need to absorb it.”

“What?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“Have you forgotten the essence of our powers already—the power of your physique?”

“You mean I can just refine the illness into essence and absorb it, turning it into my power?” Yuan asked with a somewhat dumbfounded face.

Shiva silently nodded.

“In that case, let’s return now,” Yuan said to Xiao Meilin.

“What about the Spirit Tree? We’re about to arrive.”

“That can wait. Your father will be crafting Xiao Hua’s vessel soon, and I don’t want anything to go wrong when that happens.”

Yuan didn’t want to risk Xiao Cangming’s illness affecting him during the creation of Xiao Hua’s vessel and decided to take care of it as soon as possible. Moreover, Xiao Cangming’s life was on the line.

“If you say so.”

Xiao Meilin didn’t argue and immediately turned around.

Sometime later, they returned to the room where Xiao Cangming and Li Zhenwu were.

“You’re back already? Did you forget something?” Xiao Cangming asked.

“No. I came back because I learned about the cause of your illness and the method to cure it,” said Yuan. ᴛhis chapter is ᴜpdated by Nove1Fire.net

“What?” Xiao Cangming stared at him with a bewildered face.

“How did you acquire this knowledge in such a short time?”

After all, they’d only been gone for a few minutes.

Yuan looked at Mu Xuelian and explained, “Actually, there is an Outer God dwelling within my friend here, and she was the one who told me about it.”

“That’s…”

Xiao Cangming was left speechless. As far as he was aware, all Outer Gods were enemies.

Seeing his reaction, Yuan quickly explained, “Just like humans, not every Outer God is friendly with each other, and they even fight amongst themselves. In other words, not all of them are our enemies.”

“I see… So, what about my illness?”

Mu Xuelian then spoke, “According to Lord Shiva, it’s a technique used by the one known as ‘Saaruk’, meant to torment you with unbearable pain.”

“Then what about the treatment?”

“I just need to absorb it,” Yuan replied.

“Didn’t you try that already?”

“That’s different, since I treated it like a poison. What I am going to do this time is entirely different.”

“It sounds risky. How risky is it?” Xiao Cangming asked with a slight frown.

“That’s… it shouldn’t be too risky, right?” Yuan turned to look at Mu Xuelian.

“Lord Shiva said it’s not risky, but it will be very painful for you, the absorber, since you’re essentially consuming the power of an Eternal.”

“You heard her. It’s not risky.” Yuan said with a calm smile.

“When do you plan on absorbing it?” Xiao Cangming then asked.

“Right now.”

“Right now? But we’re about to start crafting Xiao Hua’s vessel.”

“That’s exactly why we must do it now. I don’t want anything to go wrong, and since there’s a chance the illness might strike during the process, I want to deal with it before you start.”

Xiao Cangming couldn’t refute his reasoning. Since it was true that the illness could surface at unpredictable intervals, Yuan’s concern was entirely justified.

“Alright, let’s get this over with.” Xiao Cangming nodded.

Hearing this, Yuan gestured for Xiao Cangming to take a seat before him.

Once Xiao Cangming was seated, Yuan took his place behind him and rested both hands against his back.

Yuan closed his eyes and started trying to sense the illness in Xiao Cangming’s body.

Li Zhenwu, who had been focused on the ingredients, paused and watched them in silence, a faint trace of worry flickering in her eyes.

A few minutes later, once Yuan found the source of the illness, he said, “I am going to start now.”

Then, he started to refine its very essence.

Shortly after Yuan started to absorb it, a loud and unfamiliar voice thundered inside his head.

“Which bastard dares to covet this mighty Saaruk’s power?!”

chapters.title: Chapter 2216 Saaruk'S Threat
chapters.body:
Chapter 2216 Saaruk'S Threat

When Yuan heard this voice, he immediately delved into his mind, as this voice seemed more than just his imagination. Within his consciousness, he sensed that a fragment of the Saaruk’s power had transformed into a kind of link, forging a connection between them. It functioned much like a communication jade slip, allowing their minds to intertwine and exchange words directly.

“Hm? Wait a second… this presence… you’re not an Eternal!” Saaruk’s voice returned before Yuan could respond to it.

“A mortal! How dare a mere mortal attempt to covet the power of the mighty Saaruk!” the voice roared, its fury intensifying as it realized its own strength was being refined by a human.

“And what are you going to do about it?” Yuan responded in a provocative tone.

“This mighty Saaruk has memorized your scent! The moment you leave the safe zone, this mighty Saaruk will devour you!”

“Hahaha!” Yuan burst into laughter. “You, who was driven back by a mere mortal while trying to steal the Spirit Tree, think you can devour me? You Eternals truly have wild imaginations!”

For a moment, the voice fell silent. When it returned, it dripped with malice. “You are but an insignificant insect—one who cannot even begin to comprehend the magnitude of our power. Were it not for the Absolute Power’s interference, your pitiful existence would have long since been erased.”

“When this mighty Saaruk attempted to covet the Spirit Tree, this mighty Saaruk had only been using a single percent of their true power while severely underestimating their opponent. However, it will not happen a second time.”

“If you’re truly so powerful and mighty, then why do you need the Spirit Tree—something planted by the very mortals you so disdain?” Yuan asked calmly.

“This mighty Saaruk has no intention to entertain a mortal’s curiosity. Leave the safe zone if you dare… This mighty Saaruk will be waiting for you…”

The voice gradually faded, and the link between them was abruptly severed. Though their exchange had lasted only a few brief sentences, several days had passed in the real world, and Yuan had refined half of Saaruk’s power from Xiao Cangming.

Once he returned to his senses, Yuan ramped up the speed he absorbed Saaruk’s power, and after a few more hours, he completely devoured it.

Yuan lifted his sweat-soaked palms from Xiao Cangming’s back and drew a deep breath. “It’s done,” he said softly.

Xiao Cangming stared at his hands in awe. “The invisible weight that’s always pressed down on me is gone.”

Ever since Saaruk’s power took hold, his body had always felt sluggish and burdened, but now, that sensation had completely disappeared.

He turned around to face Yuan, who was utterly drenched in sweat, and asked, “Are you okay, my lord?”

Yuan nodded, “Despite my appearance, I am actually overflowing with energy.”

He turned to look at Mu Xuelian and said, “I spoke with Saaruk.”

Shiva instantly took over Mu Xuelian’s body and responded, “What did you talk about?”

“Nothing much. They only threatened to devour me the moment I leave the Nine Heavens.”

Shiva laughed, “I can’t say I’m surprised. You should prepare yourself.”

Yuan raised an eyebrow and asked, “What do you mean?”

“Even amongst the Eternals, Saaruk is considered very powerful—around the top 20.”

“You guys have a ranking? How many Eternals are out there?”

“Before I was sealed in the Nine Heavens, there were a total of 76 Eternals. Although more may have been born since then, it won’t be more than a handful.”

“76 Eternals, huh? Then where do you rank?” Yuan asked.

“Somewhere in the middle, I guess.”

Yuan was surprised to hear this.

Shiva frowned at Yuan’s expression. “How dare you look at me like that? Are you mocking me for my ranking? Yes, there’s a ranking among us, but the difference in prowess isn’t as vast as you think. Saaruk may rank above me, but that’s only because the order is based on the quantity of our Essence. In terms of strength, the gap isn’t that great. If we fought seriously, there’s no telling who will win.”

“You misunderstood. I wasn’t looking down on you.” Yuan calmly shook his head but refused to elaborate further.

“Anyway, I have a question,” Yuan said. “The Primordial Realm lies at the edge of the Nine Heavens, so it should still be within the safe zone. Yet Saaruk still managed to attack Cangming. I thought the Eternals weren’t allowed to intervene because of this Absolute Power?”

“The Absolute Power’s influence weakens the farther you are from its center,” Shiva explained. “So while we can’t touch the Nine Heavens, we’re still able to intervene through other methods. However, even then, it’s not easy and requires a lot of energy.”

“Is that why Saaruk hasn’t returned since he failed to covet the Spirit Tree?” Yuan mumbled to himself.

After a moment of silence, Yuan asked Shiva, “What are the chances of me defeating Saaruk right now?”

Shiva sneered in disdain, “With your current level of Eternal Essence, you won’t be able to touch him, let alone defeat him.”

“Is that so?” Yuan didn’t inquire further.

Sometime later, Xiao Cangming said, “Now that my illness is gone, I feel far more confident about our chances of success.”

He looked at Li Zhenwu and asked, “How are your preparations going?” “I will be ready to begin tomorrow,” she confidently replied.

While they continued to prepare, Yuan went to take a bath.

Time passed quickly, and after a full day of preparation, it was time to create Xiao Hua’s new vessel.

“My lord, before we begin, there’s something I must warn you about,” Xiao Cangming said to Yuan.

“What is it?”

“Once we transfer Xiao Hua’s soul into her new vessel, it may take some time for her to adjust. During that period, her memories could regress, and it will take some time for them to return.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2217 Xiao Hua'S New Vessel
chapters.body:
Chapter 2217 Xiao Hua'S New Vessel

“Her memories will regress? How far will it regress?” Yuan asked after hearing this.

Xiao Cangming shook his head and said, “It differs every time, so we won’t know until it happens.”

“I see. Then we can only deal with it when the time comes,” Yuan said.

Moments later, Xiao Cangming and Li Zhenwu approached the cauldron and sat before it.

“Ready?” Xiao Cangming asked as he faced Li Zhenwu.

She took a deep breath before responding in a serious tone, “I am ready.”

Without another word, Li Zhenwu summoned her alchemy flames. Crimson fire blossomed around her, surging forward to envelop the cauldron until it was completely engulfed.

While Li Zhenwu tended to the cauldron, Xiao Cangming focused on preparing the ingredients. Once the cauldron reached the proper heat, he began adding them one by one. Yet not every material required the cauldron, as some demanded entirely different techniques and handling.

There were many methods to craft a vessel, but Xiao Cangming chose the most ancient one, as it was the only one that existed in the Primordial Era.

As the ingredients within the cauldron melded together, Xiao Cangming employed a special technique to shape the remaining materials. The result, however, resembled not a human form, but rather a crude doll.

“The ingredients are ready,” Li Zhenwu said a few hours later.

Without a word, Xiao Cangming opened up the cauldron and tossed the doll inside.

Once the doll entered the cauldron, Xiao Cangming began infusing the rest of the ingredients together.

Time flowed swiftly, and before they knew it, three days had passed since they began crafting Xiao Hua’s vessel.

“It’s ready,” Xiao Cangming said as he lifted the lid of the cauldron and carefully drew out the vessel. Yet it was far from complete, as the vessel bore no rebalance to a human form, but rather an enlarged pill of condensed flesh.

“My lord, can you take out Xiao Hua?” Hearing this, Yuan immediately retrieved Xiao Hua from his Dantian.

“Meilin.” Xiao Cangming suddenly called for her.

Without a word, she walked over to Xiao Hua before placing her hand on her head.

“I’m going to split her soul now. It will take a moment, and she will start squirming around. Restrain her movement as much as possible,” Xiao Meilin said to Yuan.

“Alright,” he nodded.

“…”

After a moment of silence, Xiao Meilin shouted, “Now!”

The next instant, Xiao Hua jolted awake—and a piercing scream tore from her lips, as though she were being consumed by unbearable pain.

Yuan quickly restricted her movements.

“AHHHH!”

It was Yuan’s first time seeing Xiao Hua in such agony, and the sound of her screams made his chest tighten with a deep, aching pain.

Xiao Hua’s screaming did not stop for several minutes, but eventually, Xiao Meilin managed to separate her soul from the other.

After retrieving Xiao Hua’s soul, Xiao Meilin immediately gave it to Xiao Cangming, who forcefully shoved it into the pill of flesh.

Almost instantly, the fist-sized pill began to expand, swelling until it grew to the size of a watermelon within moments.

Moments later, limbs began to sprout from the pill, its shape twisting and expanding until it rapidly took on the form of a human body.

The form first took shape as that of a newborn, then swiftly matured into a young girl. As the growth ceased, her features began to refine, each detail becoming clearer, her face and form taking on a lifelike complexity.

In the span of a single minute, the pill had transformed into a beautiful young lady, her features resembling Xiao Hua’s, yet distinct enough to seem like a different person.

Lastly, her hair began to flow down her back—a lustrous pitch black, streaked with faint shades of violet that shimmered in the light.

“How did it go?” Yuan asked in a slightly nervous tone.

Xiao Cangming responded as he placed a piece of cloth over Xiao Hua’s naked body, “It went well. Her soul has accepted its new vessel. Now, we just need to wait for her to wake up.”

“What about her?” Yuan looked at the young girl resting in his arms.

Xiao Meilin pressed her hand against the young girl’s forehead to check her soul.

“Her soul isn’t damaged, but it’s severely weakened. If you have any soul-strengthening medicine, feed it to her once she wakes up.”

“Alright.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2218 Evil God’S True Bloodline
chapters.body:
Chapter 2218 Evil God’S True Bloodline

“So Xiao Hua becoming an exile wasn’t part of our plans…?” Yuan mumbled after learning of this surprising information.

“Why do you ask? Is there something wrong with that girl?” Xiao Cangming asked.

Yuan looked at the slumbering girl and sighed, “She has the Evil God’s True Bloodline.”

“What?! That’s impossible!” Xiao Cangming immediately exclaimed in a voice of pure disbelief. “Unless there is another Evil God out there that we don’t know about, that would imply she belongs to your family!”

Even Xiao Meilin couldn’t help but stare at him with eyes as wide as saucers.

Yuan sighed. “I’m just as confused as you are. At first, I thought the bloodline belonged to Xiao Hua, but now it’s clear that the original owner of this body is the one who possesses the bloodline.”

“It makes no sense,” Xiao Cangming said. “There’s no doubt this girl was born in the Primordial Realm. Unless you conceived a child during your last visit, there’s no way she could carry your bloodline. Unlike physiques, bloodlines must be inherited.”

Yuan shook his head. “Not always. Some bloodlines aren’t tied to one’s flesh, but to the soul itself. I’m living proof of that.” ɴᴇᴡ ɴᴏᴠᴇʟ ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀs ᴀʀᴇ ᴘᴜʙʟɪsʜᴇᴅ ᴏɴ N()velFire.net

“You are…?” Xiao Cangming mumbled.

Despite never bearing a child as the Immortal Monarch, Yuan somehow managed to be born with the Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline, which meant that some bloodlines—if unique and powerful enough—could be bound to one’s soul and be passed down even after their death through reincarnation.

Yuan spoke softly, “Before any being comes into existence, their soul must first take shape. In other words, the soul is born before all else, meaning even an unborn child would possess one.”

“Are you saying that this child is the reincarnation of your…?” Xiao Cangming swallowed nervously at Yuan’s suspicion.

Yuan smiled and said, “While this is just pure speculation, I cannot think of another reason why she possesses such a bloodline. Maybe there really is another ‘Evil God’ out there, and she is their child.”

Xiao Cangming shook his head. “Do you know what it takes for a bloodline to be recognized by the heavens—the universe—and come into existence? Power and fate. Only those with immense strength and a destiny that shakes the world have their bloodline recognized. The greater one’s fate, the stronger their bloodline. If there was truly another Evil God who managed to create their own bloodline, there’s no way we wouldn’t have known.”

The existence of bloodlines didn’t simply appear out of nowhere. When a being of immense power and destiny left a lasting impact on the world, that influence would be recognized by the heavens themselves, thus giving birth to a unique bloodline. Such bloodlines could then be passed down through generations, granting their descendants extraordinary abilities beyond those of ordinary lineages.

However, if one’s existence surpassed mortal comprehension—like that of the Immortal Monarch—their bloodline would transcend the limits of mere flesh and blood and instead bind itself to the soul.

“Such a thing is possible? This is news to me.” Xiao Cangming muttered with a dumbfounded look on his face.

“If this little girl truly turns out to be the reincarnation of your unborn daughter… what will you do then?” Xiao Cangming asked quietly.

Yuan fell silent for a long moment before replying, “How the hell am I supposed to know? Across all my reincarnations, I’ve only ever had one child—as Tian Xian—and she died before she was even born.”

He paused, his voice softening. “Besides, even if she truly is the reincarnation of my unborn daughter, she has new parents now. She’s no longer my daughter… No, in the first place, she can’t even be considered my daughter. After all, my daughter was never born.”

Unlike Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang, who retained memories of their past lives, Yuan found it difficult to have any special attachments toward her, much less see her as his daughter. Since his own child had never been born, there had been no chance for a bond to form between them.

“With that said, while she may not be my daughter, she is still family to me, something akin to a little sister.”

After a moment of silence, Yuan spoke, “Alright, let’s stop lingering around and put these two to rest properly.”

While Xiao Meilin carried Xiao Hua, Yuan continued to carry the nameless young girl, bringing them to a room where they could rest on a bed.

Sometime later, Xiao Cangming appeared before him and asked, “I know you returned early, but did you get to see the Spirit Tree?”

“No,” Yuan calmly said.

“Why don’t we go now? These two will probably remain asleep for a few days, so we have some time,” Xiao Cangming suggested.

“Sure,” he nodded.

Thus, they set out for the Spirit Tree again with Xiao Cangming tagging along this time.

Meanwhile, Li Zhenwu stayed behind to watch over the slumbering girls.

Sometime later, they arrived at their destination.

“This is the Spirit Tree…?”

Yuan’s eyes widened in surprise at the sight of the Spirit Tree. It was nothing like what he’d imagined, and its appearance defied expectation to an almost absurd degree.

“What were you expecting? A grand and massive tree?” Xiao Meilin chuckled. “Honestly? Yes.” He admitted.

Before Yuan stood the Spirit Tree—a laughably small ‘tree’ that barely reached his knees in height. Though its size was laughably small and disappointing, the tree itself was breathtakingly beautiful, almost ethereal. Its trunk gleamed with a soft emerald light, and its leaves shimmered with a gentle, otherworldly radiance.

Beyond its appearance, the tree exuded an overwhelming surge of spiritual energy, so vast and pure that Yuan had never experienced anything like it before.

“How can something so small generate so much spiritual energy?” Mu Xuelian remarked with a bewildered look on her face. “I am feeling bloated with spiritual energy just from being near it.” “If you want to know its origins, you’ll have to ask my lord,” said Xiao Cangming.

However, Yuan shook his head and sighed, “Unfortunately, I don’t remember, and it’s not triggering any memories right now.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2219 Spirit Tree
chapters.body:
Chapter 2219 Spirit Tree

“By the way, you said the Spirit Tree hasn’t fully matured yet?” Yuan suddenly recalled Xiao Cangming mentioning this earlier.

He nodded and confirmed, “The Spirit Tree is still growing…”

After a brief pause, he continued, “…No, that’s not quite right. To be honest, the Spirit Tree hasn’t grown in tens of millions of years. No matter how much cultivation I pour into it, it remains unchanged. In the beginning, it responded, showing clear signs of growth each time I fed it my energy, but that ceased not long after it reached this form.”

He sighed softly. “I suspect there’s a unique cultivation method required to nourish it. Unfortunately, I lack that knowledge… so this may be as far as I can go.”

“Is that so?”

Yuan stared at the small eternal tree before him with a pondering gaze, seemingly in deep thought.

Xiao Cangming and the others silently waited for his next action.

After a moment of stillness, Yuan raised his hand, palm facing downward above the Spirit Tree. Without warning, he drew a sharp cut across his palm, letting his blood drip onto the tree below.

The others watched in quiet intrigue, while Yuan himself felt strangely numb, as his actions were guided not by thought, but by pure instinct.

The moment Yuan’s blood touched the Spirit Tree, it was instantly absorbed, almost like a sponge with water. In the next breath, the entire tree began to change, its glowing green hue bleeding into a brilliant crimson.

“Oh?” Yuan muttered in a surprised voice after seeing the aftermath.

However, the Spirit Tree wasn’t finished with its transformation.

Mere moments later, the ground started to shake, growing more violent with every passing second.

“This is… an earthquake?” Xiao Meilin looked at the ground with a dumbfounded look.

Natural earthquakes in the Primordial Realm were impossible, and the only time the ground would shake was when cultivators collided. However, there was obviously nobody fighting right now.

Then, right before their very eyes, the Spirit Tree started to expand in size. Seeing this, Yuan and the others hastily backed away from it.

In the blink of an eye, the Spirit Tree, which was only as tall as one’s knees, enlarged until it rivaled the massive tree in Feng Yuxiang’s Immortal Cave.

Moreover, it wasn’t just its size that changed, as the amount of spiritual energy it produced drastically increased, too. In fact, the Spirit Tree released so much spiritual energy that it became visible to the naked eye, spreading through the world as a shimmering mist—an exceedingly rare phenomenon that only appears in such extreme conditions. chapters.title: Chapter 2220 Spirit Tree(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2220 Spirit Tree(2)

Even before Yuan arrived at the Asura Clan, scouts had been stationed near the Burning Tree, watching closely for any opportunity to strike.

Then, when Yuan awakened the Spirit Tree, it cracked open the Asura Clan’s world, opening its sanctuary to the outside world.

“The Asura Clan’s world is open, but I don’t see anyone going in or coming out.”

“Did something happen to the Asura Clan?”

“This might be our chance! We need to notify the others immediately!”

Without hesitation, they activated the token, assembling everyone involved in the plan.

In mere minutes, thousands of experts gathered at the Burning Tree, with many more to come.

“What happened here?”

“Are we attacking the Asura Clan today?”

The scouts explained the situation to the experts, “We don’t know what exactly, but something definitely happened inside the Asura Clan, as their world has been open for several minutes now without anyone going in or out. This has never happened before.”

“Could this be a trap?”

“No, that doesn’t seem to be the case. The portal is different than normal. It’s cracked, almost as if it was forcefully opened.”

“Not to mention the spiritual energy leaking from the portal… I can only imagine how much spiritual energy is on the other side.”

The people there swallowed nervously, their eyes shimmering with greed and desperation, their desire to take control of the Spirit Tree surging.

At that moment, ripples emanated from the portal, and they watched as several figures appeared one after another.

These figures were Xiao Cangming, Li Zhenwu, Xiao Meilin, Xiao Chen, and several of the Asura Clan’s servants.

“The Asura Clan is here!” The experts gathered at the Burning Tree were immediately alerted by their appearance. ɴᴇᴡ ɴᴏᴠᴇʟ ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀs ᴀʀᴇ ᴘᴜʙʟɪsʜᴇᴅ ᴏɴ novel⟡fire.net

“Quite the crowd we have here today,” Xiao Cangming remarked as he swept the crowd with a calm yet overbearing gaze.

“However, I don’t recall calling any of you here,” he continued. “Or is there a banquet nearby that I am not aware of, Zhao Juechen?”

Xiao Cangming’s gaze stopped on one of the figures standing at the forefront—a man he recognized as the current patriarch of the Zhao Family, one of the three great powers alongside the Inferno Alchemists and the Chaotic Sect.

Zhao Juechen took a deep breath before speaking out loud, “Xiao Cangming! We’ve come to deliver an ultimatum! Your greed and monopoly over the Spirit Tree have gone on long enough! For millions of years, countless cultivators have been starved of spiritual energy, while your Asura Clan hoards it without limit!”

His eyes burned with indignation. “This ends today! Have you learned nothing from the Celestial Emperor you once fought against? The War of Immortals and Gods began because he monopolized the world’s resources, forcing all under his rule! What you’re doing with the Spirit Tree is no different!”

“…”

Hearing Zhao Juechen’s words, Xiao Cangming didn’t even bat an eye and calmly responded, “Then allow me to ask you two questions. If you nurture a chicken in your backyard, who owns the eggs it bears? And are you responsible for feeding everyone in the world with those eggs?”

“What kind of nonsense is that?” Zhao Juechen frowned.

“Just answer my questions.”

“The answer to such a question is obvious! I am not going to entertain you!”

Xiao Cangming sighed and said, “I asked you those questions because the Spirit Tree was grown by me, so what’s with monopolizing it? And who are you to demand the spiritual energy it produces?”

“What?!” Zhao Juechen and the others expressed shock after hearing such a thing, as it was their first time learning about the Spirit Tree’s origin.

“D-Don’t lie to us! There’s no way you could’ve grown the Spirit Tree!” Zhao Juechen shouted, swinging his arm through the air in denial.

“I am lying? Then answer me this. Why is the Spirit Tree in my world, where everything within is my creation? If you’re going to claim that I moved the Spirit Tree inside, then where was the Spirit Tree beforehand?”

“That’s—!” Zhao Juechen opened his mouth but was speechless.

There were many experts present who had existed in the Primordial Realm since the very beginning, but not a single one of them had ever encountered or heard of the Spirit Tree before the Asura Clan created their own territory.

Zhao Juechen gritted his teeth in frustration, but he couldn’t stop there.

“Regardless of whether you had grown the Spirit Tree or not, it cannot be compared to a chicken! It doesn’t change the fact that you can still share it with others!”

Xiao Cangming’s eyes narrowed, a flicker of anger flashing within them. “Have I not shared it with you?” he said coldly. “I’ve shared its spiritual energy with the Inferno Alchemists so the entire world could benefit from their pills. Not only that, but everyone here has received its spiritual energy before. For you to stand before me and claim otherwise is nothing short of a bold-faced lie.”

Zhao Juechen trembled at his words before responding in a furious tone, “If you have a full bowl of rice and you only share one or two grains with us, can you really call that sharing?! How shameless of you!”

Xiao Cangming sneered, “Do you think the Spirit Tree has unlimited spiritual energy or something? I have shared what I could. If you’re not satisfied with that, it’s none of my issue. In the first place, the Spirit Tree doesn’t even belong to me. I am merely looking after it for someone.”

“Another lie! If the Spirit Tree doesn’t belong to you, who does it belong to?! Your mother?! Don’t make me laugh!”

Whoosh!

Xiao Cangming’s expression darkened, and immense killing intent surged from him.

Seeing this, Zhao Juechen and the others subconsciously backed away.

However, they quickly stopped and moved forward.

“Don’t think you can control us with fear any longer, Xiao Cangming!”

“That’s right! We’re not afraid of you any longer!”

Xiao Meilin suddenly stepped forward and said, “Father, I can’t hold it any longer. Why waste your breath on them? They’ll become corpses in a bit, anyway. Let me bathe in their blood already!”

After a brief moment of silence, Xiao Cangming quietly nodded his head before muttering, “Go ahead.”

With Xiao Cangming’s approval, Xiao Meilin drew her sword as she stepped forward, approaching the sea of experts before them.

“Do you really think you can defeat all of us with your pitiful numbers?” Zhao Juechen sneered. “Only two of you are at the ninth level of God Ascension. The rest of you are between the first level and the seventh level. Even if you doubled your number, you won’t be able to defeat us!”

Zhao Juechen was unaware that Xiao Cangming had fed his cultivation to the Spirit Tree and regressed back to the first level of God Ascension, so in reality, only Xiao Meilin was at the peak of God Ascension.

Li Zhenwu had the second-highest cultivation in the Asura Clan at the seventh level of God Ascension, with Xiao Chen being the third at the fifth level. The others were only between the first and third levels.

Suddenly, her aura erupted, flooding the area with an overbearing pressure that crushed down on the experts like an invisible mountain. Without uttering a single word, Xiao Meilin’s figure blurred and vanished like a ghost.

When she reappeared, she stood amidst their ranks—her presence radiating with a murderous intent so dense it made the air tremble.

“W-Watch out—”

Before anyone could even react, Xiao Meilin swung her sword through the air. It seemed to strike nothing, only empty space. Yet, a heartbeat later, more than twenty cultivators around her plummeted from the sky, their bodies cleaved into countless pieces.

The cultivators froze in shock for an instant, but quickly regained their senses and retaliated, unleashing everything they had at Xiao Meilin without a thought for their spiritual energy.

In the blink of an eye, the entire area erupted into chaos, transforming into a full-fledged battlefield. Li Zhenwu and the others didn’t remain idle and swiftly joined the fray. However, Xiao Cangming decided to stay behind to guard the portal, as he didn’t want anyone to sneak inside and disturb Yuan.

Because of the Asura Clan’s unique physique, which rendered physical attacks ineffective, the intruders were forced to abandon their weapons and rely solely on martial techniques to fight. Of course, this limitation applied only to Xiao Cangming and those who possessed the Violet Nebula Physique. Li Zhenwu and the servants, who were not part of the Asura Clan by blood, remained vulnerable to physical attacks. That said, their weakness did not make them any easier to deal with.

“Asura’s Blade of Execution!” Xiao Chen summoned a colossal blade forged entirely from killing intent, and with each swing, dozens of experts fell, their lives snuffed out in an instant.

As the battle raged on, the sky and earth were drenched in blood, crimson rain falling without end. Hundreds of experts under Zhao Juechen had already fallen, yet their ranks showed no sign of thinning, as reinforcements continued to pour in, replenishing their numbers as fast as they died.

“Do not falter! It is only a matter of time before the Asura Clan falls! Ming Xuanyang, Bai Zhentian, Wu Moyun, come with me! We’ll take care of Xiao Cangming together!” Zhao Juechen roared as he went to confront Xiao Cangming, followed by three other experts.

Zhao Juechen was a powerhouse at the seventh level of God Ascension, while the others were at the fifth or sixth level.

Yet, despite being on at the first level, Xiao Cangming didn’t show even the slightest hint of fear or worry in his gaze.

“God of War’s Astral Arts!” Xiao Cangming wrapped himself in a massive purple avatar and fought back without moving from his location.

Though his cultivation had weakened to the first level of God Ascension, the God of War’s Astral Arts relied more on Soul Strength than cultivation. And since only his cultivation had been affected by feeding the Spirit Tree, Xiao Cangming’s Soul Strength was still unfathomably strong, allowing him to contend with powerhouses of far higher realms easily.

In just several exchanges, Xiao Cangming had not only repelled Zhao Juechen but even killed one of the powerhouses.

“What the hell is this?! I knew he was powerful, but this is beyond anything we know!” Ming Xuanyang exclaimed in shock after witnessing Xiao Cangming’s overwhelming prowess.

“This bastard must’ve been hiding his strength this entire time!” “That’s impossible! He was already monstrously strong before, and you’re saying that wasn’t even his full power?!” Zhao Juechen shouted, clenching his fists as he glared at Xiao Cangming, his voice trembling with disbelief and the bitter realization of his mistake.

“Zhao Juechen,” Xiao Cangming said suddenly, his voice cutting through the chaos like a blade. “Your greatest sin today isn’t daring to bare your fangs at me or the Asura Clan—it’s coveting the Spirit Tree, which belongs to my lord.”

“Your lord? What the hell are you talking about?!”

“Still pretending you don’t know what I’m talking about?” Xiao Cangming said, shaking his head in disappointment. “There’s only one person I would ever address as my ‘lord.’ And you—someone who stood on the battlefield during the Heavenly War of Immortals and Gods—should know exactly who that is.”

Zhao Juechen’s entire being froze as a certain individual appeared in his mind.

“You don’t mean…” he swallowed nervously.

“No! That’s impossible!” Zhao Juechen shook his head. “There’s no way he’s still alive! After all, he perished alongside the Celestial Emperor!”

Although most of the earliest inhabitants of the Primordial Realm were imprisoned before the fall of the first Celestial Emperor, the realm itself wasn’t sealed until his successor took the throne. Before permanently closing it, the second Celestial Emperor cast a few more individuals inside, who revealed the aftermath to the others. Of course, this included the Celestial Emperor’s defeat and Tian Xian’s death.

“Does it look like I am joking to you?” Xiao Cangming said with a serious expression, refusing to elaborate further.

“…”
