Chapter 2222 Spirit Tree(4)

After a moment of silence, Zhao Juechen muttered in a low voice, “So what…?”

“SO WHAT IF THE EVIL GOD IS ALIVE?!” he suddenly roared.

“It doesn’t change anything! As long as we’re stuck in this world, the Spirit Tree is necessary to us, and we’ll do anything to acquire it!” Xiao Cangming sneered, “Even if you somehow manage to acquire the Spirit Tree, it’s not something you can manage. Do you truly think the Spirit Tree is a treasure with limitless power? How naive. Once the Spirit Tree runs out of power, you will return to being deprived of spiritual energy.”

“T-That’s—!”

Zhao Juechen couldn’t refute Xiao Cangming’s words. In fact, he never really thought about the Spirit Tree running out of power. After all, the Spirit Tree has supplied the Asura Clan with seemingly limitless spiritual energy for millions of years without any issue.

Of course, nobody but the Asura Clan was aware that to keep the Spirit Tree alive, Xiao Cangming had to feed it his cultivation.

“Zhao Juechen, I will give you and everyone here one last opportunity to turn around and forget about the Spirit Tree. Otherwise, I will have to cut you down,” Xiao Cangming said.

He turned to look at Xiao Meilin, who was still slaughtering everyone around her like a madman, and added, “The longer you take to decide, the more casualties you will have.”

Zhao Juechen gritted his teeth and trembled. After a moment of silence, he responded, “I will not yield! Today, I will either acquire the Spirit Tree or perish trying!”

Xiao Cangming shook his head in a disappointed manner.

“What a stubborn and foolish decision. If you wish to die so badly, allow me to grant your wish.”

Without another word, Xiao Cangming resumed their battle, attacking first with God of War’s Astral Arts.

Meanwhile, inside the Asura Clan’s world, Yuan finished forming the blood contract with the Spirit Tree. Follow current novels on novel⟡fire.net

<You have successfully formed a blood contract with the Spirit Tree>

After forming the contract, the massive Spirit Tree had disappeared from the Asura Clan’s world and somehow entered Yuan’s Dantian.

“Y-Young Master! What is this?! The Spirit Tree has taken root inside your Dantian!” Feng Yuxiang exclaimed in shock.

“I formed a blood contract with it… But I didn’t expect it to move into my Dantian,” he said as he looked inside his Dantian to see Feng Yuxiang and Lan Yingying gazing at the Spirit Tree with intrigue.

<Due to the Spirit Tree, your cultivation speed has increased by 500,000%>

<Due to the Spirit Tree, your Dantian has significantly expanded in size, allowing you to hold more spiritual energy>

Although the effects of the Spirit Tree seemed extremely good at a glance and could easily turn an ordinary cultivator into a prodigy, it wasn’t very beneficial to Yuan, who already had a ridiculously fast cultivation speed. As for increasing his Dantian’s size, it definitely helped a little, but it wasn’t anything game-changing.

“There must be another use for the Spirit Tree…” Yuan refused to believe that he had made Xiao Cangming sacrifice so much of his time and effort for something so insignificant and believed that he had yet to see the Spirit Tree’s true prowess.

Unfortunately, he didn’t have the time to study the Spirit Tree, as Xiao Cangming and the others were still dealing with the intruders.

However, just as Yuan prepared to leave to group up with the others, he halted his movements when he noticed a figure approaching him from a distance.

The approaching figure was a beautiful young woman who appeared to have only just reached adulthood, yet within her eyes lingered an unfathomable depth of experience.

“Xiao Hua…?” Yuan muttered as his eyes fell upon her.

He should’ve felt joy at seeing her awake, but instead, unease gripped his heart. Something was wrong. Her gaze burned with unmistakable fury, and the killing intent radiating from her was so intense it felt almost tangible, all of it directed straight at him.

“What’s wrong, Xiao Hua?” Yuan called out to her in a soft voice. “Are you hurt somewhere?”

As Xiao Hua slowly approached, a look of pure disdain twisted her beautiful facial features. Her voice dripped with venom as she snapped, “Don’t you dare act so familiar with me, you bastard!”

“…”

Yuan was speechless, but he had an idea what was going on.

Meanwhile, Xiao Hua quickened her pace, her aura flaring violently as she began to release her cultivation. Although her cultivation had reverted to the first level of Spirit Apprentice upon obtaining her new vessel, the divine nature of her new body—combined with the dense spiritual energy saturating the air—caused her cultivation to soar uncontrollably, and with every step she took, her cultivation leapt by several levels.

“My father may have forgiven you,” Xiao Hua continued, her voice trembling with fury, “but I will never forgive you for killing my mother!”

Sure enough, just as Xiao Cangming had warned, Xiao Hua’s memories had regressed, returning to the Primordial Era, to a time before she had forgiven Tian Xian for killing her mother.

“Calm down for a minute, Xiao Hua. Your memories are a bit messed up right now, but they should return to normal—”

“Shut up!” Xiao Hua suddenly summoned a sword and leaped forward, striking Yuan without warning.

Yuan calmly caught her blade with his fingers and sighed, “If it’ll make you feel better, you can strike at me as much as you want.”

Yuan decided to let Xiao Hua do as she pleased until she calmed down and immediately loosened his fingers, releasing the sword.

Without a word, Xiao Hua struck again with zero hesitation, this time with more power.

Yuan didn’t even bother to block her sword and allowed her to strike his body.

Even though her attacks had no effect on Yuan, Xiao Hua refused to stop, striking at him again and again—like a child throwing a tantrum in her fury.

“I swear I will kill you!” Xiao Hua roared as her cultivation soared again, entering Spirit Enlightenment.

chapters.title: Chapter 2223 Xiao Hua'S Awakening
chapters.body:
Chapter 2223 Xiao Hua'S Awakening

As Xiao Hua’s cultivation rose, her strikes became heavier and more powerful, and she even started using martial techniques.

“Young Master, it looks like she really wants to kill you,” Feng Yuxiang remarked as she watched from inside his Dantian, feeling a bit dazed, as she could’ve never imagined Xiao Hua attacking Yuan so fiercely.

“Her memories regressed back to the Primordial Era—to a time when we didn’t have the best relationship. Back then, she would always try to kill me when she had the chance,” Yuan sighed with a bitter smile. “I don’t blame her, though. I did kill her mother, after all.”

“What did you do to calm her?” Lan Yingying asked.

“If you do it again, it might calm her again.”

“I didn’t do anything special. Whenever she tried to kill me, I would fight back and beat her up quite badly. It took some time and a lot of beating, but eventually, she calmed down,” Yuan said as he recalled his past.

“…”

Feng Yuxiang and Lan Yingying were left speechless by his words.

“If you’re going to ask me if I am going to repeat the same process, I’ll have to decline. I was a very different person back then.”

“So what are you going to do? Continue to let her try to kill you until she regains her memories? Who knows when that will happen.” Feng Yuxiang said.

“If you’re unwilling to fight her, I’ll gladly do it in your place,” she said with a faint smile. “I’ve always wanted to face her seriously. Besides, as fellow servants under the same master, it’s our duty to clean up each other’s messes.”

Of course, Feng Yuxiang didn’t mean it in a malicious way. Due to their competitive relationship, it was only natural for her to want to fight Xiao Hua.

Sensing Feng Yuxiang’s desire to fight, Yuan seriously pondered letting her fight Xiao Hua.

“Don’t worry, I won’t harm her too much,” she assured him.

“Alright.” Yuan eventually agreed. He wanted to see if fighting Feng Yuxiang might help Xiao Hua regain her memories faster.

Right as Xiao Hua went for another strike, Feng Yuxiang immediately emerged from his Dantian to block it.

“Who the hell are you?” Xiao Hua exclaimed as she distanced herself from them.

“I am the Young Master’s number one servant,” she proudly declared.

“Attacking your own master is a grave sin for a servant, even if you have lost your memories.”

“Servant? What the hell are you talking about? There’s no way in hell I’d serve the bastard who killed my mother!” Xiao Hua roared.

Normally, servants were incapable of attacking their masters. However, Yuan didn’t view Xiao Hua or her strikes as a threat. In his eyes, she was just having a harmless tantrum, so no penalty was imposed on her.

Feng Yuxiang shrugged, “Anyway, if you insist on continuing to attack the Young Master, I will have to put an end to it.”

“You? Stop me? I’d like to see you try!” Xiao Hua shouted as she lunged at Feng Yuxiang.

By now, Xiao Hua’s cultivation had reached the peak of Divine Grandmaster. Meanwhile, Feng Yuxiang was at the peak of True Immortal.

Yet, despite the disparity between their cultivation, Xiao Hua went on the offensive, striking without a care, almost as if the difference didn’t exist. Updates are released by novｅlfire.net

If Feng Yuxiang had been an ordinary cultivator, Xiao Hua might have been able to overcome their difference and defeat her. However, Feng Yuxiang was no ordinary cultivator but a Primordial Phoenix with the Immortal Monarch’s seal.

Feng Yuxiang dodged most of Xiao Hua’s strikes but would occasionally strike back with her flames, barely putting any power into it, almost as if she was toying with Xiao Hua.

“This power… a phoenix?” Xiao Hua narrowed her eyes after recognizing Feng Yuxiang’s flames.

Though she couldn’t explain why, Xiao Hua felt an inexplicable irritation toward Feng Yuxiang’s very presence, despite having no memories of her.

“A phoenix serving a human? That’s unheard of,” Xiao Hua muttered to herself, disbelief flashing in her eyes.

“What’s wrong, you little brat? Is this all you’ve got? You won’t be able to defeat me at this rate, much less defeat the Young Master,” Feng Yuxiang continued to mock Xiao Hua as they fought, which only irritated her even further.

“Shut up, you damn pigeon! You’re annoying as hell!” Xiao Hua shouted as her cultivation soared past the Divine Realm and entered the Immortal Realm, becoming a Bronze Immortal.

Yuan raised an eyebrow when he heard Xiao Hua call Feng Yuxiang a “pigeon”—a remark that sounded exactly like something the present Xiao Hua would say.

‘Is fighting Feng Feng triggering her memories subconsciously?’ Yuan wondered. ‘This is working even better than I expected…’

Feng Yuxiang also noticed this and began to tease Xiao Hua even more.

In response, Xiao Hua’s attacks grew more fierce, and her killing intent soared.

As of this moment, Xiao Hua’s urge to kill Feng Yuxiang has surpassed her desire to kill Yuan.

Sometime later, Xiao Hua’s cultivation reached True Immortal. However, it wasn’t just her cultivation that improved. Her fighting style and movement have undergone drastic changes since they began, forcing Feng Yuxiang to fight more seriously.

“Hahaha! This is more like it! I have always wanted to fight you seriously, but never got the chance!” Feng Yuxiang laughed excitedly as she started using her phoenix power more seriously, even transforming.

After several thousand more exchanges, Xiao Hua’s aura suddenly erupted, her cultivation surging past the Immortal Realm and stepping into God Ascension. A flood of Celestial Qi burst from her body, shaking the air around them.

“Seriously?!” Feng Yuxiang cried out, eyes wide with disbelief. She had never imagined Xiao Hua’s cultivation would break beyond the True Immortal realm.

Upon reaching the first level of God Ascension, Xiao Hua’s strength soared to unimaginable heights, and she began driving Feng Yuxiang back with overwhelming force. Yet her aura showed no sign of stabilizing, and a few minutes later, her cultivation surged again, breaking through to the second level.

“Second level God Ascension?! That’s cheating!” Feng Yuxiang exclaimed.

chapters.title: Chapter 2224 Fighting Xiao Hua
chapters.body:
Chapter 2224 Fighting Xiao Hua

After Xiao Hua’s cultivation reached the second level of God Ascension, Feng Yuxiang was no longer a match and was forced to go on the defensive.

“What’s wrong, you annoying bird?! Why have you gone quiet when you were talking so much just moments ago?!” Xiao Hua spoke with a mocking smile on her face.

Feng Yuxiang gritted her teeth in frustration. She could never have anticipated that Xiao Hua would enter the God Ascension realm this quickly. After all, it would normally take decades for someone to regain their full cultivation after obtaining a new vessel, but it hadn’t been a full day since Xiao Hua entered her new vessel.

“Don’t you dare look down on me, you little brat!” Feng Yuxiang roared as her body erupted in flames, transforming into her true phoenix form. In an instant, a blazing pressure swept across the world, igniting the very air around her.

“Primordial Fire!” Feng Yuxiang cried, beating her massive, flame-wreathed wings once. A storm of invisible fire cascaded from above, raining down upon Xiao Hua like divine wrath.

A vicious light flickered within Xiao Hua’s eyes as her aura condensed for a brief moment before exploding like a bomb.

“Asura Clan’s Immortal Slaying Art!” Xiao Hua roared as she unleashed one of the Asura Clan’s supreme techniques. In the blink of an eye, her power swept across the sky, extinguishing every trace of the Primordial Fire.

“What?!” Feng Yuxiang’s eyes widened with shock, unable to believe how easily Xiao Hua had removed her fire.

However, Xiao Hua’s prowess wasn’t the only thing that shocked Feng Yuxiang, as Xiao Hua’s cultivation had increased by another level right before she struck, reaching the third level of God Ascension.

“Young Master… I don’t think I can handle her any longer, especially if her cultivation continues to rise…” Feng Yuxiang said to Yuan using voice transmission.

“…”

Yuan wasn’t too surprised about Xiao Hua’s cultivation since she had already reached this level in the past. That said, he was definitely taken aback by how quickly she regained it.

‘This can’t be just the result of her new vessel…’ he thought.

He checked her status, but the only thing that had changed from it was her cultivation.

“Young Master!” Feng Yuxiang cried for his help again when she could no longer hold back Xiao Hua.

Immediately, Yuan responded, retrieving Number One Under Heaven to block Xiao Hua’s attack.

“I’ll take care of this,” he said to Feng Yuxiang.

Although he had no intention of fighting Xiao Hua, the situation had escalated to the point where he could no longer afford to hold back.

“Look who’s finally done hiding behind a pathetic bird,” Xiao Hua mocked.

Before Yuan could respond, Xiao Hua shifted her gaze to Feng Yuxiang and continued, “And you… Now that you can no longer deal with me, you’re going to make your master fight? How laughable.”

“This damn brat…!” Feng Yuxiang’s clenched fists trembled, and her eyebrows twitched uncontrollably. However, she couldn’t refute Xiao Hua’s words.

“Too bad it won’t make any difference. Once I kill this bastard, I’ll definitely take my time to kill you next!” Xiao Hua roared as she changed her focus to Yuan, her aura surging as she attacked him with newfound ferocity.

Unlike his prior opponents, Xiao Hua used her Celestial Qi without restraint. Although it wasn’t as strong as it should be for someone at her cultivation due to how quickly she improved it, it was still strong enough to push him back.

“What’s wrong, Tian Xian?! You’re a lot weaker than I remember!” Xiao Hua mocked as she continued pushing him back.

“That’s because I am not Tian Xian. My name is Yuan.”

Xiao Hua’s brows furrowed at his response, and she shouted, “Do you really think I will fall for such a bald-faced lie?!” “My father may be foolish enough to forgive you, but I am not like him!”

13:37

Xiao Hua’s cultivation continued to surge, and after many exchanges, she had another breakthrough, reaching the fourth level of God Ascension.

“Just how much further is her cultivation going to increase?!” Feng Yuxiang’s jaws dropped at the scene.

In order to clash with Xiao Hua without hurting her or losing to her, Yuan was forced to use True Dragon Awakening and Chaotic Essence.

“Why did you have to kill Mother?! Why?!” Xiao Hua suddenly exclaimed, tears flowing down her face.

“I’m sorry.” Yuan had no excuse and could only offer his apology.

“If you truly regret it, then let me kill you! Atone with your death!”

“Sorry, but I cannot do that.”

“Then you don’t really mean it!” Xiao Hua roared, her cultivation entering the fifth level of God Ascension. Chapters fırst released on NoveIꜰire.net

“I hate you! I hate, hate, hate you!”

Xiao Hua’s cultivation continued to soar without any sign of slowing down. In fact, it was speeding up.

When Xiao Hua reached the sixth level, Yuan had to use Eternal Essence.

A few exchanges later, Xiao Hua’s cultivation entered the seventh level.

Xiao Hua was now a threat that even Yuan could not handle.

“Damn it!” Yuan growled through gritted teeth. Seeing no sign of Xiao Hua calming down, he was left with no choice but to invoke his authority as her master for the first time ever.

“Halt!” Yuan commanded, his voice echoing with undeniable authority. In an instant, Xiao Hua froze mid-motion, her body stopping as if bound by invisible chains.

“What the hell is this?! What did you do to me?!” Xiao Hua exclaimed with a bewildered look on her face.

“He simply commanded you as your master,” Feng Yuxiang calmly responded.

“What?! That’s impossible! I am not even his servant!” Feng Yuxiang shrugged, “If you’re not willing to believe it despite the evidence before you, there’s nothing else I can say that will convince you.”

Xiao Hua fell silent for a moment. Then, a purple aura—burning like ethereal flames—began to rise from her body, rippling through the air with intense power.

“I am… not… his… servant!” Xiao Hua roared, her voice echoing with defiance as she attempted to defy Yuan’s authority.

As a result, the authority immediately retaliated, inflicting immense pain upon her.

Yet, Xiao Hua continued to resist and fight back, to the point where blood started flowing from her eyes, ears, and nose. “This crazy little—!” Feng Yuxiang was left utterly speechless.

chapters.title: Chapter 2225 Fighting Xiao Hua(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 2225 Fighting Xiao Hua(2)

“Stop fighting against it. You’ll only hurt yourself doing so,” Yuan said after seeing Xiao Hua attempting to resist his authority, almost subconsciously withdrawing it.

“Shut up! I don’t know what you did to me, but I would never willingly be your servant!” she shouted back. “You must’ve forced me into becoming your servant without me noticing somehow!”

“You’ll kill yourself at this rate!” Feng Yuxiang said. ʀᴇᴀᴅ ʟᴀᴛᴇsᴛ ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀs ᴀᴛ noᴠelfire.net

“So what?! I’d rather die than become my mother’s killer—this bastard’s servant!” Feng Yuxiang looked at Yuan with a worried face. If Xiao Hua continues to resist, she will definitely die.

A master and servant pact was as sacred and powerful as a heavenly oath, so not even a God Ascension cultivator would be able to resist it.

“Ahhhh!” Xiao Hua started screaming, not from pain but from sheer determination.

“I forgot how stubborn you were in the past…” Yuan sighed.

It was clear that Xiao Hua would keep resisting—either until she broke free from his authority or perished in the attempt.

With no other choice, Yuan withdrew his authority over her.

The instant the restraints on her were released, Xiao Hua’s aura skyrocketed, and her cultivation soared straight into the eighth level of God Ascension. However, not even a heartbeat later, it entered the ninth level, reaching the peak of God Ascension.

“Ninth level God Ascension?! Don’t tell me she’s going to become a Cultivation God next?!” Feng Yuxiang exclaimed.

The moment she broke free, Xiao Hua lunged forward, her arms snapping toward Yuan’s neck in a seizing motion.

The pressure radiating from Xiao Hua’s aura was so immense that Yuan couldn’t even move a finger. In that instant, she seized his neck effortlessly, her grip tightening with terrifying strength.

Then, with her other hand, she thrust her sword forward, driving the blade straight into Yuan’s heart.

Yuan coughed up a mouthful of blood, but despite how grave it appeared, his life was not in danger, and the wound was only superficial.

However, Xiao Hua didn’t stop there, and her aura surged violently, almost as if she was preparing for her ultimate technique.

“Young Master!” Seeing this, Feng Yuxiang immediately attempted to help, but Xiao Hua’s overbearing pressure was so immense that she couldn’t even lift a finger, much less help.

“Today, Tian Xian, I’ll avenge my mother by killing you!” Xiao Hua cried, her voice raw with grief and burning fury.

Suddenly, a calm yet familiar voice echoed through the air. “Stop.”

Xiao Hua instinctively turned toward the sound, but before she could fully face it, she felt a firm hand press against her head, halting her movement.

‘When did they get behind me?!’ she cried inwardly.

The voice continued, “I won’t let you hurt Brother Yuan anymore.”

“Brother Yuan? Who the hell are you?” Xiao Hua demanded as she turned fully toward the voice.

To her astonishment, she was met with the sight of a young girl who shared her facial features—so much so that it felt as though she were staring into a mirror.

“That’s…” Feng Yuxiang swallowed hard after seeing the individual standing behind Xiao Hua, as she was the original owner of Xiao Hua’s previous vessel.

“A mere mortal… what do you think you can achieve?” Xiao Hua sneered after realizing the young girl’s inferior cultivation.

“Although our souls may have separated, I still have your memories,” said the young girl.

Then, without warning, she activated a memory-transfer technique, one she had learned from Xiao Hua’s own memories.

In the next instant, a torrent of memories surged into Xiao Hua’s mind—the very memories she had once lost.

From the moment she forgave Tian Xian in the Primordial Era to when she first met Yuan in the Lower Heaven, millions of years of memories overwhelmed Xiao Hua.

Xiao Hua released her grip on the sword and collapsed to her knees, her expression blank and unfocused, almost as if she’d become a soulless body. However, in reality, the memories she had just regained spanned millions of years, and it would take time for her mind to fully comprehend them.

While Xiao Hua was in such a state, the young girl shifted her focus to Yuan.

“Brother Yuan, are you okay?” she approached him with a worried face.

Even though Yuan knew the person before him was no longer the Xiao Hua he once knew, a wave of nostalgia washed over him, as if she had never left his side at all.

“I’m fine,” Yuan said as he forcefully yanked Xiao Hua’s sword from his body. “Is she okay? What did you do to her?”

“I only returned to her her memories,” she calmly replied. “It might take some time for her to snap out of it, but she’ll be fine.”

“…”

After a moment of silence, Yuan asked, “You’re the original owner of that body, right? What is your name?”

“I…”

The young girl opened her mouth, but she froze for a moment before responding, “I don’t have a name. By the time my consciousness had formed, I was already known as Xiao Hua.”

“What about your parents? Surely, they gave you a name.” Feng Yuxiang said.

The young girl shook her head softly and said, “I don’t have any parents. To be precise, I wasn’t born through ordinary means between two beings. From what I know, I came into existence without a physical body and wandered this world as a formless being—until Xiao Hua’s soul merged with mine. Only then did my body begin to take form.”

“What in the…?” Feng Yuxiang was left speechless by such an odd story.

“A natural birth? Like natural treasures and spirits? Are you even human?” she couldn’t help but ask.

The young girl looked at her hands, seemingly pondering Feng Yuxiang’s question.

“Does it even matter?” Yuan suddenly said.

He approached the young girl and patted her head like he used to before asking, “If you don’t have a name, you can just give yourself one.”

The young girl stared him in the eyes and calmly said, “Brother Yuan, I want you to give me a name.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2226 Lingyue
chapters.body:
Chapter 2226 Lingyue

“Me? Naming you?” Yuan’s eyes widened slightly at the young girl’s request.

The young girl nodded in confirmation.

“…”

Yuan fell silent for a good moment before speaking again, “In that case, how does Lingyue sound?”

A weird light flickered within Yuan’s eyes as he spoke the name.

“Lingyue…” The young girl mumbled.

“Do you want a different name?” Yuan asked after seeing her reaction.

“No!” she hastily responded. “Lingyue is good!”

“It’s a pleasure to meet you, Lingyue.”

“Un!” Lingyue nodded with a bright smile on her face.

“Ugh…” A grunt suddenly resounded, causing Yuan and the others to look at Xiao Hua, who was starting to wake up from her daze.

A few more moments later, Xiao Hua no longer had a blank look on her face, and she started looking around with a somewhat confused expression.

“Xiao Hua,” Yuan called softly.

She turned to face him but remained silent. Then, her expression began to contort with sorrow, and moments later, tears streamed down her face as she broke into a heart-wrenching cry.

“Waaah! I’m so sorry!” she started apologizing.

“What are you apologizing for?” Yuan said with a smile. “If this is about what happened just now, it’s not your fault. Your memories were messed up, after all.”

“That doesn’t change the fact that I pointed my blade at you! I even seriously tried to kill you! If she hadn’t stopped me in time, I would’ve likely killed you!” Xiao Hua said as she looked at Lingyue.

“Kill me?” Yuan chuckled. “You should know me well enough to know that I won’t die to something like that.”

“Regardless of what you say, I deserve to be punished! Please, punish me, Brother Yu—Master Tian!” Xiao Hua said. “I won’t complain even if you decide to kill me!”

“What would I gain from killing you?” Yuan shook his head. “As for punishing you…”

He pondered for a moment before speaking, “Alright, I have decided your punishment. You are forbidden from calling me Master Tian.”

“That’s…” Xiao Hua wore a dumbfounded look on her face.

“What? Don’t tell me you’re not going to accept the punishment?”

“But what else am I supposed to call you…?” she asked.

“That’s something for you to decide. You can continue to call me Brother Yuan, but now that you have regained all of your memories, I understand that it might be difficult for you, so I’ll let you decide.”

“…”

Xiao Hua fell silent for a good minute before responding, “Master Yu—”

“Rejected,” Yuan instantly interrupted her from finishing.

“I won’t accept anything that starts with ‘master’ or ‘lord’ or anything similar,” he continued.

Xiao Hua sighed and said, “In that case, I’ll just call you Yuan.”

Yuan nodded.

“Now then, we’ve wasted enough time here. Let’s go out and help the Asura Clan with the intruders. We’ll continue this afterward.”

“Okay.”

They quickly left the Asura Clan’s world and appeared outside shortly after.

However, by the time they arrived, Xiao Cangming and the others had already cleaned up half of the intruders.

“My lord,” Xiao Cangming, who was still guarding the portal, greeted him the moment they appeared. “Sorry it took so long. Something came up.” Yuan said, his eyes pointing at Xiao Hua and Lingyue.

“They’re already awake?” Xiao Cangming was surprised by how quickly they woke up, as he had anticipated it would take several days, if not weeks.

“I’ll explain everything later. What happened here?” Yuan asked, though the answer was already clear to him.

The ground was strewn with mangled corpses and soaked in blood—a sight eerily reminiscent of the Primordial Era, when he himself had painted countless battlefields in crimson as Tian Xian.

Xiao Cangming turned toward Xiao Meilin and the others, who were still fighting off the intruders, and said calmly, “We gave them a chance to turn back and leave, but as you can see, they chose otherwise.”

“…”

Without a word, Yuan suddenly approached the battlefield.

“All of you, stop.” Read full story at novel·fiɾe·net

His calm voice rippled across the battlefield, imbued with quiet authority, and in an instant, all movement came to a halt.

“If you came here for the Spirit Tree, you’re too late, as it no longer exists. So you can either leave now with your life or die a pointless death.”

“The Spirit Tree no longer exists? You’re lying!” one of them shouted.

Yuan merely shrugged. “Go see for yourself if you don’t believe me. Even if I were lying, it changes nothing—you still won’t get the Spirit Tree, and trying will only get you killed.”

The cultivators gritted their teeth in frustration, yet none could refute Yuan’s words. With most of their comrades already defeated, continuing the battle was meaningless. Still, none bothered to flee since they knew that even if they turned their backs now, the Asura Clan would hunt them down without mercy.

Yuan could see their concerns and said, “If you leave now, I promise you that we won’t chase after you later. We’ll even pretend none of this happened.”

Yuan was willing to show them mercy, for he found little fault in their actions. Deprived of spiritual energy and driven by desperation, these cultivators had turned their blades against the Asura Clan, who had long monopolized the Spirit Tree, something that would solve their problem, even if only temporarily. Moreover, these cultivators had no clue about the origin of the Spirit Tree or that the Asura Clan had grown it. In their eyes, the Asura Clan was doing something the first Celestial Emperor had done, and if Yuan had been in their shoes, he would’ve likely done the same.

“How… How do we know that you won’t go back on your word?” One of the cultivators suddenly questioned, his face full of skepticism.

Yuan shrugged, “You will have to take my word for it. Or would you rather continue this meaningless battle and die for certain? The choice is up to you.”

When no one moved after a full minute, Yuan spoke again—this time to the Asura Clan.

“If any of them remain by the time I finish counting to three, kill them all.”

“As you wish, Master!”

Xiao Meilin was the first to acknowledge Yuan’s command, showing the cultivators that Yuan truly held some authority over the Asura Clan.

Chapter 2227 As A Friend
