Some time later, Yuan and the Asura Clan gathered in a room to talk about Xiao Hua’s situation and the Asura Clan’s future.

The moment they sat down, Xiao Cangming shifted his gaze toward Xiao Hua and spoke, “Even though it’s normal for cultivators who have changed vessels to regain their cultivation quickly, you have recovered your cultivation a little too quickly. Did something happen?”

Xiao Hua’s body trembled at Xiao Cangming’s words, but not out of fear that he would discover what had happened. Merely recalling the moment she had attacked Yuan was enough to send a shiver through her.

“I don’t know. It just happened,” she calmly replied.

Xiao Cangming turned to look at Yuan to see if he knew anything, but he also shook his head.

“In that case, what about your memories? How much did it regress?” Xiao Cangming continued to question Xiao Hua.

“That’s…”

Xiao Hua hesitated to tell the truth this time. After all, if Xiao Cangming learned that she’d tried to kill Yuan, regardless of her situation, he’d definitely be enraged. Of course, she wasn’t afraid of him and preferred not to deal with him. “Don’t worry, she’s regained all of her memories already,” Yuan suddenly answered. “And it was all because of her.”

Xiao Cangming followed Yuan’s gaze to Lingyue, who was quietly sitting beside him.

“You’re…”

“My name is Lingyue,” she introduced herself.

“What?” Xiao Cangming’s eyes widened with bewilderment after hearing her name.

It was a reaction that puzzled Xiao Hua and the others in the room. After all, it was a secret that Yuan had revealed only to him.

“I gave her that name,” Yuan explained.

“…”

Xiao Cangming fell silent for a moment before muttering, “Is that so…”

Like Xiao Hua, Lingyue’s cultivation had risen since her awakening, though not as dramatically. She had only managed to reach the peak of the Divine Ancestor realm.

That said, it wasn’t unnatural. After all, Xiao Hua had reached the peak of God Ascension in her past lives, while Lingyue was just someone who’d received her memories.

Soon, they moved the topic to the Spirit Tree.

“What about the Spirit Tree?” Xiao Cangming asked.

“It entered my Dantian,” Yuan replied.

“Without the Spirit Tree, this world will collapse soon,” Xiao Cangming remarked.

In the Primordial Realm, only the Asura Clan had the luxury of having their own world because it required an immense amount of spiritual energy to maintain one, something they could only achieve with the Spirit Tree.

Seeing the guilty look on Yuan’s face, Xiao Cangming smiled and said, “Well, it doesn’t really matter since we won’t be staying in the Primordial Realm for much longer.”

“Does that mean you plan to leave the Primordial Realm?” Yuan asked even though he already knew the answer.

“That’s right. Now that you have returned and I have fulfilled my mission, there’s no longer any reason for us to stay in here.”

“I see… What will you be doing once you are outside?”

Xiao Cangming pondered for a moment before asking, “The Shadow Army, do they still exist?”

He nodded, “Yes, and they’re more active than ever.”

“In that case, I’ll stay with them and work towards recovering my cultivation. Only then will I be able to serve you properly again.”

Yuan smiled and said, “Cangming, you have already served me long enough. Now that you have a wife again, you should focus on your family.”

“That’s unacceptable. I swore to be your sword until I draw my last breath,” he quickly refused.

However, Yuan shook his head and said, “Don’t take this the wrong way, but a lot of time has passed since then, and I already have a reliable sword by my side. You have done enough for me.”

“…”

Xiao Cangming fell silent again.

After several minutes of silence, Yuan spoke, “With that being said, I will likely still need your help from time to time, and when that time comes, I hope you will be willing to assist me as a friend.”

“As a friend, huh?” A profound smile appeared on Xiao Cangming’s face.

“If this is what you truly want, then I have no reason to decline.”

“Just call me Yuan from now on.”

“Alright, Yuan.”

“Thanks.”

Xiao Meilin stared at her father with wide eyes, utterly surprised that he had agreed to something like this so easily.

“W-What about me?” she suddenly asked.

chapters.title: Chapter 2228 A Broken Treasure
chapters.body:
Chapter 2228 A Broken Treasure

“W-What about me?” Xiao Meilin suddenly asked.

Yuan looked at her and replied with a question, “What about you? I never even accepted you as my servant in the first place.”

“No way! How can you say that to me now?! That’s too cruel, Master Tian! Are you really going to throw me away after using me for so long?!” Xiao Meilin exclaimed.

Yuan showed a stiff smile and said, “Don’t make it sound so weird. You’re not a toy. Also, while you’re not my servant, you’re still my disciple. Do you intend on staying as a disciple for the rest of your life?”

“Of course!” Xiao Meilin answered without any hesitation.

Yuan rubbed his eyes and sighed, “In that case, I have failed as your teacher and don’t qualify to be one anymore.”

“You’re acting pathetic, Elder Sister,” said Xiao Hua. “You should stop bothering Yuan.”

“That’s easy for you to say since you’re always following him around! I’ve been wondering for a long time now, but why did you get to go with him while I am stuck here? And did you just call him ‘Yuan’?! What happened to Master Tian? Did you really regain all of your memories? Or did you hit your head somewhere after awakening?” “Blame your own incompetence for not being chosen,” Xiao Hua said with a calm sneer. “I was chosen because I’m not only more talented and powerful than you but, more importantly, I’m not annoying like you.”

“You little bitch… are you looking for a fight with me now?” Xiao Meilin’s eyebrows twitched nonstop at Xiao Hua’s provocation. “Stop it. Both of you.” Yuan sighed.

Xiao Meilin gritted her teeth and fell silent, but it was only for a brief moment.

“You didn’t answer my other question. Why are you suddenly calling him by his name? That’s disrespectful, and you should know that.”

“That’s none of your business,” Xiao Hua said, refusing to answer.

Yuan then said, “I told her to stop calling me Master Tian. I’m sure I asked you to do the same when we first met.”

“So not only is she annoying, but she’s also disobedient? Why am I not surprised?” Xiao Hua chuckled.

Xiao Meilin trembled in anger, and her aura started to surge violently. However, before she could even say another word, Xiao Cangming hammered her head with his fist, instantly wiping away her killing intent.

“Your inability to control your temper is exactly why I never allowed you to follow Yuan,” he said calmly. “Rather than be helpful, you’d only cause more trouble for him.”

“…”

Xiao Meilin fell silent again.

“Don’t be so down,” Li Zhenwu finally spoke up. “You can always temper your control, and now that we’ll be leaving the Primordial Realm, you’ll have plenty of chances to—” ɪꜰ ʏᴏᴜ ᴡᴀɴᴛ ᴛᴏ ʀᴇᴀᴅ ᴍᴏʀᴇ ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀs, ᴘʟᴇᴀsᴇ ᴠɪsɪᴛ NoveIꜰire.net

“I know.” Xiao Meilin interrupted, but she didn’t say more and fell silent again.

Some time later, Yuan spoke, “I have been thinking about something. The Primordial Realm… It’s no longer filled with criminals like it was in the past, correct?”

Xiao Cangming nodded, “Yes, since most of the first generation are long dead. Why do you ask? Don’t tell me you want to…”

Yuan smiled faintly. “I wonder… would it be alright to open the Primordial Realm and let those who wish to leave do so?”

He had been pondering this ever since his encounter with Wu Qi, and that thought had only deepened after witnessing the cultivators’ desperation.

Xiao Cangming responded seriously, “Honestly, I have thought about this more than just a few times. While there are many who wish to leave this world, and we have the power to release them, the problem is what comes after.”

“If the current Celestial Emperor wanted to release us, he would’ve done so long ago,” Xiao Cangming said with a faint bitterness in his tone. “While it’s technically possible to leave through the Supreme Heaven’s Legacy, that so-called path is nothing but false hope—a cruel game that dangles freedom before the desperate. After all my time in the Primordial Realm, I can count on one hand those who’ve ever succeeded in fulfilling the legacy.”

“So what you’re saying is, we have to deal with the Celestial Emperor before we can release these people? I figured it would be like this.” Yuan sighed.

“After all, even if the people get to leave, as long as they have the exile’s branding on them, they won’t truly be free.”

Yuan narrowed his eyes slightly and asked, “This branding… what is it? It can’t be a curse since it didn’t appear when I used the Sacred Chalice of Purification on you.”

Xiao Cangming shook his head.

“I’m not sure either.”

“It’s a curse.”

“What?”

Everyone in the room turned to Mu Xuelian, who’d just spoken.

“Lord Shiva said it’s a curse.”

“Can you elaborate? If it’s a curse, why didn’t my Sacred Chalice of Purification activate? It’s a treasure that can remove all curses.”

Shiva took control of Mu Xuelian’s body and beckoned Yuan, “Show me that little treasure of yours.”

Yuan didn’t question her and handed her the Sacred Chalice of Purification.

“Hmph.”

Shiva took a quick glance at it before tossing it back to Yuan as if it were trash.

“The curse was created with the power of an Eternal. A broken toy like that won’t have any effect on it.”

“Wait a second…” Yuan rubbed his eyes in a stressful manner and continued, “So you’re telling me that not only is an Eternal involved, but my Sacred Chalice of Purification is a ‘broken’ treasure? Even though it functions properly and is powerful enough to remove most curses in existence?”

“An Eternal also created that treasure, and I happen to know its creator,” Shiva revealed. “What? An Eternal creating treasures?” Yuan’s eyes widened at this information.

“I didn’t even think an Eternal would show such interest.”

Shiva snickered, “Not even Gods are impervious to boredom, you know? And while that thing might be a treasure to you, it’s nothing but a toy in our eyes. In fact, most of the treasures that appear ‘naturally’ in this world are our creation.”

chapters.title: Chapter 2229 Forbidden Void Treasures
chapters.body:
Chapter 2229 Forbidden Void Treasures

“The Eternals are the creators of natural treasures? You’ve got to be kidding me…” Yuan mumbled in a bewildered voice after learning this information from Shiva.

“Most people believe treasures are creations of the Heavens,” Shiva continued, her tone laced with mockery. “But that’s merely a delusion born from their ignorance of the unknown. Honestly, it’s quite hilarious.”

Xiao Cangming then said, “But I have witnessed treasures materializing out of thin air with my very eyes. It usually happens in places where spiritual energy is abundant.”

“Sorry to disappoint you, but that is also not ‘natural’. As for how it’s achieved… It’s a little complicated, so I’ll save myself the trouble.”

Yuan then spoke, “I couldn’t care less how it’s done. I want to know why. I highly doubt the Eternals are putting these treasures in our world for fun.”

Shiva smiled faintly and said, “Then you underestimate how unbearably bored we become. We Eternals have existed since the dawn of time—you can’t even begin to fathom how many ages we’ve spent drifting alone in the void.”

“That said, I’m sure not every Eternal is doing it just for fun. Some probably don’t want to waste their effort or just want their toys to be used.”

“What about cursed treasures?” Li Zhenwu asked. “Those that possess great power but also carry dangerous backlashes.”

“Anything that you humans didn’t create was created by Eternals.” Yuan then spoke with a mocking tone. “In that case, we humans aren’t far behind—and some of us might even surpass the Eternals in craftsmanship, considering how some of those natural treasures are complete trash.”

“Hahaha!” Shiva suddenly burst out laughing.

Moments later, she stopped and said, “How naive. Most of the treasures were made trash on purpose.”

“Why would they do something like that?” Xiao Meilin asked.

“Due to the absolute power, our influence in this world is heavily restricted, and that includes our creations. If we created treasures too powerful, they wouldn’t be able to enter this world.”

“Wait a second…” Yuan suddenly spoke up. “Are you referring to the Forbidden Void Treasures?”

“What are Forbidden Void Treasures?” Xiao Chen asked with a puzzled look on his face.

“There existed treasures born from the Starry Sky—found drifting within the endless void,” Yuan explained. “However, no matter the method, these treasures could never be brought back to the Nine Heavens, as though some unseen force forbade their entry into our world. That is why they came to be known as Forbidden Void Treasures.”

“I have never heard of such a thing until today,” Xiao Cangming remarked, and he considered himself very knowledgeable.

“Me neither,” Xiao Meilin said. Dıscover more novels at ɴovelfire.net

“Well, they weren’t discovered until after the Immortal Monarch’s Era came to an end,” he said.

“That explains it.”

“Anyway, you said my Sacred Chalice of Purification is broken? How can I fix it?”

“Fix it? You don’t have that capability.” Shiva said while shaking her head.

“I may not look like it, but I know a thing or two about crafting treasures,” said Yuan.

“So what? Even if you’re the best craftsman in the world, you won’t compare to an Eternal.”

“So it’s a matter of technique.”

“At least you get it.”

Yuan may have been the God of Creation in the past with god-like abilities, but there’s a vast difference between god-like and truly godly skills. Not only that, but Eternals likely had their own crafting methods, so regardless of Yuan’s abilities, he wouldn’t be able to fix the treasures created by them until he acquired their techniques.

“Then do you have any suggestions?” Yuan then asked.

“Who knows? Maybe I do, maybe I don’t. I’ll consider it if you ask me nicely.”

Yuan sighed, “If I had known you would be this useless, I wouldn’t have agreed to letting you follow me.”

Shiva frowned at his obvious provocation and said, “What an odd way of asking nicely.”

Yuan silently shrugged.

After a moment of silence, Shiva spoke, “If you do something for me, I’ll not only tell you how to restore that toy, but also teach you how we Eternals craft treasures.”

“What are you scheming?” Yuan narrowed his eyes at her, clearly suspicious of whatever she was thinking.

“I know of an Eternal who was sealed in this world even before me, and he’s very proficient when it comes to crafting. If you help me unseal him, I’ll persuade him to teach you his techniques.”

“Interesting,” said Yuan. “Can you tell me your relationship with this Eternal?”

“…”

“He’s something akin to a mentor to me.”

“Do you know where he’s being sealed?”

“I do.”

Yuan pondered her offer for a moment before responding, “Let me speak with him first. If I deem him to be a threat, I won’t unseal him.”

“Fine by me.” Shiva nodded. “I’ll give you more details after we leave this place.”

“Sure.”

Yuan returned to talking about the Primordial Realm with the Asura Clan shortly after.

“At the end of the day, as long as the Celestial Emperor is our enemy and the people’s exile brand remains, they won’t be able to live peacefully even if they leave this world, so we can only wait until after we deal with these two issues,” said Yuan.

Xiao Cangming nodded, “There are also a lot of other things that we must consider.”

Some time later, Yuan asked them, “Now then, are you all ready to leave the Primordial Realm?”

“I have been waiting for this moment since I was born,” said Xiao Chen, his face full of excitement.

Yuan nodded.

“Then we’ll leave in a week. If you need more time to pack your things, let me know.”

Yuan retrieved a handful of spatial rings and placed them on the table.

“You can use these.”

“Thank you.”

Once everyone in the Asura Clan had a spatial ring, they left to pack their belongings.

However, Xiao Hua stayed behind since she had nothing to pack.

“Yuan, I have a question,” Xiao Hua suddenly spoke to him.

“What is it?”

“Once we leave the Primordial Realm… will I still be able to follow you? Or am I going to have to stay with my family?” she asked.

chapters.title: Chapter 2230 Leaving The Primordial Realm
chapters.body:
Chapter 2230 Leaving The Primordial Realm

Yuan smiled and responded to Xiao Hua’s question, “That will depend on you. If you want to continue following me, I have no reason to refuse. Of course, if you wish to stay with your family, I will also respect that decision.”

“I wish to continue following you!” She immediately gave her answer.

He nodded with a warm smile.

“What about me?” Lingyue suddenly asked.

“Of course, the same goes for you. What do you want to do?”

“I wouldn’t know what else to do if I were not by your side, Brother Yuan. If it’s possible, I would like to continue to follow you.”

“Then it’s decided.”

Moments later, Lingyue entered Yuan’s Dantian to rest.

“Wow, it’s so different in here,” Lingyue remarked after seeing the Spirit Tree.

“What do you think about it?” Yuan asked.

“It’s very comfortable in here because of the abundance of spiritual energy, and with the Spirit Tree constantly producing more spiritual energy, I can feel my cultivation improving just by being in here.”

“That’s good to hear.”

Inside his Dantian, Lingyue faced Feng Yuxiang and Lan Yingying.

Lingyue offered them a sheepish smile as she spoke, “Um… I know I’m not Xiao Hua, but I still have all her memories. Still, I don’t expect you to treat me the same as before, so—”

“What are you talking about?” Feng Yuxiang cut her off. “Xiao Hua or not, you were essentially the same person back then. Our bond with her is the same bond we share with you.”

Lan Yingying nodded with a gentle smile, “She’s right, so don’t suddenly start acting like we’re strangers.”

“Thank you, Senior Feng, Senior Lan…” Lingyue spoke with teary eyes.

“That’s not right. You can call me Senior Sister or Feng Feng.”

“You can also call me Sister Yingying.”

“Un. Senior Sister, Sister Yingying.”

Feng Yuxiang then spoke, “Also, I didn’t get to say this back then, but thank you for stopping Xiao Hua.”

“Please don’t mention it. I only did what I had to in order to protect Brother Yuan.”

“Still, you did what I couldn’t.” Feng Yuxiang sighed in a dejected manner.

However, she didn’t stay dispirited for long, and she said, “With that being said, I am still the Young Master’s number one servant.”

“Of course.” Lingyue nodded with a bright smile.

Meanwhile, Xiao Hua said to Yuan, “Since we have some time, there’s something I would like to do with you.”

“What is it?”

Xiao Hua retrieved a book from her spatial ring and said, “I would like to read you this book.”

A nostalgic smile immediately appeared on Yuan’s face when he saw the book in Xiao Hua’s grasp. The latest_epɪ_sodes are on_the NovєlFіre.net

“Sure.”

The two of them left the Asura Clan’s residence and took a seat under a nearby tree.

Some time later, Xiao Hua opened the book and began reading its contents.

Yuan listened with closed eyes.

<Your comprehension of Heaven’s Secret Art has improved significantly>

<Heaven’s Secret Arts Mastery Level has increased to level 5>

<Your comprehension of Heaven’s Secret Art has improved significantly>

<Heaven’s Secret Arts Mastery Level has increased to level 6>

“…”

A few hours later.

<Heaven’s Secret Arts Mastery Level has reached max level>

“I know you already fully comprehended this technique in the past, but I still wanted to do this,” Xiao Hua said as she closed the book.

Since Heaven’s Secret Arts had been with the Asura Clan even before they were sealed inside the Primordial Realm, Tian Xian had already learned and mastered it in the past.

Yuan smiled and said, “It may not be the strongest technique out there, but it is definitely one of the most unique.” “…”

“Yuan, before we leave the Primordial Realm, there is something I must tell you,” Xiao Hua suddenly said. “Although my soul has accepted this vessel, and there are no compatibility issues, my soul right now is greatly weakened, so even though I am at the peak of God Ascension, I won’t be able to use my full power until I recover enough.”

“Don’t worry, I won’t ask you to fight.”

“That’s not—”

“I know,” Yuan interrupted. “But even if you were in perfect condition and had the cultivation of a Cultivation God, I still wouldn’t want you to fight for me. At the end of the day, you’re a precious comrade, and unless absolutely necessary or we’re just messing around, I don’t want to put you in harm’s way.”

“Also, we have another expert at the peak of God Ascension with us, so if I ever encounter a threat I cannot handle, we still have her.”

Xiao Hua quietly nodded.

Time flew by, and in the blink of an eye, a week had passed.

The moment had finally come for Yuan and the Asura Clan to depart from the Primordial Realm.

“Are you all ready to leave?” Yuan asked them.

“We are,” said Xiao Cangming.

Without another word, Yuan retrieved the Ancient Key and used it to open a portal before them that would lead them straight outside.

“Then, I’ll see you on the outside,” Yuan said before entering the portal, disappearing from the Primordial Realm.

Mu Xuelian and Xiao Hua went afterward, followed by Xiao Chen, Li Zhenwu, the servants, and finally, Xiao Cangming.

Once outside, Yuan expected to find Dong Ye and the others waiting for him. However, the sight that greeted him was entirely different.

Dong Ye and the rest of the Shadow Army were still there, but they were locked in fierce battle against a vast force of cultivators. Over a thousand soldiers clad in armor filled the battlefield, each one emanating the aura of the God Ascension realm.

‘Those guys! Heaven’s Mandate! Did the Celestial Emperor anticipate my plan to enter the Primordial Realm and set up an ambush here?’ Yuan pondered with a deep frown on his face.

“My liege! Thank the lord! You’re out just in time!” Dong Ye’s voice suddenly resounded through voice transmission.

“What’s the situation?!” Yuan asked.

“As you can see, the Celestial Emperor’s dogs are here. A surprise inspection took place a few days ago, and although we were prepared for it, something unforeseen occurred, messing everything up! Long story short, the Celestial Emperor is now aware of the Primordial Realm being opened!”

chapters.title: Chapter 2231 Outside The Primordial Realm
chapters.body:
Chapter 2231 Outside The Primordial Realm

“What is going on out here? Why are we suddenly in the middle of a battlefield?” Xiao Chen questioned with a dumbfounded look on his face after coming out of the Primordial Realm, the excitement on his face quickly replaced by confusion.

“Their armor… They’re the Celestial Emperor’s lapdogs, the Heaven’s Mandate!” Xiao Meilin’s eyes flickered with killing intent after seeing their familiar-looking armor.

Despite being in the Primordial Realm for tens of millions of years, she’d never forget the Heaven’s Mandate.

“My lord—Yuan, what is the situation?” Xiao Cangming asked him.

“According to Dong Ye, there was a surprise inspection, which alerted the Celestial Emperor of our activity,” he gave a brief explanation.

Xiao Cangming narrowed his eyes.

“So, can we kill them all?”

Yuan pondered for a moment before responding, “Go help out Dong Ye and the Shadow Army, but be prepared to leave at any moment. Since the Celestial Emperor is aware of our activity, there’s a good chance more reinforcements will appear. We’re not prepared to fight the Celestial Emperor and his army right now.”

Without another word, Xiao Cangming and the Asura Clan joined the battle.

Of course, Heaven’s Mandate had been aware of their presence since they left the Primordial Realm. “Exiles have escaped the Primordial Realm! Kill them all!” The commander of the army shouted and commanded over a hundred God Ascension experts to deal with them.

Soon, the Asura Clan and Heaven’s Mandate clashed.

“Hahaha! I never thought I would get to fight with the Celestial Emperor’s dogs so soon after leaving the Primordial Realm! How refreshing!” Xiao Meilin laughed excitedly as her cultivation exploded outward.

“Be careful! That exile is at the peak of God Ascension!”

“What?! How did she reach such a level within the Primordial Realm?! I thought there was no spiritual energy in that world!”

The soldiers were both bewildered and puzzled by the Asura Clan’s unexpected prowess.

Most of the soldiers there were between the third level and the fifth level of God Ascension, so they were all powerless before Xiao Meilin.

“God of War’s Astral Arts!”

Despite his lack of cultivation, Xiao Cangming was still an unstoppable force on the battlefield with his martial techniques.

Yuan didn’t immediately join the battle and continued speaking with Dong Ye.

“My liege, we’re only staying here to fight because you were still inside the Primordial Realm. Now that you’re out, we should leave this place as soon as possible,” said Dong Ye.

“Not only will there be more reinforcements later, but there’s a small chance the Celestial Emperor might even show up himself.”

“I agree. I’ll distract them while you withdraw with the Asura Clan.”

“You will? How are you going to do that?”

“I have my ways. However, there are a little too many people for me to do it right now, so I’ll start once we cut down their number some more.”

“I understand.”

“Time to warm up,” Yuan mumbled to himself when he noticed several presences approaching him.

When he turned to look at them, he could see four soldiers soaring directly at him, and the strongest amongst them was only at the third level of God Ascension.

“Let me deal with them for you,” Xiao Hua suddenly said.

“No, you should save your energy and focus on recovering your soul. I can handle them without breaking a sweat.”

“Okay…”

Without another word, Xiao Hua entered his Dantian.

Shortly after, the soldiers arrived and surrounded Yuan.

“You’re not an exile. Who the hell are you?”

The soldiers questioned him when they couldn’t sense the aura of an exile on him.

“I’m the one who went inside the Primordial Realm and freed those people,” Yuan calmly responded.

“A mere Immortal like you? You have no idea what you got yourself into!”

“Let’s not kill him and capture him instead. We’ll need to question him,” one of them said.

“Good idea.”

Yuan chuckled and said, “I’d like to see you try.”

The very next moment, Yuan retrieved Number One Under Heaven and struck first, attacking the strongest amongst them.

Despite his sudden attack, none of the soldiers seemed concerned. If anything, they sneered in disdain.

“How foolish.”

The soldier targeted by Yuan muttered as he raised his weapon to block the incoming attack.

However, Yuan’s aura suddenly exploded right before he struck.

Not only did he activate True Dragon Awakening, but he also enhanced his prowess with Chaotic Essence.

Before they left the Primordial Realm and while the Asura Clan prepared for their departure, Yuan approached Xiao Cangming to see if he had any Chaos Coins for him to consume.

As the most influential and powerful family in the Primordial Realm, the Asura Clan was naturally unfathomably rich. Although most of their wealth was made up of the Inferno Alchemists’ currency, they also held currencies from the other two powerhouses.

In the end, Yuan was able to acquire around a hundred billion small Chaos Coins, ten times the amount he attained from selling the spirit stone. And with that amount of Chaos Coins, he was able to advance his second cultivation to the peak of Chaotic Immortal, which was equivalent to a True Immortal who was on the verge of entering God Ascension realm. However, for Yuan, in terms of prowess, he could easily rival a third-level God Ascension cultivator if he used it to enhance his strength.

“W-What the hell is that ridiculous power?! Is he really a True Immortal?!” The soldiers cried out in shock after witnessing his overwhelming prowess.

As for the soldier targeted by Yuan, he had died in a single strike because he had underestimated Yuan and failed to block his attack.

After killing the first soldier, Yuan immediately turned his attention to the next closest one.

Seeing this, the other two soldiers quickly reacted and went to help their comrade.

However, not only were they unable to save their comrade, but they also died at the hands of Yuan in a single strike. ɪꜰ ʏᴏᴜ ᴡᴀɴᴛ ᴛᴏ ʀᴇᴀᴅ ᴍᴏʀᴇ ᴄʜᴀᴘᴛᴇʀs, ᴘʟᴇᴀsᴇ ᴠɪsɪᴛ novel★fire.net

chapters.title: Chapter 2232 An Otherworldly Presence
chapters.body:
Chapter 2232 An Otherworldly Presence

After defeating the four soldiers, Yuan immediately went to assist the others.

“Fuck! Who the hell are these bastards?!” ᴜᴘᴅᴀᴛᴇ ꜰʀᴏᴍ novel·fire.net

The commander of the soldiers trembled in anger after witnessing how badly they were losing to the Asura Clan.

“Wait a second! That technique he just used! Why does it look so familiar?!”

Although he didn’t realize it at first, the commander slowly started to recognize the Asura Clan’s martial techniques. “Their overwhelming killing intent… those techniques… don’t tell me—! The Asura Clan?!”

Even though he had never fought against the Asura Clan or seen their techniques before, the commander was almost certain of their identity.

Due to the Asura Clan’s immense reputation and their pivotal role in the Heavenly War of Immortals and Gods, their legacy remained ingrained in history and taught to every new soldier of Heaven’s Mandate, even to this very day.

Even so, the commander could hardly believe it.

“The Asura Clan—second only to the Evil God in their contributions in their war against the first Celestial Emperor—has escaped the Primordial Realm?! No! I cannot allow them to leave alive!” the commander barked, clenching his fists.

He immediately retrieved a communication jade slip and requested more reinforcements even though he’d already done so earlier.

“Send the reinforcements immediately, and send as much as you can! The Asura Clan has escaped from the Primordial Realm!”

The receiver of the commander’s transmission jolted in shock upon hearing the message.

“The Asura Clan?! I must notify His Majesty, the Celestial Emperor, immediately!” he exclaimed, his voice trembling with disbelief.

Without even the slightest delay, the man rushed to find the Celestial Emperor. However, he was stopped at the Celestial Palace’s front gate.

“What do you want now, Messenger Huang?” The guard asked the messenger.

“This is about the Primordial Realm! I must speak with the Celestial Emperor as soon as possible! It’s an emergency!”

“The Primordial Realm? The reinforcements are already on their way.”

“No! You don’t understand! I just received news from Commander Lee that some exiles have just escaped from the Primordial Realm, and they’re the Asura Clan!”

“Asura Clan…?” The name didn’t immediately register in the guard’s mind.

“The Asura Clan?!” However, once he realized the severity of the issue, he immediately opened the gates to allow the messenger through.

Some time later, the messenger reached the Celestial Emperor’s room. But he was stopped at the door again.

“Messenger Huang? What are you doing here?”

The messenger immediately repeated what he said to the guard outside.

“The Asura Clan, you say?!”

The guard quickly retrieved a communication jade slip and relayed the information to the other holder.

Moments later, the door behind him started to open, and a servant walked out.

“His Majesty, the Celestial Emperor, is already aware of the Asura Clan’s situation. You may return.”

Without waiting for a response, the servant closed the doors.

“The Celestial Emperor is already aware? Did he predict this would happen?” The messenger mumbled to himself before leaving the Celestial Palace shortly after.

Meanwhile, the Celestial Emperor stood calmly before a massive device, staring at the slumbering figures shown within it.

“The Asura Clan… the ones who fought beside the Evil God, Tian Xian, against the first Celestial Emperor, huh?” he muttered with a faint smirk. “They may have been formidable once, but now they’re nothing more than ancient relics—hardly worth mentioning.”

Suddenly, his entire body stiffened as he caught sight of movement from one of the slumbering figures. What began as faint, almost imperceptible twitches soon grew more pronounced, rippling through the stillness of the void.

A wide smile appeared on the Celestial Emperor’s face as he rejoiced, “At last!” Meanwhile, outside the Primordial Realm, Yuan and the Asura Clan had cut down more than half of Heaven’s Mandate’s forces.

“Alright, all of you, prepare to leave.” Yuan sent a voice transmission to everyone from the Asura Clan and the Shadow Army. Yuan suddenly stopped moving and started gathering his spiritual energy, even using his Eternal Essence.

A few moments later, his aura exploded forward, enveloping everyone there.

“What is he trying to do?!”

The soldiers of Heaven’s Mandate were instantly alerted and turned their attention to him.

“Heaven’s Seventh Art — Heaven’s Grand Snare!”

In the next instant, hundreds of golden chains materialized around Yuan and lashed outward toward the soldiers, as if seeking to ensnare them.

The soldiers hastily launched counterattacks, but to their horror, their spiritual energy scattered the moment it touched the chains, rendering their techniques utterly useless.

As for those who struck using their weapons, not even Mythic-grade treasures were able to put a mark on the chains, almost as if they were indestructible.

Some tried to flee, but the golden chains moved at the speed of light, outspeeding them with ease.

“What the fuck is this?!” The soldiers cried out loud as the golden chains wrapped around their bodies, restricting their movements while sealing their cultivation at the same time.

“Now! Leave!” Yuan commanded them to leave once he had captured nearly every single soldier there.

Without hesitation, Dong Ye retrieved a treasure and immediately created an escape portal.

“Over here!” Seeing the portal, the Shadow Army and the Asura Clan quickly rushed toward it.

While the others escaped, Yuan focused on maintaining the technique. Since he had to use the technique on hundreds of God Ascension cultivators, his spiritual energy was being drained at a drastic rate.

Yet, with his powerful cultivation speed, Yuan was able to restore his spiritual energy just fast enough to barely keep up with the demand.

“My liege, everyone has safely escaped!”

Hearing Dong Ye’s words, Yuan deactivated the technique and rushed toward the portal, as he was unable to move while using the technique.

However, right before reaching the portal, Yuan suddenly stopped moving.

“My liege?! What are you waiting for?! The portal will close soon!” Dong Ye exclaimed.

Yuan gritted his teeth and responded with voice transmission, “Something is restricting my movements!”

“What?!”

Before Yuan could say another word, an unfathomable presence suddenly descended, enveloping the space around him with an otherworldly presence.

“This presence… an Eternal!”

Yuan’s brows furrowed into a frown after recognizing the identity of the presence.

chapters.title: Chapter 2233 The Appearance Of An Eternal
chapters.body:
Chapter 2233 The Appearance Of An Eternal

“Dong Ye, listen to me carefully. Close the portal immediately. An Eternal is coming,” Yuan said.

“What?! What about you, my liege?!”

“I cannot move my body right now, let alone leave. It’s clear that this Eternal has business with me, and that it won’t let me leave.”

“Are you telling me to leave you to face a God alone?! Impossible! I will return to assist you!” Dong Ye exclaimed.

“Dong Ye, are you disobeying me right now?” Yuan spoke in a cold tone.

“That’s not—”

“Even if you are here with me, what can you achieve? Can you fight a God?” Yuan interrupted in a cold tone. “No… I cannot…”

As much as he hated to admit it, Dong Ye stood no chance against an Eternal and would only be a burden.

A calm smile appeared on Yuan’s face as he spoke, “Have you forgotten who I am? This isn’t my first time dealing with them. I’ll return once I deal with the Eternal.” “I understand. Please, be safe, my liege.”

The portal started closing the very next moment, leaving Yuan stranded in the Primordial Chaos with the remaining soldiers of Heaven’s Mandate and an incoming Eternal.

“Where is Yuan? Why did you close the portal?” Xiao Cangming demanded after seeing the portal close, his expression darkening into a deep frown as killing intent flickered in his sharp gaze.

“I swear… If you’d betrayed us…”

Xiao Meilin and the others subconsciously reached for their weapons.

“Don’t you dare mock me! Even if it’s you, I won’t stand for such slander!” Dong Ye snapped back, his voice full of anger.

“Then why the hell did you—”

“It was his liege who commanded me to close the portal! He was halted by a God before he could enter the portal!” “What?! An Eternal appeared?!” Xiao Cangming’s eyes widened with disbelief. “Then we need to go back and—”

“And do what?! We may be powerful, but we’re nothing but ants in the eyes of these Gods! Only his liege has the power to face them!”

Xiao Cangming fell silent at Dong Ye’s words, the tone of defeat in his voice leaving no room for response.

“We’d only get in his way if we were there,” Dong Ye continued with a deep sigh. “The only thing we can do now is wait for his return.” The source of this content ɪs N0veI.Fiɾe.net

“Hey, let me go.”

A voice suddenly resounded.

“What?” Dong Ye turned to look at Mu Xuelian, who’d just spoken. “Did you not hear what I just—”

“You may not be able to handle an Eternal, but don’t compare me to you mortals.”

“What the hell are you…?”

“There is an Eternal residing in her body,” said Xiao Cangming.

“What?!” Dong Ye jumped in a startled manner.

After snapping out of his daze, Dong Ye spoke, “In that case, you might be able to help my liege!”

“Help? You’re misunderstanding something. I won’t be helping him.”

Dong Ye frowned at her words, but before he could say anything, Mu Xuelian, now controlled by Shiva, continued, “I am an observer—his observer, so I want to witness his fight with the Eternal. That brat dared to claim that he could put Eternals to sleep, so I want to witness it myself.”

“With that being said, if he begs me to save his life, I might consider it.”

Dong Ye gritted his teeth when he saw Shiva’s sadistic expression.

“What are you waiting for? Hurry up. I don’t want to miss the show,” she urged him.

“Fuck! Whatever!” Dong Ye activated the treasure, creating a portal that led back to Yuan.

Without hesitation, Shiva walked into the portal,

“Are you sure that was a wise move?” Xiao Meilin asked.

“What else was I supposed to do? If she’s really a God, nobody here can defy her. Also, despite what she said, I doubt she’d really let him die.”

Xiao Cangming nodded in agreement, “I don’t know why she’s following Yuan, but I’m sure she won’t let him die so easily.”

Meanwhile, Yuan let out a deep sigh as the portal closed before him. He then shifted his gaze toward the soldiers who had taken the opportunity to surround him while his movements were restricted.

“I don’t know why you didn’t escape while you had the chance, but you won’t be going anywhere now,” said the commander.

Yuan smiled and calmly responded, “If you guys value your lives, you should leave while you still can.”

“Still acting tough? I don’t know what martial technique you used to restrict our movements, but I bet it’d exhausted your cultivation, and I doubt you will be able to do it again. Maybe it’s the reason why you cannot move right now.”

Suddenly, the space before Yuan twisted.

“What the?” Yuan’s eyes widened as the portal that had closed moments ago reopened, and a beautiful figure emerged from it.

“Mu Xuelian…? No… Shiva.” Yuan mumbled after seeing her beautiful face. “What are you doing, coming back here?”

“Did you really think I would give up the opportunity to watch you clash against an Eternal?” Shiva responded as the portal closed behind her. “Don’t worry, I won’t ruin your fun, as I am only here as a spectator.”

“How twisted.” Yuan sighed.

“I don’t know what the hell is going on, but—” The commander began, only to be cut off as an unfathomable pressure suddenly descended, crushing the air around him.

“W-What the hell is this pressure?!” he exclaimed in a terrified voice, his body trembling uncontrollably. It wasn’t just him. Every single soldier there was shaking like they were naked in a blizzard.

“Speaking of the devil…” Shiva mumbled with a calm smile on her face.

“You had your chance to leave, but alas…” Yuan sighed.

“Run! Get away from here as soon as possible!” The commander roared, shouting at his soldiers.

Yet, none of the soldiers left, as they were all frozen in fear.

As the pressure intensified, agonized screams resounded. One by one, the soldiers’ bodies began to swell like overfilled balloons until they burst apart in a gruesome chain reaction, starting with those of the weakest cultivation.

chapters.title: Chapter 2234 Fighting An Eternal
chapters.body:
Chapter 2234 Fighting An Eternal

“What the hell is going on?! What did you do to us?!” The commander shouted at Yuan for answers, thinking he was responsible for the situation.

Yuan calmly replied, “I didn’t do anything. Hell, I was even nice enough to give you a warning. Unfortunately, you didn’t heed it, and as a result, you’ll die.”

In mere seconds, all of the soldiers perished under the pressure.

“AHHHH!” The commander—stronger than the rest—managed to endure the crushing pressure for a few moments longer before unleashing a final, agonized scream. Moments later, his body erupted violently, and his soul was instantly annihilated, leaving him no chance to escape.

Though the soldiers of Heaven’s Mandate had been annihilated, Yuan couldn’t afford to relax, as the true threat was rapidly approaching.

Moments later, a massive, ethereal figure materialized before Yuan. It had no physical form, its ghostly body wavered like mist, yet it radiated an overwhelming aura of malice that seemed to corrode the very space around it.

Of course, it was no ordinary aura, as it was formed from Eternal Essence, and the sheer concentration of it far surpassed anything Yuan had attained thus far.

“Did you really have to kill them? Aren’t they on your side?” Yuan asked.

“What nonsense are you uttering?” The Eternal responded in a coarse voice.

Yuan raised an eyebrow at the unexpected response.

“The Celestial Emperor didn’t send you here?”

“The Celestial Emperor…?” The Eternal repeated in a puzzled voice. “Have you already forgotten about this Mighty Saaruk? Even though you were acting so boldly not too long ago?”

“You’re Saaruk?!” Yuan exclaimed after realizing the Eternal’s identity.

“I didn’t think you’d come after me so soon. Are you really that impatient?”

“Hahaha! Are you trembling in fear? Do you regret opposing this Mighty Saaruk?!”

After a moment of silence, Yuan responded, “To be honest, I was expecting more from an Eternal ranked in the top 20.” “Hmph!” Saaruk sneered. “Because we’re still within the Absolute Power’s influence, I can appear only in this ethereal form, and while in this form, I can wield barely ten percent of my true strength.”

Saaruk had originally planned to wait until Yuan left the Absolute Power’s domain before making good on his threat, but impatience won out, so he arrived ahead of time at the cost of this inconvenience.

“Even though I am only wielding 10 percent of my true prowess, it’s more than enough to handle an ant like you,” Saaruk mocked.

“We’ll see about that,” Yuan replied. Read full story at novel(ꜰ)ire.net

“There’s nothing to see when you can’t even break out of such a simple restraint.”

Saaruk pointed at Yuan, who still had his movements restricted by an invisible force.

“You mean this little trick?”

Yuan’s body suddenly erupted with Eternal Essence, and within moments, he shattered the restraints binding him.

Saaruk didn’t react to Yuan breaking free. Instead, his gaze shifted toward Shiva, who hovered calmly nearby, watching the scene unfold with the detached air of a spectator.

“How unexpected,” Saaruk said, his tone laced with disdain. “I hadn’t anticipated encountering another Eternal here, much less someone like you, Shiva. When did you regain your freedom?”

“Very recently,” she calmly replied.

“Who helped you break out of the seal? Don’t tell me… this ant?” “…”

Shiva didn’t respond.

“Hahaha!” Saaruk burst into laughter, his voice echoing like thunder. “Truly hilarious! To think an Eternal would stoop so low as to seek help from a mere mortal! Did you lose your pride during the time you were sealed?”

“I can’t wait for Ishrak to hear about this! I wonder how he’ll react to you breaking out of his seal. Maybe he’ll track you down to seal you again.”

Shiva’s eyes flickered with immense killing intent after hearing the name Ishrak.

“How dare you speak his name in my presence? I’d love to make you pay for it, but I’ll let him deal with you today,” she growled.

“You will make this Mighty Saaruk pay?” Saaruk started laughing again. “With what? That mortal body? You won’t be able to defeat this Mighty Saaruk even if you had your eternal body, much less in that trash!”

Enraged by Saaruk’s provocation, Shiva erupted in a burst of Eternal Essence. The surge was overwhelming, and Mu Xuelian, merely a mortal, could not withstand it and started bleeding from her orifices.

“Hey, what the hell are you doing? Have you forgotten that body doesn’t belong to you? Or are you trying to kill Mu Xuelian?” Yuan frowned after seeing this.

“If you’re just going to observe, do it properly.”

“…”

Upon hearing Yuan’s words, Shiva quickly calmed down and immediately withdrew her Eternal Essence before starting to heal Mu Xuelian’s injuries.

“To think you’d shut Shiva up with so little effort. Not bad, mortal,” Saaruk remarked with a mocking smile. “Are you done talking? If so, let’s get this over with. I have other business to attend to.”

“Do you really want to die so quickly? Unfortunately for you, this Mighty Saaruk has no plan on letting you die a swift death. It’ll be a slow and painful one.”

Without another word, Yuan activated True Dragon Awakening and retrieved Number One Under Heaven before making the first move.

However, wary of Saaruk and his unknown power, Yuan kept his distance and attacked using Supreme Sword Aura.

Saaruk didn’t move an inch, showing no intent to defend himself. When the Supreme Sword Aura struck him, it fractured instantly—shattering like glass against an invisible wall—leaving Saaruk completely unharmed.

“Using a mortal’s power on this Mighty Saaruk… You’re nothing but talk.” Saaruk shook his head in disappointment while a thick layer of Eternal Essence shrouded his entire form.

Of course, having fought Eternals before, Yuan was well aware that only Eternal Essence would be able to harm them. However, he’d never fought an Eternal in their ethereal form, so he had to do some tests.

“Don’t just stand there now. Come. Keep entertaining this Mighty Saaruk. This Mighty Saaruk won’t attack until you realize that you are powerless before this Mighty Saaruk and fall into despair,” Saaruk beckoned Yuan, his voice full of arrogance.

chapters.title: Chapter 2235 Mighty Saaruk!
chapters.body:
Chapter 2235 Mighty Saaruk!

Despite Saaruk’s provocation, Yuan remained calm.

“Since you insist, I’ll continue doing just that,” he said as he continued trying different things.

From martial techniques to spiritual techniques such as True Primordial Fire, Heaven’s Final Judgement, and even Heaven’s Bane. Yet, they were all useless against Saaruk and his Eternal Essence. It was akin to trying to destroy a brick wall with nothing but raw eggs.

“You’re much more foolish than I thought, mortal ant,” Saaruk laughed loudly at Yuan’s futile efforts.

Even Shiva couldn’t help but feel agitated at Yuan.

“What the hell are you doing?! Are you seriously messing around before an Eternal?!” she yelled at him using voice transmission.

“Does it really look like I am messing around?” he asked.

“Yes, it does! Now start fighting him seriously, or you’ll really die!”

Yuan sighed inwardly..

The next moment, his aura shifted drastically, and Eternal Essence surged from his entire being.

Saaruk narrows his eyes at the sight.

“A mortal being using Eternal Essence, what an odd and unpleasant sight,” he muttered with a disgusted expression.

To Saaruk, watching Yuan wield something meant only for Eternals was like seeing a pig pretend to be human—not just distasteful, but downright offensive.

However, amidst his disdain, Saaruk was also astonished by Yuan’s achievement. He couldn’t fathom how a mere mortal could have learned to wield Eternal Essence, as it wasn’t something that could be taught.

After all, most humans would perish just from learning about the existence of Eternals, much less learning and wielding their powers.

He looked at Shiva and wondered if she had anything to do with it.

“The real fight will start now,” Yuan said.

Then, without warning, he launched his strike.

An overwhelming amount of Eternal Essence engulfed Number One Under Heaven, and it unleashed a devastating sword light that was made entirely out of Eternal Essence.

Yet, despite knowing that Yuan’s strike contained Eternal Essence and could potentially harm him, Saaruk didn’t lift even a finger, almost as if he still had no intention of blocking it.

In the next instant, Yuan’s Eternal Essence collided head-on with Saaruk. Unlike his earlier techniques, it didn’t shatter upon impact, but even so, it failed to break through Saaruk’s barrier.

Not only did it fail to break through, but it was even absorbed by Saaruk.

“So this is what Eternal Essence created by a mortal ant tastes like. As expected, it’s weak and disgusting.”

Saaruk narrowed his eyes at Yuan and spoke in a disinterested tone, “This won’t do. At this rate, forget about entertaining this Mighty Saaruk. You’ll bore this Mighty Saaruk to death instead.”

The very next moment, the Eternal Essence around Saaruk became violent, and his aura surged. Then, he vanished into the void.

Yuan became as still as a statue as he searched for Saaruk, but his divine sense picked up nothing.

Suddenly, a numbing sensation crawled up Yuan’s back, and he instinctively turned—only to find Saaruk standing behind him like a ghost, silent and menacing.

‘That was the Power of the Void!’ Yuan realized what Saaruk just did because of his own understanding of the Power of the Void.

“This Mighty Saaruk could’ve killed you a hundred times just now,” said Saaruk.

“Then why didn’t you?”

“Because this Mighty Saaruk has yet to torture you for daring to bare your fangs at a God. However, it will start now.”

Saaruk raised a single finger, forming a ‘small’ spear-like projectile with Eternal Essence before shooting it at Yuan. Saaruk’s ethereal form was vast—large enough to rival a small planet—so to Yuan, it truly felt as though he were an ant fighting against a giant.

When Yuan attempted to block Saaruk’s strike, he was immediately sent flying deeper into the void.

‘The difference is immense! And he can only use 10 percent of his full prowess?!’ Yuan cried inwardly.

Even without witnessing Saaruk’s full power, Yuan could tell that this Eternal far surpassed any he had faced before. The realization struck him hard—that the Eternals he’d once battled were nothing more than small fry in comparison.

“Do you understand the difference between us now, you insignificant mortal ant?” Saaruk mocked after seeing how far Yuan was thrown.

“…I’ll admit that you’re a little stronger than I anticipated. However, it doesn’t change anything, as I still defeat you in the end,” he said. Follow current novᴇls on n͟o͟v͟e͟l͟f͟i͟r͟e͟.net

“…”

Saaruk remained silent, but the displeasure in his gaze was as clear as day.

“A mere lowly mortal ant…” he suddenly mumbled. “I’ve had enough of your disrespect and arrogance.”

Without another word, Saaruk charged at Yuan, his entire figure engulfed with overwhelming Eternal Essence.

Yuan didn’t falter and charged straight into close-quarters combat with Saaruk. Despite channeling every ounce of Eternal Essence he could muster, he was steadily forced back, overwhelmed by Saaruk’s sheer might.

“…”

Shiva watched in silence. Even though Yuan was losing to Saaruk, she was still impressed by his prowess, as this was her first time witnessing a human fighting an Eternal.

‘No mortal has ever defied an Eternal before, much less directly clash with them,’ she sighed inwardly.

Yuan had been fighting Saaruk for an entire week at this point.

‘It’s a pity that he offended someone like Saaruk. Although our journey was short, it was enjoyable while it lasted.’

Without a word or warning, Shiva’s figure suddenly vanished into the void. In her eyes, Yuan’s defeat was inevitable, and once he fell to Saaruk, she would be targeted next.

“Lord Shiva, are we really going to leave him alone?” Mu Xuelian asked as they traversed using the Power of the Void.

“His fate is already sealed. There is no point staying around and waiting for our turn. It’s unfortunate, but there’s nothing I can do against another Eternal while I am in this condition.”

“What are we going to do now?” Mu Xuelian then asked.

“We’ll focus on strengthening your body and cultivation. Once prepared, we’ll start looking for my body.”

“I understand.”

Even if Mu Xuelian didn’t want to abandon Yuan, she had no choice in the matter. After all, Shiva could take control over her body at any time, and her orders were absolute.

Shortly after Shiva left, Saaruk noticed their disappearance.

“Hahaha! Shiva, that coward!” Saaruk roared with laughter. “To think she’d flee while her savior lies on the brink of death!”

His mocking voice echoed as he gazed at Yuan—bloodied, exhausted, and barely conscious.

Despite having powerful regeneration, Saaruk’s Eternal Essence seemed to have a property that weakened his regenerative abilities.

“Young Master! This monster is too powerful! I’ll distract him while you escape!” Feng Yuxiang’s voice suddenly responded in Yuan’s head.

“I will also help,” Xiao Hua then said.

“…”

Lan Yingying and Lingyue also wanted to help, but they were too weak to even do anything.

“No, I won’t allow that,” Yuan immediately refused.

“Then what do you suggest?! You’ll die to that monster at this rate!” Feng Yuxiang exclaimed.

“Just a little more…” Yuan responded.

“What?”

“Just give me a little more time.”

Without explaining further, Yuan temporarily sealed off his connection with them, so their voices could no longer reach him.

“Young Master?!” Feng Yuxiang was left baffled and confused.

“Let’s just believe in him,” Lan Yingying suddenly said. “I don’t think Yuan has any plans on dying here, especially not when we’re still with him.”

“I want to believe him, but I have never seen the Young Master in such a bad state before,” Feng Yuxiang sighed.

“What’s wrong, mortal ant? You’ve gotten quiet.” Saaruk spoke.

Yuan smiled and said, “I don’t blame Shiva for leaving. There was nothing she could do, anyway.”

“That’s because she’s weak. If she were even half as strong as this Mighty Saaruk, she wouldn’t have been sealed away in the first place.”

“Anyway, this Mighty Saaruk has wasted enough time on you. You should feel honored to have fought and died at the hands of this Mighty Saaruk.”

“What’s the rush? I’m not done yet.” Yuan said as his weakened aura suddenly started surging again.

However, Saaruk didn’t even bat an eye and said, “No, you’re done. The only reason you even lasted this long is because this Mighty Saaruk allowed you. Moreover, your Eternal Essence is too weak to do anything.”

“If it’s too weak, then I just need to strengthen it.”

“Strengthen it? Hahaha! You’re just a mortal ant! Regardless of how you learned how to wield Eternal Essence, only true Eternals can strengthen it!”

Saaruk was unaware of Yuan having a true Eternal Constitution, hence why he doesn’t believe Yuan could strengthen his Eternal Essence.

Without a word, Yuan suddenly closed his eyes and took a deep breath.

“It took some time, but I can finally feel the ‘essence’ of the void,” he mumbled a second later.

“What…?” Saaruk’s eyes widened at his words.

Chapter 2236 Essence Of The Void

When Yuan started attempting to refine the essence of the void, Saaruk didn’t intervene. In fact, he even watched with intrigue, almost as if he wanted to see the results.

‘A mortal ant wants to absorb the essence of the void? How laughable! Even Eternals with a true Eternal Constitution struggle to do so!’ Saaruk sneered inwardly.

Unlike spiritual energy or any other known force, the void was something entirely different. Though it enveloped most of the universe, even experts with millions of years of cultivation failed to comprehend it, let alone wield it. For that reason, the Power of the Void was regarded as the most difficult and enigmatic path in all of cultivation.

This held true even for the Eternals, gods of the universe. While their boundless experience and knowledge allowed them to comprehend the Power of the Void, refining its essence was an entirely different matter altogether.

Because of this, Saaruk had been utterly shocked when Yuan claimed he could sense the essence of the void. And when Yuan went so far as to attempt refining it, Saaruk couldn’t resist witnessing it himself—to see whether a mere mortal could truly accomplish the impossible despite already knowing the answer.

‘There’s no way he’ll be able to refine the essence of the void. The moment he attempts to do so and fails, his entire being will be erased from existence. Although this means this Mighty Saaruk won’t be able to kill the mortal ant with his own hands, it’ll be worth it!’ Saaruk laughed inwardly.

While Saaruk watched with excitement and anticipation of his gruesome death, Yuan calmly refined the essence of the void.

‘He’s not doing anything to stop me? So he must be confident that I would fail or that I still wouldn’t be able to defeat him…’ Yuan thought to himself after seeing Saaruk’s reaction.

‘Whatever his reason is, this is beneficial for me, so there’s no reason for me to stop here.’

Without a moment’s hesitation, Yuan started refining the essence of the void that existed in every direction and even further than he could perceive.

Even though Saaruk was in an ethereal form, Yuan could tell that he was trembling in anger after having his attack blocked. “This is your second time underestimating a human,” said Yuan. “It will also be your second time being defeated by one.”

Yuan’s aura erupted, his Eternal Essence surging outward like a tidal wave that crashed violently against Saaruk. For origınal chapters go to ⓝovelFire.net

“If you think you can defeat this Mighty Saaruk just because you refined some essence of the void, you’re greatly mistaken! This Mighty Saaruk has been refining the essence of the void for countless years!”

“Had you fought with your full strength, I wouldn’t have stood a chance, but that’s not the case, is it?” Yuan remarked with a calm smile.

Even as they clashed, Yuan continued refining the essence of the void, his Eternal Essence steadily evolving—growing denser, sharper, and gradually surpassing Saaruk’s own.

Eventually, Yuan’s Eternal Essence grew strong enough to overwhelm Saaruk, allowing him to reach Saaruk’s ethereal form.

“Argh!” Saaruk roared in agony as Yuan’s strike pierced him, inflicting pain upon him for the first time.

“To think a mere mortal ant would inflict pain upon this Mighty Saaruk! This Mighty Saaruk will never forget this humiliation!”

chapters.title: Chapter 2237 Enraged Saaruk
chapters.body:
Chapter 2237 Enraged Saaruk

Realizing he could no longer overpower Yuan, Saaruk abruptly withdrew, putting distance between them, as if he’d lost all intention of continuing the fight.

“Where the hell do you think you’re going?!” Yuan shouted as Saaruk pulled farther away. “Don’t tell me you’re running! A god fleeing from a mere mortal?! That’s hilarious!”

Yuan didn’t bother giving chase. He knew that if Saaruk truly intended to flee, there was no way he could catch him.

“Running away? This Mighty Saaruk?” Saaruk sneered in disdain at the thought of running away.

“Even if this Mighty Saaruk has to suffer a little, this Mighty Saaruk will make sure you die here today!”

Suddenly, Saaruk’s Eternal Essence skyrocketed, instantly surpassing Yuan’s Eternal Essence.

‘What the hell?! Don’t tell me he hasn’t been using his full strength this entire time?!’ Yuan exclaimed inwardly after seeing Saaruk’s aura suddenly become nearly twice as strong as before.

Unbeknownst to Yuan, Saaruk had been using his full strength before.

Although the Absolute Power’s restrictions bound the Eternals’ prowess within its vicinity, they could temporarily exceed those limits as long as they were at the edge of the power’s influence, where it was the weakest. Moreover, they had to pay a tremendous cost.

“Bear witness, mortal ant! This is Mighty Saaruk unleashing twenty percent of his true power!” he roared with manic laughter, hurling an attack that was twice as devastating as before.

“Twenty percent?! What nonsense is that?! What about the Absolute Power?!” Yuan exclaimed as he channeled as much Eternal Essence as he could muster to block the incoming strike.

“Argh!”

Moments after unleashing his attack, Saaruk suddenly screamed in agony, as though an unseen force were ravaging him, inflicting far greater pain than anything Yuan’s blows had caused.

Then, without waiting around to see the outcome, Saaruk immediately turned tail and fled, racing in a direction that took him farther and farther from the Nine Heavens until he was no longer within its vicinity.

Had he hesitated even a second longer, his ethereal form would have been annihilated by the unseen force. Though it wouldn’t have killed him outright, the damage would have weakened his Eternal Essence, requiring trillions of years to recover.

However, that wasn’t to say Saaruk escaped unscathed. By daring to defy the Absolute Power and drawing upon strength beyond what was permitted, he’d forfeited roughly ten percent of his total strength. It was a heavy price, enough to drop his standing among the Eternals and cast him out of the top twenty.

After escaping and returning to his true body somewhere in the endless void, Saaruk unleashed a deafening roar of fury so powerful that it vaporized every star within hundreds of light-years, erasing entire constellations in an instant.

“THAT DAMN MORTAL ANT! HOW DARE HE HUMILIATE THIS MIGHTY SAARUK IN SUCH A MANNER?!”

Saaruk caused such a disturbance within the void that it attracted the attention of other nearby Eternals, and when they saw that he had weakened, they wasted no time questioning him.

“Saaruk, what happened to your Eternal Essence? It’s so much weaker than before!”

“Who did you fight this time?”

“Can you even call yourself mighty now that you’re this weak?” Of course, they also didn’t hesitate to mock him.

Saaruk ignored the other Eternals, refusing to entertain them. If they ever discovered that a mere mortal had reduced him to such a pitiful state, he would be mocked and scorned until the end of time.

Fortunately for him, Shiva had left before she witnessed everything, and he was sure that Yuan had perished from his final strike.

“…”

“What is this ruckus you’ve caused in my domain, Saaruk?”

Suddenly, another voice called out to him.

Saaruk’s body tensed up slightly after hearing this voice.

“No reason at all…” he responded, as the voice belonged to an Eternal that he couldn’t afford to ignore.

“So you destroyed all those resources for nothing? Is that what you’re telling me?” The voice returned, its tone colder than the void itself and dripping with restrained fury.

To the Eternals, all of existence was nothing more than a reservoir of resources, something to be refined into essence and consumed for power. Of course, this included the stars Saaruk had destroyed in his fury.

“Saaruk offers his apology…”

Not only did he lower his head in apology, but before this Eternal, Saaruk didn’t even dare refer to himself as ‘the Mighty Saaruk’, a title he proclaimed with pride before all others.

As for the other Eternals, they left the moment they noticed the presence of this Eternal.

After a silence that seemed to stretch across the stars, the voice returned, its tone no longer angry, but tinged with curiosity.

“Your condition… It was caused by the Absolute Power, wasn’t it?”

“T-That’s—!” Saaruk hesitated to answer.

“So, even you seek to covet that power for yourself now? Tell me—what for? To defy me?” the voice said, its tone cold and razor-sharp, like judgment itself.

“No! That’s not it! Saaruk swears upon his essence!”

“In that case, you won’t mind sharing with me your memories, will you?” Follow current novels on novęlfire.net

Saaruk fell silent. Of all beings in existence, this was the one he most dreaded discovering his humiliation. Unfortunately, he could not refuse, as it would result in humiliation far worse than what Yuan had inflicted upon him.

“Go ahead,” he said in defeat.

In the next moment, a light flickered in the far reaches of the void—first like a distant star, then rapidly expanding, its brilliance devouring the darkness itself.

Within moments, the star reached him, and as it got close enough to see, it revealed itself to be the end of a single glowing string. The moment it touched Saaruk, it latched onto his body like a divine tether.

Several minutes later, the voice returned, its tone laced with amusement and intrigue.

“A mortal who not only wields our power but could even refine the essence of the void? How fascinating… how utterly unprecedented!”

“…”

Saaruk was dumbfounded by the Eternal’s reaction; never before had he witnessed them display such excitement. However, he was also nervous, as he had already slain the very being that had stirred such a reaction.

chapters.title: Chapter 2238 Unknown Eternal
chapters.body:
Chapter 2238 Unknown Eternal

After reading all of Saaruk’s memories, the unknown Eternal withdrew its glowing string from his body and fell silent.

“It’s been so long since I last experienced excitement. I must speak with this mortal who stirred such an emotion within me,” said the unknown Eternal.

“!!!”

Saaruk’s eyes widened with disbelief at the unknown Eternal’s words.

“B-But Saaruk has already killed that mortal!”

The unknown Eternal responded, “You left before you could confirm the mortal’s death, didn’t you?”

“That’s correct, but there’s no way he could’ve survived twenty percent of Saaruk’s power! It’s simply impossible!”

Saaruk refused to believe that Yuan had survived his attack, especially after he’d sacrificed a portion of his own strength to ensure the mortal’s destruction.

“Impossible, you say? That mortal has already defied logic and done the impossible multiple times. Even if there’s only a slight chance that the mortal is alive, I’m willing to bet on that chance. After all, it’s not like there’s anything else for me to do right now.”

“…”

Saaruk was speechless.

“I was going to punish you for destroying my resources, but since you have made such an exciting discovery, I’ll forgive you this time,” said the unknown Eternal.

Saaruk sighed in relief after hearing this. There were very few things—enough to count with one hand—that Eternals feared in this universe, and this unknown Eternal was one of them.

As for the reason, it was pretty simple. This unknown Eternal possessed such overwhelming strength that it could instill fear in other Eternals.

“Saaruk is grateful,” he said.

The unknown Eternal no longer spoke, almost as if they’d left. Sure enough, Saaruk could feel the unknown Eternal’s presence getting further and further away.

While one might assume that the unknown Eternal could reach Yuan’s location swiftly, in truth, it would take many years to traverse such an immense distance, even with its prowess and the Power of the Void. The only reason Saaruk had found Yuan so quickly was because he’d already been nearby, and the only reason he returned to his true body so fast was due to being in his ethereal form.

As for the unknown Eternal, Saaruk could tell that they were traveling with their true body.

Meanwhile, after Saaruk had left, Yuan poured all of his power into defending against Saaruk’s attack, which contained 20 percent of his true prowess.

Unfortunately, there was simply no way he could completely defend it, and the most he could do was weaken it and hope for the best. As for trying to avoid it… the attack was as massive as a star, making evasion impossible unless his Power of the Void reached a certain level that allowed him to travel through the void.

Right before the attack reached him, Yuan activated the Robes of Destruction’s ability.

<Dragon of Destruction’s Ultimate Chaos activated>

When Saaruk’s attack finally struck, the Robes of Destruction absorbed as much of the energy as it could. Afterward, the unthinkable happened.

Despite being said to be indestructible, it shredded into countless pieces and was reduced to nothing.

<Robes of Destruction have been destroyed> Dıscover more novels at novel⟡fire.net

Although the treasure was said to be indestructible by the system, it was a system that did not take into account Eternals, godly beings with unfathomable prowess.

However, even though the Robes of Destruction were destroyed beyond repair, it still managed to do its job, absorbing as much of Saaruk’s attack as it could, even rebounding what it absorbed as it was destroyed.

Because of the Robes of Destruction, Saaruk’s attack was weakened even further.

But even with that, Saaruk’s attack remained immensely powerful and still a threat to Yuan’s life.

Suddenly, Number One Under Heaven trembled, and Zi Xuan’s spirit emerged from it.

Without uttering a word, she swung her arm at Saaruk’s attack and unleashed a powerful aura that was similar to Supreme Sword Aura but was clearly different at the same time.

The sword energy clashed with Saaruk’s attack and managed to endure for a full second before being destroyed.

After unleashing her attack, Zi Xuan quietly returned to Number One Under Heaven and immediately went into a deep slumber after exhausting all of her energy.

Although Zi Xuan couldn’t stop Saaruk’s attack, she managed to weaken it some more.

After Zi Xuan disappeared, Dragon God’s Vengeance appeared of its own will, and Goddess Yeyou emerged from it.

She cast a quick glance at Yuan before turning toward Saaruk’s attack, her mouth opening wide. In the next instant, a radiant beam erupted forth, piercing through Saaruk’s assault like a needle tearing through silk.

Saaruk’s attack dispersed like mist for a fleeting moment before reforming, but its strength had drastically weakened. What once carried the might to annihilate even a Cultivation God with ease was now weak enough for Yuan to defend against it.

“Thank you all,” Yuan said as Dragon Goddess Yeyou disappeared alongside the Dragon God’s Vengeance.

Now that Saaruk’s attack was weak enough, Yuan faced it with what little Eternal Essence he had left before leaving the rest for his physique to deal with.

<Eternal Constitution(Incomplete) activated>

<Eternal Constitution(Incomplete) has refined Saaruk’s Essence>

<Through Saaruk’s Essence refinement, your understanding of Eternal Essence has improved drastically>

In the midst of blocking the attack with his bare body after exhausting his Eternal Essence, Yuan actually started to refine the attack, turning it into his own power.

Since he’d already refined Saaruk’s powers once, he was able to do so again with much more ease.

Some time later, Saaruk’s attack had vanished, and Yuan drifted motionlessly through the void, his body battered and bare. Every inch of him ached, and even the slightest movement felt impossible.

“Young Master! Are you okay?!” Feng Yuxiang emerged from his Dantian and immediately began applying medicine to his body, pouring various expensive elixirs all over his body.

Xiao Hua also appeared and started transferring spiritual energy into his body.

“I’m fine,” he said calmly. “My body isn’t injured, just drained. I pushed myself past my limits and exhausted too much Eternal Essence. It’s not something medicine can heal. Give me some time, and I’ll be able to move again soon.”

“Okay…” Feng Yuxiang nodded with teary eyes.

Yuan fell silent, his gaze drifting into the endless void, eyes distant as if searching for something beyond its darkness.

“I am still too weak,” he suddenly mumbled. “You’re not weak!” Xiao Hua said.

“She’s right, Young Master. There is nobody at your cultivation who could hope to accomplish what you can. You can even defeat God Ascension cultivators despite only being in the True Immortal realm.”

Yuan let out a hollow chuckle. “Cultivation? Such things are meaningless before the Eternals. My true enemies aren’t cultivators. They’re the Eternals themselves. So what does it matter how many cultivators I can defeat if I can’t overcome them?”

“Don’t beat yourself so much, Yuan.” Lan Yingying said. “I know you’ve experienced many reincarnations, but you’re currently still young and have a lot of room to improve.”

Yuan smiled faintly. “I know. I’m just lamenting my own failures. I’ve been trying to take things slowly, even though I know time isn’t on my side. Forget Saaruk—those Eternals I put to sleep before will awaken soon enough and resume their attempt to destroy our world.”

“What about the Absolute Power? Although I don’t know anything about it, doesn’t it protect the Nine Heavens from the Eternals?” Feng Yuxiang asked.

“I don’t know much about the Absolute Power either. However, while it may restrain the Eternals from wielding their power, it cannot suppress their influence. Take the Celestial Emperor, for instance. The second Celestial Emperor once served the Eternals, carrying out their will in their stead.” Yuan sighed.

“Although I was able to stop him before he could do any damage, there are probably many more out there doing their bidding.”

“Then what should we do?” Xiao Hua asked.

“While we can stop them, it’ll only delay the inevitable unless we can take care of the core problem—the Eternals themselves.”

After a moment of silence, Yuan declared, “I am no longer going to take things slowly and will try to regain my strength as fast as possible.”

Meanwhile, somewhere in the Starry Sky, Saaruk sensed that his essence had been absorbed.

“This feeling! That mortal ant is still alive!” His body trembled in anger. He wanted to return to kill Yuan immediately, but with the unknown Eternal already making their way to Yuan, it would be impossible for him to reach Yuan before them.

Moreover, even if he somehow managed to reach Yuan first, he would definitely incur the unknown Eternal’s wrath if he killed Yuan while knowing that they were interested in him.

That said, he had no intention of giving up.

After pondering for a moment, Saaruk remembered something and thought of an idea to deal with Yuan without incurring the unknown Eternal’s wrath.

“That’s right! Those Eternals trying to acquire the Absolute Power are using the mortals to do their bidding! This Mighty Saaruk can do the same and let them slaughter that wretch in his stead!!”

Even though it meant he wouldn’t personally deliver the killing blow, Saaruk no longer cared. All that mattered now was seeing Yuan dead.
